# AdvanceModule candidate council verdicts (2026-07-09)

Claude 5x3 diverse-lens (biased-to-kill) + Codex decorrelated.
Verdicts: C2 3/3 STANDS+Codex REAL | C4 3/3+REAL | C5 3/3+REAL | C3 3/3 | C1 2/3.



---
I have enough to reach a verdict. Let me confirm the two remaining structural questions: whether any sibling write-guard covers the `rawFulfillRandomWords` mid-day else-branch, and whether the window truly has `LR_MID_DAY=1` with `rngRequestTime=0`.

The trace is now fully established from the a79a9809 source. Findings:

**Window exists (LR_MID_DAY=1, rngRequestTime=0, word landed):**
- `requestLootboxRng` (AdvanceModule L1189-1205) sets `LR_MID_DAY=1` (L1194) only when a ticket batch was swapped, then requests VRF and sets `rngRequestTime` (L1205).
- `rawFulfillRandomWords` mid-day else-branch (L1950-1957) writes `lootboxRngWordByIndex[N]=word1`, emits `LootboxRngApplied` (public reveal), and clears `vrfRequestId=0`/`rngRequestTime=0` — but LEAVES `LR_MID_DAY=1`. The latch is only cleared later when a subsequent advance drains the swapped batch (L256 same-day / L354 new-day). So the post-fulfillment / pre-drain window has exactly `LR_MID_DAY=1` ∧ `rngRequestTime=0`, word already committed and emitted.

**The branch uses the wrong in-flight proxy:**
- `updateVrfCoordinatorAndSub` (AdvanceModule L1878) gates the mid-day re-issue solely on `LR_MID_DAY != 0`. Its own comment (L1873-1880) assumes that means "mid-day in flight (word not delivered)." But `LR_MID_DAY` actually means "swapped batch not yet drained." These diverge precisely in this window. In it, L1881 issues a spurious `_requestVrfWord`, sets a new `vrfRequestId`, and L1882 sets `rngRequestTime=now`, with `rngLockedFlag` still false.
- The sibling daily branch (L1883-1891) guards on `rngWordCurrent == 0` before re-issuing. The mid-day branch has NO equivalent `rngRequestTime != 0` freshness guard.

**Overwrite is unguarded:**
- When the fresh word2 fulfills: L1942 passes (`requestId==vrfRequestId`, `rngWordCurrent==0`), `rngLockedFlag==false` → else-branch. `index = LR_INDEX-1 = N` (LR_INDEX untouched by the branch), and L1953 writes `lootboxRngWordByIndex[N]=word2` UNCONDITIONALLY, re-emitting `LootboxRngApplied(N)`.
- This is the ONLY write to `lootboxRngWordByIndex` that lacks an overwrite guard: `_finalizeLootboxRng` guards `!= 0 return` (L1331), the daily preIdx path guards `== 0` (L301), and the public `requestLootboxRng` path is explicitly blocked by `if (LR_MID_DAY != 0) revert MidDayActive()` (L1145) with the comment "prevents entropy reroll by requesting a new VRF word after inspecting the current one." That public-path guard is the designers' own acknowledgement of exactly this attack; the governance path reopens it.

**Structural-protection lens result:** I searched all sibling write-guards (L301, L1331, L1145, L1885) and none covers the `updateVrfCoordinatorAndSub` mid-day branch or the `rawFulfillRandomWords` mid-day else-branch. Nothing fails closed, self-heals, or clamps the overwrite. word1 is publicly emitted at fulfillment, so re-rolling it to word2 (even while the slot is technically unconsumed) violates the VRF freeze/commit invariant (v45): a word touching entropy is re-requested after being observed, changing the un-opened lootbox outcomes at index N. Governance/ADMIN-gated, and it re-rolls non-consenting players' committed lootbox entropy — not an admin self-break.

I could not find a guard that kills this on the structural-protection lens; every analogous path proves the omission is real rather than covered.

VERDICT: STANDS
SEVERITY-IF-STANDS: entropy


---
Verified against `a79a9809` sources (AdvanceModule, MintModule, GameStorage, plus queue-writer call sites in WhaleModule/DecimatorModule/LootboxModule).

## Mechanics check (arithmetic + state transition)

**Advance side (DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol L456–484).** The only "housekeeping already done" marker is `resumingFF = (ticketLevel == ffLevel | TICKET_FAR_FUTURE_BIT)` (L462-463). If false, `_processPhaseTransition(purchaseLevel)` runs (L465) and the marker is set (L467). Then `_processFutureTicketBatch(ffLevel, rngWord)` runs and `if (ffWorked || !ffFinished) break;` (L474) leaves `phaseTransitionActive` true.

**Mint side (DegenerusGameMintModule.sol processFutureTicketBatch).** A call that finishes the FF drain executes the `finished` path L453-468: `delete ticketQueue[rk]`, and since `inFarFuture`, the else-branch L465-467 sets `ticketCursor = 0; ticketLevel = 0`. Critically, a non-empty queue's finishing call **always** has `worked = (used > 0) = true` — even pure skip/cleanup entries charge a budget unit (L374-377), and the only `(false, true)` returns are `total == 0` (L325-328) or a stale cursor `idx >= total` on entry (L337-341), which cannot arise from a prior partial batch (a batch that reaches `idx == total` reports `finished` in that same call; the mid-stop path L469-474 only persists `idx < total`).

So whenever the ffLevel FF queue is non-empty: the finishing call returns `(worked=true, finished=true)` **and zeroes ticketLevel**; Advance L474 breaks with `STAGE_TRANSITION_WORKING`; `phaseTransitionActive` stays true; `dailyIdx` not advanced so `advanceDue()` stays true. Next advance: nothing between rngGate (idempotent, word cached, gapDays=0) and L456 touches `ticketLevel`; `resumingFF` evaluates false (`0 != ffLevel|FF_BIT`) → `_processPhaseTransition` runs a **second** time. `_queueEntries` (GameStorage L655-684) has no idempotence guard — `owed += entries` (L680-682), `rngBypass=true` skips the RngLocked check (L670), and since SDGNRS/VAULT are already in the queue (`packed != 0`) there is no duplicate push, just accumulation to 32 entries each instead of 16 at `purchaseLevel + 99`. The second FF batch call then sees the deleted queue (`total == 0` → `(false, true, 0)`), the `||` at L474 is false, and the transition completes normally. Exactly one extra credit per occurrence; no runaway loop.

## Reachability (concrete sequence)

The precondition is simply "FF bucket at `ffLevel = level+5` non-empty at transition." Player-reachable early and guaranteed later:

1. **Early, single-actor:** any whale pass — `DegenerusGameWhaleModule.sol` L328/336/654/662/1024 and `DegenerusGameDecimatorModule.sol` L660 call `_queueEntryRange(..., 100 levels, ...)`; every level `> level+5` in the range routes to `_tqFarFutureKey` (GameStorage L774-776). One whale-pass purchase at level N makes the level+5 FF bucket non-empty at every transition for ~95 subsequent levels. Also reachable via far-future ticket purchases (MintModule L1151), lootbox FF rolls (LootboxModule L1391), and genesis pass ranges (DegenerusGame.sol L226). Sequence: (a) whale buys pass at level N; (b) level N's purchase phase ends, `phaseTransitionActive` set; (c) advance #1: `_processPhaseTransition` runs, FF drain of level N+6 works+finishes in one or more batches, final batch zeroes `ticketLevel`, break; (d) advance #2 (same day): second `_processPhaseTransition`, double credit.
2. **Self-perpetuating, zero-actor:** the vault perpetual entries themselves target `purchaseLevel+99` = level+100, which is drained at the transition where level becomes (level+100)−5... i.e., every transition from ~level 95 onward finds SDGNRS+VAULT in its ffLevel bucket, so from that point the double-credit fires at **every** level transition unconditionally.

No structural guard kills it: the comment at L466 ("ticketLevel signals we've completed transition housekeeping") describes intent, but MintModule's finish path destroys the signal in the work-and-finish case.

## Impact bound

Per occurrence: SDGNRS and VAULT each receive 32 entries (8 whole tickets) instead of 16 (4) at level+100, plus one redundant `_autoStakeExcessEth` (harmless) and one extra keeper advance tx (paid stage, by-design mult). No player funds move; vault/sDGNRS jackpot weight at far-future levels is silently 2x the calibrated perpetual allocation, diluting player odds at those levels. Not a brick, not a solvency break, not a liveness stall.

VERDICT: STANDS
SEVERITY-IF-STANDS: accounting


---
Verification complete. Findings on the ARITHMETIC/reachability lens:

**Window exists.** `rawFulfillRandomWords` mid-day else-branch (L1949-1957) writes `lootboxRngWordByIndex[index] = word`, sets `vrfRequestId = 0` and `rngRequestTime = 0`, but does NOT clear `LR_MID_DAY`. The latch is cleared only later, in the advance ticket-drain paths (L256 same-day, L354 new-day), when `_runProcessTicketBatch` finishes draining the swapped batch. So between mid-day word delivery and the next advance that fully drains, the state `LR_MID_DAY==1 && rngRequestTime==0 && lootboxRngWordByIndex[N]!=0` is real and reachable.

**Guard asymmetry is real.** In `updateVrfCoordinatorAndSub` (L1878-1882) the mid-day branch fires on `LR_MID_DAY != 0` alone and unconditionally issues `vrfRequestId = _requestVrfWord(VRF_MIDDAY_CONFIRMATIONS)` with `rngRequestTime = now`. The daily branch immediately below (L1885-1891) re-issues only when `rngWordCurrent == 0` (i.e., word not yet delivered). The mid-day branch has no parallel "word already delivered" test (the natural one being `rngRequestTime != 0`, which mid-day fulfillment zeroes).

**Overwrite reaches, unguarded.** When the spurious re-request fulfills, L1942 `if (requestId != vrfRequestId || rngWordCurrent != 0) return;` passes (new id matches; `rngWordCurrent==0` for mid-day), `rngLockedFlag==false` routes to the else-branch, and L1953 overwrites `lootboxRngWordByIndex[N]` with word2 + emits a second `LootboxRngApplied(N)`. This is the ONLY VRF-word write site lacking a write-once guard: `_finalizeLootboxRng` (L1331) has `if (...!=0) return;`, the pre-drain path checks `==0` (L301), and `requestLootboxRng` explicitly blocks re-request while `LR_MID_DAY!=0` (L1145, comment: "prevents entropy reroll by requesting a new VRF word after inspecting the current one"). The mid-day fulfill branch and the `updateVrfCoordinatorAndSub` mid-day branch are the gap through that protection.

**Corruption is genuine, not benign re-roll.** `processTicketBatch` (MintModule L659) reads `entropy = lootboxRngWordByIndex[N]` fresh on every chunk and resumes from persisted `ticketCursor`. For a batch large enough to span multiple advance txs, chunks before the overwrite resolve against word1 and chunks after against word2 — split entropy within one committed, write-once slot. Even in the single-tx case the overwrite lets an inspected, already-committed word be re-rolled before consumption — exactly the manipulation the sibling `requestLootboxRng` guard exists to prevent. Arithmetically the reserved index N is not advanced by this path, so word2 lands in the same live slot the drain consumes; the write-once invariant breaks on a reachable state.

The arithmetic/reachability does not save this: the window is real, the re-request is unconditional on the dual-meaning `LR_MID_DAY` latch, and the fulfill write has no `!=0` guard. Admin/governance-gated and timing-dependent (needs no intervening full drain between re-request and fulfillment), which bounds likelihood but not reachability.

VERDICT: STANDS
SEVERITY-IF-STANDS: entropy

I have verified all three load-bearing facts. Here is my analysis.

## Verification of the three checkpoints

**(i) Does rngGate bump storage `purchaseStartDay` past `dIdx+1`?** YES. `purchaseStartDay` is a persistent storage var. At L1252-1264, when a fresh word arrives after a multi-day stall (`currentWord!=0`, `rngWordByDay[idx+1]==0`, `day>idx+1`), it computes `gapCount = day - idx - 1` and executes `purchaseStartDay += gapCount` (L1263). This write persists across calls. With the trace numbers (level opens day 100, sealed through day 101 so `dailyIdx=101`, word arrives wall-day 110), `gapCount = 110-101-1 = 8`, so `purchaseStartDay` goes 100 → 108. `_backfillGapDays(word,102,110)` (L1255/1978, loop `gapDay<endDay`) fills `rngWordByDay[102..109]`. advance breaks at `STAGE_GAP_BACKFILLED` (L450-452) WITHOUT `_unlockRng`, so `dailyIdx` stays 101 and `rngLockedFlag` stays true.

**(ii) Does the clamp rewind `day` below `psd` while unlocked?** YES. The RNGREUSE clamp (L190-192) cannot distinguish a backfilled gap day from a legitimately-unsealed in-progress day — both have `rngWordByDay[dIdx+1]!=0`. It walks the backfilled days one per advance:
- advance2 (wall-day 110, `locked=true`): clamp → `day=dIdx+1=102`; turbo gate (L202) is skipped because `!locked` is false; the purchase-daily path runs and calls `_unlockRng(102)` (L551) → `dailyIdx=102`, `rngLockedFlag=false`. (`purchaseStartDay` untouched — only L480/L1263 write it — stays 108.)
- advance3 (wall-day 110, `locked=false`): clamp checks `rngWordByDay[103]!=0` (backfilled) → `day=103`; `psd=purchaseStartDay=108`.

`dailyIdx` is frozen at 102 forever because every unlocked advance panics before it can seal day 103, so the clamp always yields 103 < 108. Permanent.

**(iii) Does L203 run before any revert/gameover on advance3?** YES. The turbo gate is at L202-210, strictly ABOVE the game-over gate (L216) and the do-while. On advance3, `!inJackpot(T) && !lastPurchaseDay(T) && !locked(T)` is entered, and L203 `uint32 purchaseDays = day - psd` computes `103 - 108`. Both operands are `uint24` (`day` L176, `psd` L195); the subtraction is evaluated in checked `uint24` (assignment target `uint32` does not promote operands), so it underflows → Panic(0x11) unconditionally, before the `if` predicate and before the L216 game-over/deadman escape. Same-shaped underflow exists at L547 (`day - psd <= 3`) but L203 is the binding one.

The precondition `lastPurchaseDay=false` on advance3 holds whenever the level's prize-pool target was not yet met in advance2 (typical mid-level state) — L543-544 leaves it false. No adversary needed: any purchase-phase VRF stall of sufficient length (gap making `purchaseStartDay` overshoot `dailyIdx+2`) reaches this. The keeper calling `advanceGame` repeatedly on the same wall-day is exactly the intended `STAGE_GAP_BACKFILLED` multi-advance flow (advanceDue stays true).

Reaching sequence (concrete): level opens day100; days100-101 sealed (`dailyIdx=101`, `purchaseStartDay=100`); VRF stalls; word arrives wall-day110. advance1 backfills 102-109, `purchaseStartDay=108`, breaks at STAGE_GAP_BACKFILLED (dailyIdx still 101, locked). advance2 clamps day=102, seals it via `_unlockRng` → dailyIdx=102, unlocked. advance3 clamps day=103, `locked=false`, enters turbo gate L203 → `103-108` underflow → revert. Every later call repeats identically → permanent brick.

VERDICT: STANDS
SEVERITY-IF-STANDS: brick


---
All three premises confirmed. Final verification of the reaching sequence complete.

## Verification of CANDIDATE C2

**(i) Does rngGate bump purchaseStartDay past dIdx+1 on this path?** YES.
On advance1 (wall-day 110, `dailyIdx=101`), line 190 does NOT clamp because `rngWordByDay[102]==0` at entry (backfill hasn't run yet), so `day=110` reaches `rngGate`. Inside rngGate, `currentWord!=0 && rngRequestTime!=0` (line 1244), `idx=101`, `day(110) > 102 && rngWordByDay[102]==0` (line 1253) → `gapCount = 110-101-1 = 8`, `_backfillGapDays(currentWord,102,110)` fills `rngWordByDay[102..109]` (line 1983), `_applyDailyRng(110,...)` fills `rngWordByDay[110]` (line 2033), and **line 1263 `purchaseStartDay += 8` → storage psd = 100→108**. Returns `gapDays=8`. Back in advanceGame, line 435 mirrors it into the local (`psd=108`), line 450 `gapDays!=0` → `STAGE_GAP_BACKFILLED` break (line 451) **without reaching `_unlockRng`**, so `dailyIdx` stays 101 and `rngLockedFlag` stays true.

**(ii) Does the clamp rewind day below psd while unlocked?** YES.
- advance2: `dIdx=101`, line 190 `day(110)>102 && rngWordByDay[102]!=0` → `day=102`. `locked=rngLockedFlag=true` (never unlocked in advance1; `rawFulfillRandomWords` line 1947-49 keeps the daily lock set). Line 202 block is guarded by `!locked` → **skipped, no underflow**. rngGate(102) returns the backfilled word with `gapDays=0`; purchase-phase daily reaches line 551 `_unlockRng(102)` → `dailyIdx=102`, `rngLockedFlag=false`, `rngWordCurrent=0`.
- advance3: `dIdx=102`, line 190 `day(110)>103 && rngWordByDay[103]!=0` (still backfilled) → `day=103`. Now `locked=false`. `psd=purchaseStartDay=108`. **103 < 108.**

**(iii) Does line 203 run before any revert/gameover on advance3?** YES.
`!inJackpot && !lastPurchaseDay && !locked` all hold (pre-target purchase phase, unlocked) → block entered. Line 203 `uint32 purchaseDays = day - psd` computes `103 - 108` in **uint24** (both operands uint24; widening to uint32 applies only after the subtraction), not in an `unchecked` block → **Panic(0x11)**. This precedes the gameover gate (line 216) and `_vrfDeadmanFired`, so there is no liveness escape. The panic aborts before `_unlockRng`, so `dailyIdx` is frozen at 102; every subsequent call re-clamps to 103 and re-panics identically = permanent brick. The design comment at line 189 ("Distinct from a VRF gap stall (rngWordByDay[dailyIdx+1]==0 → backfill path)") is only true *before* the backfill writes those slots; *after* backfill the clamp fires on the very days the deferral created, and the one-shot `psd += gapCount` bump outruns the one-day-per-advance clamp walk. The twin site at line 547 (`day - psd <= 3`) shares the shape but is downstream of the line-203 brick.

Reachability of the seed state: an 8-day VRF outage is a genuine reachable condition — during the stall advanceGame reverts `RngNotReady` (line 1321) / re-fires via the 12h retry (line 1317), `dailyIdx` stays 101, and the gap `102..109` materializes when the word finally lands on wall-day 110.

Authoritative source: `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol` @ a79a9809, lines 190-192, 202-210, 428-453, 1244-1265, 1906-1928, 1970-1992.

VERDICT: STANDS
SEVERITY-IF-STANDS: brick


---
Analysis complete. Both L203 and L547 are plain checked `uint24 - uint24` subtractions (no `unchecked` wrapper).

## Verification of Candidate C2

I traced the exact reaching sequence against the a79a9809 source. All three verification asks resolve in favor of the finding, and I could not find a structural protection that kills it.

**(i) rngGate bumps purchaseStartDay past dailyIdx+1** — CONFIRMED. `rngGate` L1253-1264: on the fresh-word backfill path (`currentWord != 0 && rngRequestTime != 0`, `day > idx+1`, `rngWordByDay[idx+1] == 0`), it computes `gapCount = day - idx - 1` and does storage write `purchaseStartDay += gapCount` (L1263). For the scenario (level opened day100 with psd=100, days100-101 sealed so dailyIdx=101, word arrives wall-day110): `gapCount = 110-101-1 = 8`, so `purchaseStartDay: 100 → 108`, while `dailyIdx+1 = 102`. `_backfillGapDays(currentWord, 102, 110)` fills `rngWordByDay[102..109]` (endDay exclusive). rngRequestTime is NOT cleared by `rawFulfillRandomWords` on the daily path (L1947-1949), so the backfill gate is satisfied. advance1 then breaks at STAGE_GAP_BACKFILLED (L450-452) **before any `_unlockRng`**, so dailyIdx stays 101.

**(ii) the RNGREUSE clamp rewinds day below psd while unlocked** — CONFIRMED. `_unlockRng` (L1906-1911) sets `dailyIdx = day` (direct assignment, not +1) and clears `rngLockedFlag`. advance2 (dIdx=101): clamp L190 (`day=110 > 102 && rngWordByDay[102] != 0`) sets `day=102`; L202 is skipped because `locked` is still true; it resolves day102 normally and calls `_unlockRng(102)` at L551 → `dailyIdx=102`, `rngLockedFlag=false`. advance3 (dIdx=102): clamp L190 (`110 > 103 && rngWordByDay[103] != 0`) sets `day=103`; `psd = purchaseStartDay = 108`; `locked=false`. So `day(103) < psd(108)` with the RNG unlocked.

**(iii) L203 runs before any revert/gameover on advance3** — CONFIRMED. L202 guard `!inJackpot(T) && !lastPurchaseDay(T, pre-target) && !locked(T)` all pass → L203 `uint32 purchaseDays = day - psd` executes as a **checked uint24 subtraction** `103 - 108` → Panic(0x11). The game-over gate is at L216, strictly after L203, so there is no liveness escape and the deadman path (L216) is unreachable.

**Permanence** — advance3 reverts before `_unlockRng`, so `dailyIdx` stays 102 and `purchaseStartDay` stays 108 forever. Every subsequent call re-derives `day=103` (clamp), `locked=false`, `lastPurchaseDay=false` → identical L203 panic. No external setter resets these. Permanent brick.

**Structural-protection lens (bias to refute) — nothing neutralizes it:**
- The clamp does not fire "harmlessly": `rngWordByDay` is never cleared, so the backfilled days keep the clamp alive across all future calls.
- `locked` is genuinely false at advance3 (cleared by advance2's `_unlockRng`), so the L202 skip that protects advance2 does not protect advance3.
- The revert does NOT fail-closed to a recoverable state — advance1/advance2 already committed the poison state (bumped psd + sealed day102) before advance3 reverts.
- L547 is a second, same-shaped underflow (`day - psd <= 3`) that fires on advance2 itself if `targetMet` becomes true at day102 (`102 - 108`), so even the `lastPurchaseDay=true` branch is not a safe exit — it panics too.
- Root cause is real and unguarded: `purchaseStartDay` is bumped by the FULL gap for death-clock purposes (L1261-1263 comment), but the same variable is reused as the purchase-phase-day counter in `day - psd`, while the clamp forces `day` to re-walk the gap days one-per-advance strictly below the bumped psd.

Reachable on an ordinary multi-day VRF stall during a pre-target purchase phase (the common building-pool state). The finding is not killed on this lens.

VERDICT: STANDS
SEVERITY-IF-STANDS: brick


---
I've traced the full mechanism against the a79a9809 source. Let me confirm the one remaining structural question: whether anything between the two advances re-establishes the marker or blocks the re-run.

The chain is fully confirmed. Here is my analysis.

**Reaching sequence (all at a79a9809):**

1. **Advance N — first transition advance.** `phaseTransitionActive` true (set at `_endPhase`, L769). At entry `ticketLevel != (ffLevel|BIT)`, so `resumingFF=false` (AdvanceModule L462-464) → `_processPhaseTransition(purchaseLevel)` runs (L465): `_queueEntries(SDGNRS, P+99, 16, true)` and `_queueEntries(VAULT, P+99, 16, true)` (L1621-1633). Then `ticketLevel = ffLevel|TICKET_FAR_FUTURE_BIT`, `ticketCursor=0` (L467-468).
2. `_processFutureTicketBatch(ffLevel, rngWord)` (L470) delegatecalls MintModule.processFutureTicketBatch. With a **non-empty** FF queue at ffLevel that fully drains in one write budget (`WRITES_BUDGET_SAFE=550`, ~357 after cold scaling; the 16+16 vault perpetual entries across 2 queue slots cost ~70 writes), the loop exits with `idx>=total` → `worked=(used>0)=true`, `finished=true`. The `finished` block (MintModule L455-467) runs the `inFarFuture` else branch: `delete ticketQueue[rk]; ticketCursor=0; ticketLevel=0` (L464-466).
3. Back in advance: `if (ffWorked || !ffFinished)` = `true || false` = **true** → `break` (L474-476). Transition NOT completed (`phaseTransitionActive` stays true), and `ticketLevel` is now **0**.
4. **Advance N+1.** `level` did not advance (completion at L478-483 never reached), so `purchaseLevel` unchanged. `resumingFF = (ticketLevel == ffLevel|BIT) = (0 == marker) = false` → `_processPhaseTransition(purchaseLevel)` **runs a SECOND time** → `_queueEntries` accumulates (`owed += entries`, GameStorage L667/681) → SDGNRS and VAULT each now hold **32** entries at the same `targetLevel = P+99` instead of 16. Then it sets the marker, drains the now-deleted (empty) FF queue → `total==0` → `(false,true,0)` → `false||false`=false → falls through to completion (L478-483).

**Structural-protection lens — no guard kills it:**
- The completion path (`!ffWorked && ffFinished`, L474 inverted) is only reachable when the FF queue is *empty* at call start (`total==0` early return is the sole `worked=false,finished=true` producer; even pure skips set `used>0`). So the transition can only complete on an advance where the queue is empty — but the advance that *empties* it (work+finish) always clears `ticketLevel=0`, and nothing between advances re-writes `ticketLevel` (only writers: AdvanceModule L467 and MintModule's own paths). `ffLevel|BIT ≥ 5|BIT ≠ 0`, so `resumingFF` is unconditionally false on the following advance.
- `_queueEntries` has no idempotence/already-consumed guard; it accumulates unconditionally.
- The only no-double case is an empty FF queue at advance N (single-advance completion, correct). But vault perpetual entries self-perpetuate (queued at `P+99`, landing exactly at the `(P+95)+4` boundary 95 levels later), so from ~level 96 the FF queue at `level+5` is generically non-empty every transition; any player far-future ticket at `level+5` reaches it earlier at any level.

The root defect: `ticketLevel` is overloaded as both the FF-drain resume cursor (MintModule zeroes it on finish) and the "housekeeping-done" marker (advance's `resumingFF` gate), and a batch that both works and finishes destroys the marker while the `ffWorked` break defers completion.

`VERDICT: STANDS`
`SEVERITY-IF-STANDS: accounting`

I have verified the full reaching path. Let me confirm the exact double-run mechanism holds.

Analysis of the a79a9809 source:

**The guard and how it fails:**
- Advance L462-463: `resumingFF = (ticketLevel == (ffLevel | TICKET_FAR_FUTURE_BIT))`. This is the ONLY guard preventing `_processPhaseTransition` (L465) re-running. L466 comment confirms intent: "ticketLevel signals we've completed transition housekeeping."
- `_processPhaseTransition` (L1618-1637) calls `_queueEntries` which **accumulates** (`owed += entries`, GameStorage L680-681) — not idempotent.

**The break-condition / marker-wipe collision:**
- Advance L470-473 calls `_processFutureTicketBatch(ffLevel)` (delegates to MintModule.processFutureTicketBatch). Since Advance set `ticketLevel = ffLevel|FF_BIT` at L467, `inFarFuture = true` inside MintModule (L320).
- When that drain **finishes** (`idx >= total`), the inFarFuture finished branch (Mint L465-467) sets `ticketCursor=0; ticketLevel=0`. It returns `worked=true` (Mint L450, `used>0` since it actually minted entries) and `finished=true`.
- Back at Advance L474: `if (ffWorked || !ffFinished)` = `(true || false)` = **true → break STAGE_TRANSITION_WORKING**, leaving `phaseTransitionActive = true` and `ticketLevel = 0`.

**Second advance (same day; dailyIdx unadvanced, advanceDue still true; subs skipped since `_afkingResetDay==day`; rngGate idempotent gapDays=0 → reaches L456 again):**
- `resumingFF = (0 == ffLevel|FF_BIT)` = **false** → `_processPhaseTransition` runs a **second time** → +16 entries each to SDGNRS and VAULT at `targetLevel = purchaseLevel+99` (a bucket distinct from the just-drained ffLevel bucket, so no self-cancel).
- Drain now hits empty queue (Mint L325, `total==0`) → `worked=false, finished=true` → `(false||false)` false → transition completes cleanly.

**Unavoidability for any non-empty ffLevel bucket:** the final draining batch always reports `worked=true`+`finished=true` (the not-finished path L469-473 persists `ticketCursor=idx<total` and never zeroes `ticketLevel`, so mid-queue resumes keep `resumingFF=true` and skip PPT; but the terminal batch zeroes `ticketLevel`). A finishing batch with `worked=false` only occurs on an already-empty queue (L325/L337), which is the clean single-advance path with no double-run. So exactly one extra `_processPhaseTransition` fires per transition whenever the ffLevel FF bucket is non-empty.

**Reachability:** ffLevel = purchaseLevel+4 = level+5. Vault perpetual entries queued at `purchaseLevel+99` (level+100) mature exactly when `ffLevel = level+5 = that target`, i.e. 95 levels later — so from ~level 95 onward **every** transition has a non-empty ffLevel bucket and double-credits. Additionally, any player far-future ticket targeting level+6 makes the bucket non-empty at the next transition (any level). No idempotence guard or already-consumed marker kills it; the sole guard (`resumingFF` via `ticketLevel`) is wiped by the finished path before the re-entry reads it.

Arithmetic: `owed += 16` twice → SDGNRS and VAULT each receive 32 instead of 16 VAULT_PERPETUAL_ENTRIES at `purchaseLevel+99`; well within uint32, so no overflow saves it — the extra credit is real, not a phantom value.

SEVERITY-IF-STANDS: accounting

## Verification result — CANDIDATE C1

**The code asymmetry is real.** AdvanceModule `updateVrfCoordinatorAndSub` L1878 (`_lrRead(LR_MID_DAY) != 0`) gates the mid-day re-issue on the LR_MID_DAY latch, with no delivery-state check, whereas the daily branch L1885 guards on `rngWordCurrent == 0`. And LR_MID_DAY is genuinely NOT an in-flight marker: the mid-day fulfillment else-branch (L1950-1957) clears `vrfRequestId`/`rngRequestTime` but leaves LR_MID_DAY=1; it is cleared only when the swapped ticket batch finishes draining (L256 same-day / L354 cross-day). So the window **`LR_MID_DAY=1 ∧ rngRequestTime=0 ∧ slot N filled`** is reachable, and a rotation fired in it does issue a spurious VRF request (L1881) whose fulfillment re-writes `lootboxRngWordByIndex[N]` (L1953) and double-emits `LootboxRngApplied`.

**But the overwrite is the benign variant the candidate asked me to rule out, not corruption of a consumed word:**

1. **The stored word is a forward-only, lazily-consumed input.** The ticket-batch drain does NOT consume the lootbox word for outcomes — it only queues boxes. The word is read at box-OPEN time: `_openLootBoxLeg`→`lootboxRngWordByIndex[index]` (LootboxModule L532-537, L643, L694). An opened box is zeroed/dequeued (`packed & LB_AMOUNT_MASK == 0 → continue`, L712). So overwriting slot N can never mutate a completed open — it only changes the base entropy for boxes still unopened at that index.

2. **The harmful "consumed word" state is structurally excluded.** L1878 can fire only while LR_MID_DAY=1, i.e., while the batch that populates index N is still draining — the box population at N is still growing and not final. There is no state where slot N is fully settled AND LR_MID_DAY=1 AND a re-read reverses an outcome; already-opened boxes are dequeued and never re-read.

3. **No predictability edge for any actor.** word_1 and word_2 are both fresh VRF output. No player can trigger the rotation (ADMIN-only, L1868), and the admin cannot predict word_2, so an admin/player collusion re-roll is 50/50, not value-extracting. Lootbox EV is frozen at deposit and payout is bounded by the committed pending budget → no solvency/accounting drift.

4. **No new liveness gap.** A dangling spurious request just re-arms the designed `MIDDAY_RNG_STALL_TIMEOUT` (4h) promotion path (rngGate L1314-1320); the batch keeps draining against word_1 (slot non-zero, L233-238 passes) meanwhile.

5. **Trigger is emergency ADMIN/sDGNRS governance.** Rotating a coordinator that just successfully delivered is irrational; the residual is a cosmetic double `LootboxRngApplied` emit (indexer-only) plus a fresh-entropy re-roll of not-yet-opened boxes.

Residual real effect: one spurious `LootboxRngApplied` event and a fresh-VRF re-roll of unopened boxes at index N. No settled outcome is reversed, no actor gains an edge, no value moves.

VERDICT: REFUTED
SEVERITY-IF-STANDS: entropy

## C5 verification result

**Mechanism confirmed.** `ticketsFullyProcessed` is cleared (`=false`) at exactly one site — `_swapTicketSlot` (Advance.sol L1740). The only swap sites are L745 (game-over drain block, itself gated behind `!ticketsFullyProcessed`), L1193 (mid-day *lootbox* request path), and L1764 (`_swapAndFreeze`, the normal daily-request path). `processTicketBatch` (Mint.sol L621-625) materializes only the **read** slot (`_tqReadKey`) into trait buckets; `runTerminalJackpot` (Jackpot.sol L238-274 → `_processDailyEth`) pays only materialized trait-bucket holders. Write-slot entries are therefore invisible to the terminal jackpot unless swapped into read first.

**Resting-state precondition is the machine's normal idle state.** After any daily seal completes, L510 sets `ticketsFullyProcessed=true`, the read slot is drained/empty, and post-request buys accumulate in the write slot (`_queueEntries` L671-673 routes to `_tqWriteKey(level+1)`, no flag touch, no swap). The next day's advance would swap+drain them (`_swapAndFreeze` L437), but if the game dies in this window they sit un-materialized with the flag stale-true.

**Reaching sequence (scenario i, full abandonment, purchase phase, lvl≠0):**
1. Day D advance seals: L503-510 drain read slot, `ticketsFullyProcessed=true`, `_unlockRng`.
2. Players buy regular tickets after the seal → `_tqWriteKey(lvl+1)` populated; flag stays true (no swap; regular buys don't hit the L1189-1195 mid-day lootbox swap).
3. No further advance for 120 days → `_livenessTriggered()` true (Storage L1523, `currentDay-psd>120`; deadman L1535 also latches and freezes since `dailyIdx` won't advance).
4. Advance call A: L216 enters `_handleGameOverPath`; L700 `rngWordByDay[day]==0` → `_gameOverEntropy` L1424 `_tryRequestRng` (no swap, no flag write) → returns 1 → L707 exit STAGE_GAMEOVER.
5. Word fulfills. Advance call B: `_gameOverEntropy` L1350-1373 applies word via `_applyDailyRng`, returns real word (not 1/0). Back at L725: **`ticketsFullyProcessed==true` → entire drain+swap block (L725-752, including the L745 `_swapTicketSlot`) is skipped** → `handleGameOverDrain` (L754) → `runTerminalJackpot(remaining, lvl+1, rngWord)` distributes to materialized level+1 holders only.

**Scenario (ii)** is identical past step 4: `_tryRequestRng` reverts → L1428-1430 sets `rngRequestTime=ts`, returns 0; after 14-day `GAMEOVER_RNG_FALLBACK_DELAY` the L1385-1421 fallback commits a real word; call B hits L725 with flag still stale-true. Neither entropy path ever calls `_swapTicketSlot`, and once liveness/deadman latches, `_handleGameOverPath` never returns `(false,0)` (so the normal swapping advance path at L437 is never reached to consume the word).

The write-slot buyers' ETH is already folded into the prize pools (zeroed at L147-149 and paid out), so their funds are redistributed to the materialized set and the sinks while they receive nothing — contradicting the block's own stated intent at L710-724 ("every purchased ticket is eligible for the terminal jackpot trait-bucket share"). The code comment at L718-719 assumes the flag is true on `handleGameOverDrain` entry *only because the L725 block completed it* — but it is equally true from a prior daily seal, which is the defect.

No structural guard kills it: the flag is never reset at game-over onset, and regular ticket buys never trigger a swap.

VERDICT: STANDS
SEVERITY-IF-STANDS: accounting

I have verified the full path. Let me confirm the two key facts underpinning the exclusion arithmetic.

The mechanism is confirmed. Summary of verification against `a79a9809`:

**Where traits/eligibility are created.** `runTerminalJackpot` (JackpotModule L238) draws winners only from `lvlTraitEntry[targetLvl]` via `_randTraitTicket` (L1129-1135, L1479). `lvlTraitEntry` is populated exclusively by `processTicketBatch` (MintModule L621), which reads `ticketQueue[_tqReadKey(lvl)]` (L625). Tickets sitting in the WRITE slot have no `lvlTraitEntry` entry and are invisible to the terminal draw.

**Steady-state before game-over.** A normal daily seal requests VRF via `rngGate`→`_swapAndFreeze` (Advance L437), flipping the write slot to read and setting `ticketsFullyProcessed=false`; the fulfill tx drains that read slot and sets `ticketsFullyProcessed=true` (L348/L510). Post-seal invariant: `ticketsFullyProcessed==true`, read slot empty, and every buy since the last RNG request accumulates in the WRITE slot `_tqWriteKey(level+1)`.

**Game-over entry does not clear the flag.** `_handleGameOverPath` runs at L217 before the daily do-loop, so the only slot swap reachable during game-over is inside the L725 block. `_gameOverEntropy` (L1342) and `_finalizeRngRequest` (L1779) perform NO `_swapTicketSlot` and never touch `ticketsFullyProcessed`. So the flag stays stale-true across both the request tx and the fulfill tx.

**The skip.** On the fulfill/fallback tx, `_gameOverEntropy` returns a non-zero word (L1351-1373 or fallback L1385-1421), execution reaches L725 `if(!ticketsFullyProcessed)` → FALSE → the entire drain+`_swapTicketSlot` block (L725-752) is skipped → straight to `handleGameOverDrain` (L754). The write slot is never swapped to read, `processTicketBatch` never runs on it, those buyers never enter `lvlTraitEntry[level+1]`, and `runTerminalJackpot(remaining, lvl+1, …)` (GameOver L198) distributes the 90% pool only among earlier processed holders. `handleFinalSweep` folds leftovers to vault/sDGNRS/GNRUS, never to the stranded buyers.

**Reachability of both sequences.**
- (i) Purchase-phase abandonment, level L>0: after day-D seal (`ticketsFullyProcessed=true`, target not met so L691-697 does not short-circuit), buys land in write slot on D+1…; 120-day inactivity makes `_livenessTriggered` true (Storage L523); first advance issues VRF via `_tryRequestRng` (L1424, returns 1, L707 return); word fulfills; next advance hits L725 false-branch. Confirmed.
- (ii) `_tryRequestRng` catch → returns 0 → L1428 sets `rngRequestTime=ts`; after `GAMEOVER_RNG_FALLBACK_DELAY` (14d, L124) the fallback word commits (L1386-1421) with no swap. Same L725 skip. Confirmed.

**Contrast confirming design gap.** The mid-day request path explicitly guards this exact case: `requestLootboxRng` L1192 `if (ticketQueue[wk].length > 0 && ticketsFullyProcessed) { _swapTicketSlot(); }`. The game-over drain block has no equivalent write-slot-non-empty check; its gate is only `!ticketsFullyProcessed`. The L719 comment ("entered with ticketsFullyProcessed already set") encodes the faulty assumption that the flag being set means all tickets are drained, when post-seal it only means the read slot is drained while the write slot still holds the last window's buys.

The arithmetic exclusion is real for a realistic, reachable value (any non-empty write slot at game-over): those buyers' ETH is redistributed to other holders/decimator/sweep sinks while they receive nothing from the terminal jackpot. This is the mainline abandonment path, not the L721 catastrophic-revert fallback the comment carves out.

VERDICT: STANDS
SEVERITY-IF-STANDS: accounting

## C5 Verification — stale `ticketsFullyProcessed=true` skips the game-over write-slot drain

All citations are to `git show a79a9809:` sources: `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol` (ADV), `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameMintModule.sol` (MINT), `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameGameOverModule.sol` (GO), `contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol` (ST).

**Flag writer set (tree-wide grep, all 24 contract files):** `ticketsFullyProcessed = false` is written ONLY inside `_swapTicketSlot` (ADV L1738-1741). Its only callers: ADV L323 + L437 (`_swapAndFreeze` on the normal daily rngGate path), L1193 (`requestLootboxRng` mid-day path), L745 (inside the very block being skipped). Confirmed: `_gameOverEntropy` (ADV L1342-1432) contains no swap on any of its three branches — cached-word commit (L1351-1373), deadman/fallback commit (L1385-1421), fresh `_tryRequestRng` (L1424); `_tryRequestRng`/`_finalizeRngRequest` (L1667-1855) never touch the slot either.

**Sequence (i) reaches L725 with flag=true and a populated write slot — verified step by step:**
1. Last completed day D: drain finishes → flag=true (ADV L255/L348/L510); `_unlockRng` clears `rngWordCurrent`, `rngRequestTime`, `rngLockedFlag` (ADV L1906-1911). Buys route to `_tqWriteKey(lvl+1)` (ST L673/L717) and stay open until liveness fires at `day - purchaseStartDay > 120` (ST L1523; purchase gates at MINT L942/L1103/L1362/L1829). Up to ~120 days of purchases accumulate in the write slot.
2. First advance after liveness/deadman: `_handleGameOverPath` (ADV L216→L659). `gameOver=false`, liveness true, pool-met bypass fails (or deadman overrides, L691-697). `rngWordByDay[day]==0` → `_gameOverEntropy` → `_tryRequestRng` succeeds → returns 1 → exit (L707). **No swap.**
3. VRF fulfills (`rngWordCurrent=word`, ADV L1949). Next advance: `_gameOverEntropy` L1351 branch commits via `_applyDailyRng`, returns word → falls through to L725. `ticketsFullyProcessed==true` → entire drain block L725-752 skipped → `handleGameOverDrain(day)` runs (L754).

**Sequence (ii)** (coordinator revert → L1429-1431 sets fallback timer → after `GAMEOVER_RNG_FALLBACK_DELAY` the L1385 branch commits the historical fallback and returns it same-tx) reaches L725 identically. **No swap.** In fact the flag=true entry is the *generic* game-over entry state — even a keeper-alive 120-day target-miss game-over enters with flag=true and the write slot holding all buys since the previous day's RNG request; the L725 block only ever helps when game-over interrupts a mid-drain day.

**Genuinely never drained / no self-heal:** `handleGameOverDrain` latches `GO_JACKPOT_PAID` + `gameOver` (GO L76, L137, L146) and never touches `ticketQueue`; after latching, `_handleGameOverPath` short-circuits at L673 to `handleFinalSweep` only — L725 is unreachable forever. `runTerminalJackpot` (Jackpot L238-274 → `_processDailyEth`) samples only materialized trait entries; `processTicketBatch` reads only `_tqReadKey` (MINT L625), so write-slot entries never materialize. No refund surface: purchase/redeem/sell paths all revert on `_livenessTriggered()`/`gameOver`; `entriesOwedPacked[writeKey]` is read only by views (Game L2109/L2556). The excluded buyers' ETH sits in `available` and is distributed to *other* ticketholders/decimator, remainder swept to vault/sDGNRS/GNRUS at final sweep.

**Structural protections examined and eliminated:** mid-day swap at ADV L1190-1194 is blocked during a stall (`requestLootboxRng` reverts at L1152 once `rngWordByDay[currentDay]==0`) — incidental luck at best; the FUND-RELEASE-FALLBACK forfeiture comment (L720-724) covers only the catastrophic-revert case; the stated design intent at L710-713 is explicit — "Best-effort drain … so every purchased ticket is eligible … **Both ticket slots** drain ONE batch per tx" — and the implementation reaches the write slot only when entered with flag=false. This is the exact asymmetry the mid-day path guards against (write-queue-nonempty && flag-true → swap) and the game-over path lacks.

Impact bound: mis-distribution of the terminal jackpot (up to ~120 days of ticket purchases excluded from the 90% terminal share in the abandonment case; the final partial day's buyers in every purchase-phase game-over). No fund lock, no over-payment, no brick: game-over completes and total funds are conserved — they go to the wrong eligibility set.

VERDICT: STANDS
SEVERITY-IF-STANDS: accounting

Verification complete. Full trace below, all citations from commit `a79a9809`.

## Reaching sequence (Scenario A — locked last-purchase day, VRF dies)

1. **Setup.** Purchase phase at level L; buys queue at write key `L+1` (MintModule L1967: `targetLevel = cachedJpFlag ? cachedLevel : cachedLevel + 1`). On the last purchase day, advance hits `rngGate` → `_requestRng` → `_finalizeRngRequest` (AdvanceModule L1831-1835): fresh daily request with `isTicketJackpotDay=true` sets **`level = lvl` (pre-increment to L+1)** and `rngLockedFlag=true`; advance then executes `_swapAndFreeze()` (L436-437) → `_swapTicketSlot()` toggles the buffer, so the final-day cohort now sits in the **read slot at key `L+1`** with `ticketsFullyProcessed=false`.
2. **Stall.** VRF never fulfills. Every subsequent advance takes the L289 gate: `purchaseLevel = (lastPurchase && locked) ? lvl : lvl+1` = **`lvl`** (L213, correct key), finds the queue non-empty, `lootboxRngWordByIndex[preIdx]==0`, `rngWordCurrent==0`, and the mid-day promotion requires `!rngLockedFlag` (L317) → `revert RngNotReady()` (L330). Nothing can drain the cohort without a word. `lastPurchaseDay`/`level` stay frozen (transition code at L571-596 never runs).
3. **Deadman.** After `_VRF_DEADMAN_DAYS`, `_vrfDeadmanFired()` (Storage L1534-1536, phase-independent day math) flips the L216 gate → `_handleGameOverPath(day, lvl=L+1)`. `_livenessTriggered` (Storage L1518) returns true via the deadman; the pool-met early-return (L691-697) is deadman-bypassed; `_gameOverEntropy` (L1384-1421) commits the historical fallback word same-tx (`rngWordCurrent==0` skips the first branch, `deadman=true` skips the grace wait).
4. **The mismatched drain.** L725-749 calls `processTicketBatch(lvl + 1 = L+2)` — **unconditional `lvl+1`**, unlike the normal path's conditional key. `processTicketBatch` (MintModule L621-657) reads only `_tqReadKey(L+2)`: empty → `finished=true` without touching the `L+1` cohort. Then `_swapTicketSlot()` (L745) toggles parity and L746 re-checks **key `L+2` only**. Parity walk with read-bit b: cohort at `(L+1)|b`; pass 1 drains `(L+2)|b` (empty), swap; pass 2 drains `(L+2)|!b` (any stall-window buys), swap back; check `(L+2)|b` empty → `ticketsFullyProcessed=true`. **No leg of the game-over drain ever queries key `L+1`.** The cohort is latched out while both slots still hold its entries — exactly the "impossible non-empty" case `_swapTicketSlot`'s comment (L1732-1737) assumes unreachable.
5. **No later pickup.** `handleGameOverDrain` (GameOverModule L75-200) sets `gameOver=true` and calls `runTerminalJackpot(remaining, lvl+1 = L+2, rngWord)` (L198). `runTerminalJackpot` → `_processDailyEth` → `_processBucket` samples winners exclusively from **`lvlTraitEntry[targetLvl]`** (JackpotModule L1130) — i.e., *materialized* tickets at L+2. Queued-but-unprocessed entries are invisible to it, and even the already-materialized `L+1` holders (all prior purchase days of the completed level) are at the wrong index. Unpaid remainder sits until `handleFinalSweep` forfeits it 1/3-each to vault/sDGNRS/GNRUS (L239-250). Post-gameover, purchases are blocked and no redemption path reads `entriesOwedPacked` — exclusion is terminal.

**Scenario B (jackpot-phase deadman)** is the same shape: jackpot-phase buys queue at `cachedLevel = lvl` (MintModule L1967), the last daily request's `_swapAndFreeze` leaves them in the read slot at key `lvl`, and the game-over drain again queries only `lvl+1` keys — the cohort is never materialized.

## Refutation attempts that failed

- "Normal path drains it first": no — L330 `RngNotReady` blocks every pre-deadman advance (step 2).
- "Empty cohort": the swapped-in read slot holds the full final purchase day's buys (each daily request swaps a fresh ~24h write slot); non-empty in any live game.
- "Terminal jackpot at `lvl+1` picks them up": no — it samples `lvlTraitEntry`, not the queue, and at L+2 not L+1.
- "By-design": contradicts the in-code guarantee at L711-714 ("every purchased ticket is eligible for the terminal jackpot trait-bucket share") and `_swapTicketSlot`'s stated non-empty-read-slot invariant. Distinct from the refuted post-gameover ticket-*insertion* ruling (this is pre-gameover *exclusion*) and from the by-design prevrandao-fallback ruling (entropy source, not distribution keying).
- Return-shape kill: delegatecall decode needs ≥64 bytes — `(bool, bool)` returns exactly 64 ✓; `finished=true` on the empty queue is real (L652-654).

The asymmetry is precise: game-over code assumes the purchase-phase invariant (level not yet incremented, tickets at `lvl+1`), which is false on both deadman entries (jackpot phase / locked last-purchase), where the queue key is `lvl`. Funds are not bricked — the game-over heartbeat completes — but the largest cohort's purchases are silently voided and their share is misdirected to the L+2 stall-window buyers (if any) and ultimately the sweep sinks.

Key locations: `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol` L213, L216, L725-749 (esp. L731, L746), L1831-1835; `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameGameOverModule.sol` L75-78, L198; `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameMintModule.sol` L621-657, L1967; `contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol` L848-855, L1513-1536.

All verification legs are complete. Summary of the trace, worked entirely from `git show a79a9809:` sources:

**1. Drain key is unconditionally `lvl+1` — CONFIRMED.**
`AdvanceModule._handleGameOverPath` (L725-752): `processTicketBatch.selector, lvl + 1` (L731) and `ticketQueue[_tqReadKey(lvl + 1)].length == 0` (L746). `MintModule.processTicketBatch(lvl)` reads only `ticketQueue[_tqReadKey(lvl)]` (Mint L625-627). Queue keys encode the level itself (`Storage._tqReadKey/_tqWriteKey` L848-855: `lvl | TICKET_SLOT_BIT` or raw `lvl`), so a key-`lvl` cohort is invisible to a `lvl+1` drain — it is not a parity/buffer question.

**2. On the deadman entry the pending cohort sits at key `lvl` — CONFIRMED, both deadman sub-cases.**
- *Locked last-purchase day:* buys during the final purchase day target `cachedLevel+1 = N` (Mint L1967); at the day-end request `_finalizeRngRequest` pre-increments `level = N` (Advance L1831-1835) and `_swapAndFreeze` (L437) rotates that cohort into the read slot at `_tqReadKey(N)` with `ticketsFullyProcessed=false`. VRF then dies; every subsequent advance reverts `RngNotReady` at the daily drain gate (L301-330, mid-day promote blocked by `rngLockedFlag`), so the cohort provably sits undrained until the deadman.
- *Jackpot phase:* buys target `cachedLevel = N` (Mint L1967, `cachedJpFlag` branch), drained normally at `inJackpot ? lvl` (Advance L504); after the stalled day's request they sit at `_tqReadKey(N)` identically.
Deadman entry is live: `_livenessTriggered` returns `_vrfDeadmanFired()` in these phases (Storage L1513-1518, L1534-1535), the L691 pool-target early-return is overridden by `!_vrfDeadmanFired()`, and `_gameOverEntropy`'s deadman branch (Advance L1384-1421) commits the historical fallback word via `_applyDailyRng` (writes `rngWordByDay[day]`, L2033) and falls through to the drain block in the same tx.

**3. The cohort is really excluded, with no later pickup — CONFIRMED.**
`processTicketBatch(N+1)` drains/latches on the `N+1` buffers only; L745-748 then toggles `_swapTicketSlot` and latches `ticketsFullyProcessed=true` while both `N`-keyed buffers still hold entries — the exact state the `_swapTicketSlot` comment (L1732-1737) calls "unreachable." `handleGameOverDrain` (GameOverModule L75-200) never touches ticket queues and calls `runTerminalJackpot(remaining, lvl + 1, rngWord)` (L198); `JackpotModule.runTerminalJackpot` (L238-274) samples winners from `lvlTraitEntry[targetLvl]` (`_processDailyEth`→`_processBucket`, L931 `lvlTraitEntry[lvl][trait]`), and `lvlTraitEntry` is populated only when a ticket entry is *materialized* by a drain at its queue level (Mint L544-585). Undrained key-`N` entries never materialize, `gameOver=true` routes every later advance to `handleFinalSweep` only (Advance L673-682), `entriesOwedPacked[N]` has no post-gameover reader, and the 30-day final sweep sends residue to vault/sDGNRS/GNRUS — a sink, not a self-heal. The exclusion is actually *wider* than claimed: on the locked-last-purchase deadman the terminal 90% samples `lvlTraitEntry[N+1]` (near-future buys only, possibly empty → funds fall through to the three-sink sweep), excluding even the already-materialized level-`N` cohort, directly contradicting the L710-711 "every purchased ticket is eligible" comment and the normal purchase-phase gameover where `lvl+1` is the correct selling-cohort key.

**Structural protections examined and none neutralize:** the L691 guard (deadman-overridden), the fail-open swap comment (assumption violated on this path), the final sweep (misdirects, doesn't restore), permissionless drains (gated behind the reverting advance and dead lootbox RNG). Reachability requires a 120-day total VRF death beginning in a suppressed phase — exactly the scenario the deadman path was built to serve (and which the deadman-prevrandao ruling treats as a supported terminal path), so "unreachable" is not available as a refutation. Funds are still released (no brick, no insolvency); the defect is a misdirected terminal distribution.

**Analysis (all lines a79a9809):**

**1. Drain key is unconditionally `lvl+1`.** In `_handleGameOverPath`, `lvl` = raw `level` (Advance L194 `uint24 lvl = level;` → L217 `_handleGameOverPath(day, lvl)`). The game-over drain block hard-codes `lvl + 1`: L730 `processTicketBatch.selector, lvl + 1` and L746 `_tqReadKey(lvl + 1)`. No `locked`/`inJackpot` conditioning — unlike the normal machinery, which uses `readKey(purchaseLevel)` where `purchaseLevel = (lastPurchase && locked) ? lvl : lvl+1` (L213) and drains `inJackpot ? lvl : purchaseLevel` (L503-505). The mid-day/daily drain gates likewise use `_tqReadKey(purchaseLevel)` (L240, L290). So on a locked last-purchase day the normal path drains `readKey(lvl)`; the game-over path drains `readKey(lvl+1)`. Asymmetry confirmed.

**2. Tickets really sit at `readKey(lvl)` on the deadman path.** Purchase-phase buys at game-level G route to `writeKey(G+1)` (Mint L1967 `targetLevel = cachedJpFlag ? cachedLevel : cachedLevel+1`; Storage `_queueEntries` L671-673). The last-purchase-day advance requests VRF via `rngGate(..., lastPurchase=true, ...)`, whose request branch calls `_requestRng(lastPurchase, purchaseLevel)` → `_finalizeRngRequest` L1831-1835 pre-increments `level = purchaseLevel = G+1`. Then L437 `_swapAndFreeze` → `_swapTicketSlot` flips `ticketWriteSlot` and sets `ticketsFullyProcessed=false`. Since `_tqWriteKey(l)=ticketWriteSlot?l|BIT:l` and `_tqReadKey(l)=!ticketWriteSlot?l|BIT:l` (Storage L848-855), the buffer the last-day buys went into (`f(W,G+1)`) is now `readKey(G+1)=readKey(lvl)`. The game-over drain reads `readKey(lvl+1)=f(W,G+2)` — a different, empty key.

**3. Read slot cannot drain before the deadman.** While `rngLockedFlag=true` and `rngWordCurrent=0`, each subsequent advance hits the daily drain gate (L289-330): `ticketQueue[readKey(purchaseLevel=lvl)].length>0`, but the lootbox/current word is 0 and the stall-promotion branch is gated on `!rngLockedFlag` — so it `revert RngNotReady()` (L330). The read slot stays undrained until VRF fulfills or the deadman opens game-over.

**4. Exclusion is real.** When `_vrfDeadmanFired()` fires (L216/L686, `_simulatedDayIndex()-dailyIdx > _VRF_DEADMAN_DAYS`), `_gameOverEntropy` commits the historical fallback word (deadman branch L1384-1421), then the drain runs `processTicketBatch(lvl+1)` on the empty `readKey(lvl+1)` → returns finished. `_swapTicketSlot` (L745) flips again, moving the `f(W,G+1)` tickets into a *write* position, and the `readKey(lvl+1)` check (L746) is empty → `ticketsFullyProcessed=true`. Next tx → `handleGameOverDrain` → `runTerminalJackpot(remaining, lvl+1, rngWord)` (GameOver L198). The `readKey(lvl)` cohort is never materialized, so per the module's own comment (L710-714, L721-724) it forfeits trait-bucket eligibility. The jackpot phase is identical: jackpot-phase buys route to `readKey(lvl)` (Mint L1967, `inJackpot` drain uses `lvl` at L504), yet game-over drains `lvl+1`.

**Concrete reaching sequence:** Level G purchase phase; players buy (→`writeKey(G+1)`); target met, `lastPurchaseDay` latched (L546). Next day advance requests VRF: `level`→G+1, `_swapAndFreeze` swaps last cohort into `readKey(G+1)`, `ticketsFullyProcessed=false`. VRF coordinator dies (no fulfillment). Subsequent advances `revert RngNotReady`. After `_VRF_DEADMAN_DAYS`, `_vrfDeadmanFired()` opens game-over; drain queries empty `readKey(G+2)`; the `readKey(G+1)` cohort (everyone who bought since the last swap) is excluded from `runTerminalJackpot(..., G+2, ...)`. No structural guard prevents this — the pre-increment (L1835) and the hard-coded `lvl+1` (L730/L746) are unconditional and directly contradict the conditional key the rest of the state machine uses.

Not the refuted post-game-over *insertion* case: this is *exclusion* of already-paid, already-queued entries via a key mismatch.

VERDICT: STANDS
SEVERITY-IF-STANDS: accounting

C2 — PERMANENT advanceGame BRICK via gap-backfill underflow. Purchase-phase VRF stall: rngGate backfills the gap and credits the FULL gap to purchaseStartDay (psd += gapDays, mirrors rngGate ~L1263), fills rngWordByDay for gap days, then defers (STAGE_GAP_BACKFILLED break ~L451). On later advances the RNGREUSE clamp (L190: if day>dIdx+1 && rngWordByDay[dIdx+1]!=0 then day=dIdx+1) rewinds day to dailyIdx+1 each call, resolving one backfilled day per advance. Once a walk day seals and _unlockRng clears rngLockedFlag, the next advance reaches the turbo gate (L202, !locked true) and computes `uint32 purchaseDays = day - psd` (L203) with clamped day < bumped psd -> uint24 checked-subtraction PANIC, which precedes the game-over gate (L216) so there is no liveness escape = permanent brick. Also L547 (day - psd <= 3). Concrete: level opens day100, days100-101 sealed (dailyIdx=101), stall 8 days, word arrives wall-day110; advance1 backfills 102-109 psd=108; advance2 clamps day=102 seals; advance3 clamps day=103 unlocked -> 103-108 panics.

---
name: contract-auditor
description: Adversarial security auditor and gas optimization specialist for Degenerus Protocol contracts. Use when the user requests audits, gas-limit checks (especially advanceGame(0)), RNG/VRF exploit analysis, MEV/griefing scenarios, or economic attack reviews of Degenerus Solidity code.
---

# Contract Auditor

## Overview
Perform adversarial security review and gas optimization analysis of Degenerus contracts, focusing on RNG safety, gas limits, and economic exploits.

## Workflow

1. Scope and priorities
   - Ask for target contracts/modules, known concerns, and whether to focus on gas, RNG, or full audit.
   - Confirm if `advanceGame(0)` gas analysis should be first.

2. Map the surface
   - Identify external entry points, fund flows, RNG consumption points, and external calls.
   - Note delegatecall modules and trust boundaries.

3. Deep dive by mandate
   - Gas: trace `advanceGame(0)` and all loops; estimate worst-case bounds.
   - RNG: model request/fulfill windows; test for front-run, selective execution, and input grinding.
   - Economics: check for flash-loan, griefing, or game-theoretic exploits.

4. Report findings
   - Use the standard finding format from `references/output-format.md`.
   - Order findings by severity; provide PoCs or proof of impossibility where applicable.

## Response Rules

- Think like a 1000 ETH attacker; prioritize exploitability.
- Assume users are adversarial; external contracts are untrusted.
- Prefer code and tests for proof over opinion.
- If a doc conflicts with code, defer to code and flag mismatch.

## References

- `references/audit-brief.md` for mandates, attack scenarios, and methodology.
- `references/output-format.md` for required reporting format.

# Degenerus Contract Auditor Brief

## Mindset
- Adversarial: 1000 ETH budget, goal is to exploit, grief, or break the protocol.
- Focus: gas limits, RNG exploitation, economic attacks, MEV/griefing.

## Primary Mandates

### 1) Gas Limit: `advanceGame(0)` must remain < 15M gas
- Trace all code paths from `advanceGame(0)`.
- Identify loops and maximum bounds.
- Consider state accumulation across many levels.
- Flag unbounded or user-controlled iterations.

### 2) RNG Security: zero exploitability on money-critical outcomes
Audit RNG use in:
- Jackpot winner selection
- Coinflip resolution
- Decimator outcomes
- Lootbox prizes
- Degenerette outcomes
- Trait extermination tiebreakers (if any)

Attack vectors:
- VRF result seen in mempool before use
- Selective accept/reject of RNG result
- Input grinding that combines with VRF
- Block builder collusion
- Timing around rngLockedFlag
- Index-based protection bypass

Key question: If I could see random value 1 block early, what could I do?

### 3) Gas Efficiency
- Cache repeated SLOADs
- Avoid storage writes in loops
- Tighten loop bounds
- Minimize event data and external calls
- Use calldata for read-only data where possible

### 4) Security Best Practices
- External contracts (Chainlink, stETH) are untrusted
- Watch for: access control bypass, unchecked blocks, DoS via revert, timestamp manipulation, delegatecall to untrusted target

## Attack Scenarios to Explore

### Economic
- Flash loan amplification
- Griefing (spend 10 ETH to deny 100 ETH)
- MEV (sandwich/backrun)
- Game-theoretic manipulation (traits, activity score)

### RNG
- Timing between request/fulfill/use
- Entropy manipulation via inputs
- Selective execution or callback DoS

### Gas
- State bloat across many levels/players
- Callback bombs on external calls
- Loop exploitation and worst-case iterations

## Audit Methodology

1) Architecture review: entry points, fund flows, RNG uses, state transitions, external calls
2) Gas analysis: measure `advanceGame(0)` worst-case paths
3) RNG security: model windows and mitigations
4) Adversarial testing: construct attacks and verify failure
5) Reporting: severity, repro steps, fixes, gas savings
# Reporting Format

## Finding Template

## [SEVERITY] Title

**Location:** `Contract.sol:function():L123-145`

**Description:**
What the issue is and why it matters.

**Attack Scenario:**
Step-by-step how an attacker would exploit this.

**Impact:**
What value is at risk or what denial of service is possible.

**Proof of Concept:**
Code or trace demonstrating the issue.

**Recommendation:**
How to fix it, with code if applicable.

**Gas Impact:** (if relevant)
Current: X gas | Proposed: Y gas | Savings: Z gas

## Gas Optimization Template

## [GAS] Title

**Location:** `Contract.sol:function():L123`

**Current Implementation:**
```solidity
// current code
```

**Proposed Implementation:**
```solidity
// optimized code
```

**Savings:** X gas per call
**Rationale:** Why this is safe and correct.

   146	    uint96 private constant MIN_LINK_FOR_LOOTBOX_RNG = 40 ether;
   147	    uint48 private constant MIDDAY_RNG_STALL_TIMEOUT = 4 hours;
   148	
   149	    /// @dev Per-call afking process-STAGE gas-weight budget. Every day is uniform: the streak is
   150	    ///      computed on read from the Sub slot (no per-buy `playerQuestStates` STATICCALL, no
   151	    ///      settle day), so there is a SINGLE budget. The STAGE consumes a gas-weight per
   152	    ///      iteration — buys and finalizes are weighted by true marginal cost (a lootbox buy
   153	    ///      ≈34k = `SUB_STAGE_LOOTBOX_WEIGHT` (10), a ticket buy ≈73k = `SUB_STAGE_TICKET_WEIGHT`
   154	    ///      (21), a cross-contract sub-ending finalize / pass-evict / funding-kill ≈27k =
   155	    ///      `SUB_STAGE_EVICT_WEIGHT` (7)) — and ends the chunk on accumulated weight, not raw
   156	    ///      count, so EVERY composition (including a saturated all-evict swap-pop chunk) stays on
   157	    ///      the <10M target with deep headroom to the 16.7M advance-chain ceiling. The lootbox and
   158	    ///      ticket per-chunk counts are unchanged from the prior calibration; only the evict chunk
   159	    ///      shrinks (≈500 → ≈357 finalizes) so a saturated all-evict crank lands ≈9.5M, not ≈13.6M.
   160	    ///      A large set drains across several advanceGame calls.
   161	    uint256 private constant SUB_STAGE_WEIGHT_BUDGET = 2500;
   162	
   163	    /// @notice DGNRS reward for top affiliate: 1% of remaining affiliate pool.
   164	    uint16 private constant AFFILIATE_POOL_REWARD_BPS = 100;
   165	
   166	    /// @notice Max share of affiliate DGNRS pool segregated per level for claims (5%).
   167	    uint16 private constant AFFILIATE_DGNRS_LEVEL_BPS = 500;
   168	
   169	    /// @notice Advance game state. Called daily to process jackpots, mints, and phase transitions.
   170	    ///         Returns mult: the day-epoch stall multiplier (1 base / 2 / 4 / 6 by stall; 0 on
   171	    ///         the gameover path = no bounty). Standalone callers earn nothing — the unified
   172	    ///         afking router pays the re-homed bounty (2x * mult) only when mult > 0.
   173	    function advanceGame() external returns (uint8 mult) {
   174	        mult = 1;
   175	        uint48 ts = uint48(block.timestamp);
   176	        uint24 day = _simulatedDayIndexAt(ts);
   177	        // dailyIdx and rngLockedFlag are stable across every read below: their
   178	        // only writers (_unlockRng, _finalizeRngRequest) execute after the last
   179	        // use of these locals, or on paths that return before reaching it.
   180	        uint24 dIdx = dailyIdx;
   181	        bool locked = rngLockedFlag;
   182	        // RNGREUSE guard: never resolve a NEW wall-day with a prior day's still-unsealed
   183	        // VRF word. If the in-progress day (dailyIdx+1) already recorded its word but was
   184	        // not yet sealed (_unlockRng deferred behind chunked drains / a pending daily
   185	        // jackpot / a phase transition) and the wall-clock has moved past it, clamp this
   186	        // advance to that day so its OWN word resolves it: rngGate returns the cached word,
   187	        // the deferred jackpot half stays on its Phase-1 word, and the afking box keeps a
   188	        // real word. The next advance picks up the wall-day with a fresh VRF request.
   189	        // Distinct from a VRF gap stall (rngWordByDay[dailyIdx+1] == 0 → backfill path).
   190	        if (day > dIdx + 1 && rngWordByDay[dIdx + 1] != 0) {
   191	            day = dIdx + 1;
   192	        }
   193	        bool inJackpot = jackpotPhaseFlag;
   194	        uint24 lvl = level;
   195	        uint24 psd = purchaseStartDay;
   196	        // Turbo: if target already met on day ≤1, flag now so the upcoming
   197	        // _requestRng does the level pre-increment (matching normal
   198	        // lastPurchaseDay flow). Skipped when rngLockedFlag is set because
   199	        // rngGate will take the fresh-word path instead of _requestRng, so
   200	        // the level pre-increment would be missed and the (lastPurchase &&
   201	        // rngLockedFlag) ternary below would compute purchaseLevel = 0.
   202	        if (!inJackpot && !lastPurchaseDay && !locked) {
   203	            uint32 purchaseDays = day - psd;
   204	            if (
   205	                purchaseDays <= 1 && _getNextPrizePool() >= levelPrizePool[lvl]
   206	            ) {
   207	                lastPurchaseDay = true;
   208	                compressedJackpotFlag = 2;
   209	            }
   210	        }
   211	        bool lastPurchase = (!inJackpot) && lastPurchaseDay;
   212	        // Level already incremented at RNG request when lastPurchase=true
   213	        uint24 purchaseLevel = (lastPurchase && locked) ? lvl : lvl + 1;
   214	        // The VRF-death deadman also enters here during jackpot / last-purchase, where the
   215	        // normal !inJackpot && !lastPurchase gate would otherwise skip the game-over path.
   216	        if ((!inJackpot && !lastPurchase) || _vrfDeadmanFired()) {
   217	            (bool goReturn, uint8 goStage) = _handleGameOverPath(day, lvl);
   218	            if (goReturn) {
   219	                // Gameover path: advance ran but earns NO router bounty (the flip-credit
   220	                // coin is worthless at gameover) — return mult = 0 so mintFlip pays nothing.

   419	            // RNG: use existing word or request new one. Precompute the day's coinflip reward

   421	            // day), +6 on a post-BAF x0-level first-jackpot-day (levels 10, 20, 30, …; level 0

   423	            // player nets ~99.9% / ~101.9% RTP once the 0.75% recycle bonus compounds in.

   442	            // Decouple a multi-day VRF-stall gap backfill from the day's jackpot distribution:

   484	            }

   486	            // Process near-future ticket queues before daily draws

   499	
   500	            // Process current level tickets:

   503	            (bool ticketWorked, bool ticketsFinished) = _runProcessTicketBatch(

   505	            );

   540	                            purchaseLevel + 4
   541	                        );

  1208	    // BIT ALLOCATION MAP for VRF random word (currentWord after _applyDailyRng):
  1209	    //
  1210	    // Bit(s)   Consumer                    Operation                         Location
  1211	    // ------   --------                    ---------                         --------
  1212	    // 0        Coinflip win/loss           rngWord & 1                       Coinflip._resolveDay
  1213	    // 0        BAF fire gate               rngWord & 1                       AdvanceModule._consolidatePoolsAndRewardJackpots
  1214	    // 8+       Redemption roll             (currentWord >> 8) % 151 + 25     AdvanceModule.rngGate
  1215	    // full     Coinflip reward percent     keccak256(rngWord, epoch) % 20    Coinflip._resolveDay
  1216	    // full     Jackpot winner selection    via delegatecall (full word)      JackpotModule (payDailyJackpot)
  1217	    // full     Coin jackpot                via delegatecall (full word)      JackpotModule (_payDailyCoinJackpot)
  1218	    // full     Lootbox RNG                 stored as lootboxRngWordByIndex   AdvanceModule._applyDailyRng
  1219	    // full     Future take variance        rngWord % (variance * 2 + 1)      AdvanceModule._takeFuturePrizePoolSlice
  1220	    // full     Prize pool consolidation    via delegatecall (full word)      JackpotModule (consolidatePrizePools)
  1221	    // full     Reward jackpots             via delegatecall (full word)      JackpotModule (_runRewardJackpots)
  1222	    //
  1223	    // NOTE: Bits 0 and 8+ are the only direct bit-level consumers.
  1224	    //       All "full" consumers use modular arithmetic or keccak mixing,
  1225	    //       so bit overlap with bits 0 and 8+ is not a collision concern.
  1226	
  1227	    /// @dev Daily RNG processing gate called during advanceGame. Applies VRF word,
  1228	    ///      processes coinflip payouts, rolls daily quest, resolves pending gambling
  1229	    ///      burn redemptions, stores lootbox RNG, and handles VRF timeout retries (12h).
  1230	    function rngGate(
  1231	        uint48 ts,
  1232	        uint24 day,
  1233	        uint24 lvl,
  1234	        bool isTicketJackpotDay,
  1235	        uint8 coinflipBonus
  1236	    ) internal returns (uint256 word, uint32 gapDays) {
  1237	        // Already recorded for today
  1238	        uint256 recordedWord = rngWordByDay[day];
  1239	        if (recordedWord != 0) return (recordedWord, 0);
  1240	
  1241	        uint256 currentWord = rngWordCurrent;
  1242	
  1243	        // Have a fresh VRF word ready
  1244	        if (currentWord != 0 && rngRequestTime != 0) {
  1245	            // Backfill gap days from VRF stall before processing current day.
  1246	            // Gated on rngWordByDay[idx + 1] == 0 so the backfill runs at
  1247	            // most once per lock window: dailyIdx is only updated by
  1248	            // _unlockRng, so a multi-day drain would otherwise re-enter
  1249	            // this branch on each new wall-clock day and re-process the
  1250	            // same gap range, doubling purchaseStartDay and re-running
  1251	            // coinflip payouts for already-resolved days.
  1252	            uint24 idx = dailyIdx;
  1253	            if (day > idx + 1 && rngWordByDay[idx + 1] == 0) {
  1254	                uint24 gapCount = day - idx - 1;
  1255	                _backfillGapDays(currentWord, idx + 1, day);
  1256	
  1257	                // Backfill any lootbox indices that never got a VRF word (orphaned by stall).
  1258	                // Uses fresh VRF entropy, not predictable on-chain state.
  1259	                _backfillOrphanedLootboxIndices(currentWord);
  1260	
  1261	                // Extend death clock by the stall duration -- gap days don't count toward
  1262	                // the 120-day inactivity timeout since the game was stalled, not abandoned.
  1263	                purchaseStartDay += gapCount;
  1264	                gapDays = gapCount;
  1265	            }
  1266	
  1267	            // Normal daily RNG processing (request from current day)
  1268	            currentWord = _applyDailyRng(day, currentWord);
  1269	            coinflip.processCoinflipPayouts(coinflipBonus, currentWord, day);
  1270	            // Force the MINT_FLIP daily on the first jackpot day (lastPurchaseDay still set here,
  1271	            // jackpot not yet entered) so the FLIP-mint quest only lands when the redeem window is
  1272	            // live. Turbo (compressedJackpotFlag == 2) is skipped — its jackpot collapses at this
  1273	            // request, leaving no full open day for that quest.
  1274	            // Force the buy-a-foil-pack daily on the first purchase day of a level:
  1275	            // phaseTransitionActive is set at level end (_endPhase) and cleared only once
  1276	            // the transition completes (line 440), and this roll runs before that completion
  1277	            // in the same advance — so it is true exactly on the day the new purchase phase
  1278	            // opens. Gated on gapDays == 0 so a VRF-stall backfill (which defers the whole
  1279	            // transition to the next advance, line 412) does not roll the foil quest early.
  1280	            // Mutually exclusive with the first-jackpot-day MINT_FLIP force (opposite cycle ends).
  1281	            quests.rollDailyQuest(
  1282	                day,
  1283	                currentWord,
  1284	                lastPurchaseDay && compressedJackpotFlag != 2,
  1285	                phaseTransitionActive && gapDays == 0
  1286	            );
  1287	
  1288	            // Resolve the sentinel-stamped gambling-burn pool if any. Reading the
  1289	            // sentinel rather than deriving `day - 1` makes multi-day RNG stalls correct by
  1290	            // construction: the sentinel always names the (at most one) unresolved day, so a
  1291	            // single resolve call after the stall recovers covers the stuck pool exactly.
  1292	            {
  1293	                IsDGNRS sdgnrs = IsDGNRS(
  1294	                    ContractAddresses.SDGNRS
  1295	                );
  1296	                uint24 toResolve = sdgnrs.pendingResolveDay();
  1297	                if (toResolve != 0) {
  1298	                    uint16 redemptionRoll = uint16(
  1299	                        ((currentWord >> 8) % 151) + 25
  1300	                    );
  1301	                    sdgnrs.resolveRedemptionPeriod(redemptionRoll, toResolve);
  1302	                }
  1303	            }
  1304	
  1305	            _finalizeLootboxRng(currentWord);
  1306	            return (currentWord, gapDays);
  1307	        }
  1308	
  1309	        // Waiting for VRF - check for timeout retry. A daily request (rngLockedFlag) gets the
  1310	        // 12h VRF retry; a lootbox-only mid-day request (rngLockedFlag == false) that bled past
  1311	        // the day boundary is abandoned after MIDDAY_RNG_STALL_TIMEOUT and promoted to this day's
  1312	        // daily request — _requestRng's isRetry path keeps the reserved index, so the fresh daily
  1313	        // word finalizes that bucket just as the mid-day word would have.
  1314	        if (rngRequestTime != 0) {
  1315	            uint48 elapsed = ts - rngRequestTime;
  1316	            uint48 timeout = rngLockedFlag ? 12 hours : MIDDAY_RNG_STALL_TIMEOUT;
  1317	            if (elapsed >= timeout) {
  1318	                _requestRng(isTicketJackpotDay, lvl);
  1319	                return (1, 0);
  1320	            }
  1321	            revert RngNotReady();
  1322	        }
  1323	
  1324	        // Need fresh RNG
  1325	        _requestRng(isTicketJackpotDay, lvl);

   624	    /*+========================================================================================+
   625	      |                    ADMIN VRF FUNCTIONS                                                 |
   626	      +========================================================================================+
   627	      |  Deploy-only VRF setup called from the ContractAddresses.ADMIN constructor.            |
   628	      |  Post-deploy VRF changes use updateVrfCoordinatorAndSub (emergency rotation).          |
   629	      +========================================================================================+*/
   630	
   631	    /// @notice Wire VRF config, called once from the ADMIN constructor during deployment.
   632	    /// @dev Access: ContractAddresses.ADMIN only. No post-deploy caller exists on ADMIN;
   633	    ///      emergency VRF rotation uses updateVrfCoordinatorAndSub instead.
   634	    /// @param coordinator_ Chainlink VRF V2.5 coordinator address.
   635	    /// @param subId VRF subscription ID for LINK billing.
   636	    /// @param keyHash_ VRF key hash for gas lane selection.
   637	    function wireVrf(
   638	        address coordinator_,
   639	        uint256 subId,
   640	        bytes32 keyHash_
   641	    ) external {
   642	        if (msg.sender != ContractAddresses.ADMIN) revert OnlyAdmin();
   643	
   644	        address current = address(vrfCoordinator);
   645	        _setVrfConfig(coordinator_, subId, keyHash_);
   646	        lastVrfProcessedTimestamp = uint48(block.timestamp);
   647	        emit VrfCoordinatorUpdated(current, coordinator_);
   648	    }
   649	
   650	    /*+======================================================================+
   651	      |                    GAMEOVER / LIVENESS GUARDS                        |
   652	      +======================================================================+*/
   653	
   654	    /// @dev Handles gameover state and liveness guard checks.
   655	    ///      Returns (shouldReturn, stage). shouldReturn=true means advanceGame
   656	    ///      should emit `stage` and exit. Stages used:
   657	    ///         STAGE_GAMEOVER -- normal game-over completion or final sweep
   658	    ///         STAGE_TICKETS_WORKING -- partial best-effort drain; caller retries
   659	    function _handleGameOverPath(
   660	        uint24 day,
   661	        uint24 lvl
   662	    ) private returns (bool shouldReturn, uint8 stage) {
   663	        // Liveness guard: prevent permanent lockup if game is abandoned.
   664	        // Uses the shared _livenessTriggered() helper so purchase paths (in
   665	        // DegenerusGameMintModule) can reuse the same predicate to block new
   666	        // purchases during the multi-tx game-over drain sequence.
   667	        bool ok;
   668	        bytes memory data;
   669	
   670	        // gameOver check precedes liveness so the post-gameover final-sweep path
   671	        // stays reachable after the VRF-dead path latches gameOver with day-math
   672	        // still below the 120/365 threshold (e.g., VRF breaks on day 14).
   673	        if (gameOver) {
   674	            // Post-gameover: check for final sweep (1 month after gameover)
   675	            (ok, data) = ContractAddresses.GAME_GAMEOVER_MODULE.delegatecall(
   676	                abi.encodeWithSelector(
   677	                    IDegenerusGameGameOverModule.handleFinalSweep.selector
   678	                )
   679	            );
   680	            if (!ok) _revertDelegate(data);
   681	            return (true, STAGE_GAMEOVER);
   682	        }
   683	
   684	        // _livenessTriggered now folds in the VRF-death deadman, so it returns true here even
   685	        // during jackpot / last-purchase when the game has been stalled past the deadman.
   686	        if (!_livenessTriggered()) return (false, 0);
   687	
   688	        // Safety: don't activate game over if nextPool requirement is already met — but the
   689	        // VRF-death deadman overrides it: a permanently-stalled game must drain even if its pool
   690	        // target reads as met.
   691	        if (
   692	            lvl != 0 &&
   693	            _getNextPrizePool() >= levelPrizePool[lvl] &&
   694	            !_vrfDeadmanFired()
   695	        ) {
   696	            return (false, 0);
   697	        }
   698
