   311	    ///      stay under 15M gas block limit. Cleared after coin+ticket distribution.
   312	    bool internal dailyJackpotCoinTicketsPending;
   313	
   314	    /// @dev Jackpot compression tier: 0=normal (5d), 1=compressed (3d), 2=turbo (1d).
   315	    ///      Set when purchase-phase target is met quickly, signaling high player interest.
   316	    ///      Turbo (2): target met within 1 day — entire jackpot in 1 physical day.
   317	    ///      Compressed (1): target met within 3 days — 5 logical days in 3 physical.
   318	    ///      Cleared at phase end.
   319	    uint8 internal compressedJackpotFlag;
   320	
   321	    /// @dev True when the read slot has been fully drained (all tickets processed).
   322	    ///      Gate for RNG requests and jackpot logic in advanceGame daily path.
   323	    ///
   324	    ///      SECURITY: Must be set to true before any jackpot/phase logic executes.
   325	    ///      Reset to false on every queue slot swap.
   326	    bool internal ticketsFullyProcessed;
   327	
   328	    // EVM SLOT 0 (continued): Double-Buffer + Freeze
   329	
   330	    /// @dev Active write buffer toggle for ticket queue double-buffering.
   331	    ///      Toggled via negation (`ticketWriteSlot = !ticketWriteSlot`) during queue slot swaps.
   332	    ///      Write path uses this value; read path uses the opposite.
   333	    ///
   334	    ///      SECURITY: bool toggle via negation. Only values false/true are valid.
   335	    bool internal ticketWriteSlot;
   336	
   337	    /// @dev True when purchase revenue redirects to pending accumulators.
   338	    ///      Set at daily RNG request time; cleared by _unfreezePool().
   339	    ///
   340	    ///      SECURITY: Persists across jackpot phase days. All 5 jackpot payouts
   341	    ///      use pre-freeze pool values. _unfreezePool is the single control point.
   342	    bool internal prizePoolFrozen;
   343	
   344	    /// @dev Latching terminal for the coin-presale-box window. Set once, in the
   345	    ///      box purchase that crosses the 50-ETH cumulative box cap. While false,
   346	    ///      ETH buys accrue presaleBoxCredit; once true, no further box buys or
   347	    ///      credit accrual occur. Occupies slot-0 padding byte [30:31].
   348	    bool internal presaleOver;
   349	
   350	    /// @dev Afking process-STAGE drain-completion flag — the subscriber-drain sibling
   351	    ///      of `ticketsFullyProcessed`. False while the STAGE is stamping the funded
   352	    ///      subscriber set this day; set true once the set is fully drained; flipped back
   353	    ///      to false forward-looking at the start of the next day (the advance's
   354	    ///      `_afkingResetDay != day` gate), so it always reflects "afking done for the
   355	    ///      current day". Packed into slot-0 byte [31:32] alongside `level` /
   356	    ///      `rngLockedFlag` / `ticketsFullyProcessed`, which the advance-path STAGE
   357	    ///      already SLOADs, so its read/write is free.
   358	    bool internal subsFullyProcessed;
   359	
   360	    /// @dev One-way "all coin-presale boxes have been opened" flag. False until the auto-open sweep
   361	    ///      has advanced its cursor PAST presaleCloseIndex — i.e. every box at indices <= the close
   362	    ///      index is opened, so none can remain. Lives in slot-0 padding byte [30:31], which every
   363	    ///      open path already SLOADs (`level` / `rngLockedFlag`), so the gate `!presaleDrained` is a
   364	    ///      free read: once set, the post-presale sweep AND manual opens skip the cold presaleBoxEth
   365	    ///      SLOAD. Flipped only by the in-order sweep (never the manual path), so an out-of-order
   366	    ///      manual open of the closing box cannot trip it early and strand a still-queued box.
   367	    bool internal presaleDrained;
   368	
   369	    /// @dev FLIP ticket purchase window latch. redeemFlip lazily opens it the moment the prize
   370	    ///      target is met in the purchase phase (_getNextPrizePool() >= levelPrizePool[level], with no
   371	    ///      RNG in flight); it persists through the jackpot days and is cleared in the advance at the
   372	    ///      final jackpot day's RNG request — the same boundary where new tickets route to the next
   373	    ///      level. While closed, FLIP ticket purchases revert, so FLIP tickets only ever join a
   374	    ///      happening jackpot, never an open/stalled purchase phase. Occupies slot-0 byte [31:32], which
   375	    ///      the purchase/advance paths already SLOAD, so the gate is a free read.
   376	    bool internal ticketRedemptionOpen;
   377	
   378	    // =========================================================================
   379	    // EVM SLOT 1: Prize Pools
   380	    // =========================================================================

  1876	        // fulfills once funded. If the new coordinator also stalls, the daily advance abandons a
  1877	        // mid-day request and promotes it to the daily word after MIDDAY_RNG_STALL_TIMEOUT.
  1878	        if (_lrRead(LR_MID_DAY_SHIFT, LR_MID_DAY_MASK) != 0) {

  1902	
  1903	    /// @dev Unlock RNG after processing is complete for the day.
  1904	    ///      Resets VRF state and re-enables RNG usage.
  1905	    /// @param day Current day index to record.
  1906	    function _unlockRng(uint24 day) private {
  1907	        dailyIdx = day;
  1908	        rngLockedFlag = false;
  1909	        rngWordCurrent = 0;
  1910	        vrfRequestId = 0;
  1911	        rngRequestTime = 0;
  1912	        _unfreezePool();
  1913	        // The day-seal is the one chokepoint every completed game-day passes through (purchase
  1914	        // daily, jackpot coin+tickets, phase transition). Emit the daily pool snapshot here, after
  1915	        // _unfreezePool folds the pending accumulators back into the live pools, so the indexer
  1916	        // mirrors the settled end-of-day pools and a solvency total (ETH + stETH) from logs alone.
  1917	        // Game-over also seals here but emits its own terminal snapshot in the drain, so skip it.
  1918	        if (!gameOver) {
  1919	            emit PrizePoolDailySnapshot(
  1920	                _getNextPrizePool(),
  1921	                _getFuturePrizePool(),
  1922	                _getCurrentPrizePool(),
  1923	                claimablePool,
  1924	                address(this).balance + steth.balanceOf(address(this)),
  1925	                yieldAccumulator
  1926	            );
  1927	        }
  1928	    }
  1929	
  1930	    /// @notice Chainlink VRF callback for random word fulfillment.
  1931	    /// @dev Access: VRF coordinator only.
  1932	    ///      Daily RNG: stores word for advanceGame processing (nudges applied there).
  1933	    ///      Mid-day RNG: directly finalizes lootbox RNG, no advanceGame needed.
  1934	    ///      SECURITY: Validates requestId and coordinator address.
  1935	    /// @param requestId The request ID to match.
  1936	    /// @param randomWords Array containing the random word (length 1).
  1937	    function rawFulfillRandomWords(
  1938	        uint256 requestId,
  1939	        uint256[] calldata randomWords
  1940	    ) external {
  1941	        if (msg.sender != address(vrfCoordinator)) revert OnlyCoordinator();
  1942	        if (requestId != vrfRequestId || rngWordCurrent != 0) return;
  1943	
  1944	        uint256 word = randomWords[0];
  1945	        if (word == 0) word = 1;
  1946	
  1947	        if (rngLockedFlag) {
  1948	            // Daily RNG: store for advanceGame processing (nudges applied there)
  1949	            rngWordCurrent = word;
  1950	        } else {
  1951	            // Mid-day RNG: directly finalize lootbox and clear state
  1952	            uint48 index = uint48(_lrRead(LR_INDEX_SHIFT, LR_INDEX_MASK)) - 1;
  1953	            lootboxRngWordByIndex[index] = word;
  1954	            emit LootboxRngApplied(index, word, requestId);
  1955	            vrfRequestId = 0;
  1956	            rngRequestTime = 0;
  1957	        }
  1958	    }
  1959	
  1960	    /// @dev Backfill rngWordByDay and process coinflip payouts for gap days
  1961	    ///      caused by VRF stall. Derives deterministic words from the first
  1962	    ///      post-gap VRF word via keccak256(vrfWord, gapDay).
  1963	    ///      NOTE: Gap days get zero nudges (totalFlipReversals not consumed).
  1964	    ///      NOTE: resolveRedemptionPeriod is NOT called for backfilled gap days —
  1965	    ///      the redemption timer continued ticking in real time during the stall;
  1966	    ///      it resolves only on the current day via the normal rngGate path.
  1967	    /// @param vrfWord The first post-gap VRF random word.
  1968	    /// @param startDay First gap day (dailyIdx + 1).
  1969	    /// @param endDay Current day (exclusive — not backfilled, handled by normal path).
  1970	    function _backfillGapDays(
  1971	        uint256 vrfWord,
  1972	        uint24 startDay,
  1973	        uint24 endDay
  1974	    ) private {
  1975	        // Cap at 120 gap days to stay within block gas limit (~9M gas).
  1976	        // Backfills oldest days first (most likely to have active coinflips).
  1977	        if (endDay - startDay > 120) endDay = startDay + 120;
  1978	        for (uint24 gapDay = startDay; gapDay < endDay; ) {
  1979	            uint256 derivedWord = uint256(
  1980	                keccak256(abi.encodePacked(vrfWord, gapDay))
  1981	            );
  1982	            if (derivedWord == 0) derivedWord = 1;
  1983	            rngWordByDay[gapDay] = derivedWord;
  1984	            // Gap days are calendar days that elapsed during the stall (no advance ran on
  1985	            // them), so none is a level-0 or first-jackpot day — always non-bonus (0).
  1986	            coinflip.processCoinflipPayouts(0, derivedWord, gapDay);
  1987	            emit DailyRngApplied(gapDay, derivedWord, 0, derivedWord);
  1988	            unchecked {
  1989	                ++gapDay;
  1990	            }
  1991	        }
  1992	    }
  1993	
  1994	    /// @dev Backfill any lootbox RNG indices that never received a VRF word.
  1995	    ///      Scans backwards from lootboxRngIndex - 1 until hitting a filled index.
  1996	    ///      Uses VRF-derived entropy so lootbox outcomes cannot be front-run.
  1997	    /// @param vrfWord Fresh VRF word from the post-gap callback.
  1998	    function _backfillOrphanedLootboxIndices(uint256 vrfWord) private {
  1999	        uint48 idx = uint48(_lrRead(LR_INDEX_SHIFT, LR_INDEX_MASK));
  2000	        if (idx <= 1) return; // nothing reserved yet
  2001	
  2002	        // Scan backwards from the most recent reserved index
  2003	        for (uint48 i = idx - 1; i >= 1; ) {
  2004	            if (lootboxRngWordByIndex[i] != 0) break; // hit a filled index, done
  2005	
  2006	            uint256 fallbackWord = uint256(
  2007	                keccak256(abi.encodePacked(vrfWord, i))
  2008	            );
  2009	            if (fallbackWord == 0) fallbackWord = 1;
  2010	            lootboxRngWordByIndex[i] = fallbackWord;
  2011	            emit LootboxRngApplied(i, fallbackWord, 0);
  2012	
  2013	            unchecked {
  2014	                --i;
  2015	            }
  2016	        }
  2017	    }
  2018	
  2019	    /// @dev Apply daily RNG nudges, record the word, and emit the finalized word.
  2020	    function _applyDailyRng(
  2021	        uint24 day,
  2022	        uint256 rawWord
  2023	    ) private returns (uint256 finalWord) {
  2024	        uint256 nudges = totalFlipReversals;
  2025	        finalWord = rawWord;
  2026	        if (nudges != 0) {
  2027	            unchecked {
  2028	                finalWord += nudges;
  2029	            }
  2030	            totalFlipReversals = 0;
  2031	        }
  2032	        rngWordCurrent = finalWord;
  2033	        rngWordByDay[day] = finalWord;
  2034	        lastVrfProcessedTimestamp = uint48(block.timestamp);
  2035	        emit DailyRngApplied(day, rawWord, nudges, finalWord);
  2036	    }
  2037	}

   358	            // --- Afking process STAGE: stamp the funded subscriber set BEFORE the day
   359	            // requests its RNG. Inserted on the new-day path only, after the daily

   361	            // returns earlier, so the STAGE never runs mid-day. Chunked by SUB_STAGE_BATCH across advance calls
   362	            // (BUY_BATCH-style) so a large set stays under the 16.7M advance-chain
   363	            // ceiling — mirrors the ticketsFullyProcessed partial-drain discipline:
   364	            // break + return mult while !subsFullyProcessed; set true only at cursor
   365	            // end; then fall through to rngGate.
   366	            //
   367	            // The STAGE runs strictly pre-RNG (before rngGate writes the day's word), so
   368	            // rngWordByDay[processDay] is uncommitted when a sub is stamped — the
   369	            // load-bearing freeze property. The box reads the LIVE level +
   370	            // rngWordByDay[lastAutoBoughtDay] at open.
   371	            //
   372	            // Forward-looking per-day reset: the first time the advance enters a new `day`,
   373	            // flip the drain gate + cursor BEFORE that day's STAGE runs. subsFullyProcessed
   374	            // stays true after a day's STAGE completes (it means "afking done for that day")
   375	            // until the next day flips it here. Stamped to `day` at the reset, so it fires
   376	            // exactly once per day and never re-fires within the day (independent of when
   377	            // dailyIdx catches up in _unlockRng).
   378	            if (_afkingResetDay != day) {
   379	                _afkingResetDay = day;
   380	                subsFullyProcessed = false;
   381	                _subCursor = 0;
   382	            }

   384	                if (_subscribers.length != 0) {

  1743	    /// @dev Activate the prize pool freeze. If not already frozen, pre-seeds the pending
  1744	    ///      future-pool buffer with 1% of futurePrizePool so Degenerette ETH wins can resolve
  1745	    ///      during freeze without waiting for bet inflow. Unconsumed remainder rolls back to
  1746	    ///      futurePool via _unfreezePool. If already frozen (jackpot phase), accumulators keep
  1747	    ///      growing.
  1748	    function _freezePool() internal {
  1749	        if (!prizePoolFrozen) {
  1750	            prizePoolFrozen = true;
  1751	            uint256 futureBal = _getFuturePrizePool();
  1752	            uint256 seed = futureBal / 100;
  1753	            if (seed != 0) {
  1754	                _setFuturePrizePool(futureBal - seed);
  1755	                _setPendingPools(0, uint128(seed));
  1756	            } else {
  1757	                prizePoolPendingPacked = 0;
  1758	            }
  1759	        }
  1760	    }
  1761	
  1762	    /// @dev Swap queue buffer AND activate the prize pool freeze (daily RNG path only).
  1763	    function _swapAndFreeze() internal {
  1764	        _swapTicketSlot();
  1765	        _freezePool();
  1766	    }
  1767	
  1768	    /// @dev Apply pending accumulators to live pools and clear freeze.
  1769	    ///      No-op if not currently frozen.
  1770	    function _unfreezePool() internal {
  1771	        if (!prizePoolFrozen) return;
  1772	        (uint128 pNext, uint128 pFuture) = _getPendingPools();
  1773	        (uint128 next, uint128 future) = _getPrizePools();
  1774	        _setPrizePools(next + pNext, future + pFuture);
  1775	        prizePoolPendingPacked = 0;
  1776	        prizePoolFrozen = false;
  1777	    }
  1778	
  1779	    function _finalizeRngRequest(
  1780	        bool isTicketJackpotDay,
  1781	        uint24 lvl,
  1782	        uint256 requestId
  1783	    ) private {
  1784	        // isRetry: some VRF request already reserved the lootbox index (a daily retry OR an
  1785	        // in-flight mid-day lootbox request) — so the index must not be advanced again.
  1786	        bool isRetry = vrfRequestId != 0 &&
  1787	            rngRequestTime != 0 &&
  1788	            rngWordCurrent == 0;
  1789	        // isDailyRetry: a genuine retry of the *daily* request. A daily request holds the lock
  1790	        // (rngLockedFlag set true by the first daily request); a mid-day lootbox request leaves
  1791	        // rngLockedFlag false. Distinguishing them stops an in-flight lootbox request from
  1792	        // making this fresh daily request look like a retry and skip the level increment below.
  1793	        bool isDailyRetry = isRetry && rngLockedFlag;
  1794	        if (!isRetry) {
  1795	            // Fresh request: advance lootbox index so new purchases target the NEXT RNG.
  1796	            _lrAdvanceIndexClearPending();
  1797	        }
  1798	        // Retry: index already advanced from the original request. No action needed —
  1799	        // lootboxRngIndex - 1 still points to the pending index regardless of request ID.
  1800	
  1801	        vrfRequestId = requestId;
  1802	        rngWordCurrent = 0;
  1803	        rngRequestTime = uint48(block.timestamp);
  1804	        rngLockedFlag = true;
  1805	
  1806	        // Close the FLIP purchase window at the final jackpot day's RNG request — the boundary where
  1807	        // new tickets begin routing to the next level (mirrors the route-to-level+1 step in the mint
  1808	        // module). jackpotCounter + step catches the final daily jackpot; the isTicketJackpotDay
  1809	        // (level-transition) request catches the single-day turbo jackpot, where jackpotPhaseFlag is
  1810	        // not yet set here.
  1811	        if (ticketRedemptionOpen && (jackpotPhaseFlag || isTicketJackpotDay)) {
  1812	            uint8 jpStep = 1;
  1813	            if (compressedJackpotFlag == 2 && jackpotCounter == 0) {
  1814	                jpStep = JACKPOT_LEVEL_CAP;
  1815	            } else if (
  1816	                compressedJackpotFlag == 1 &&
  1817	                jackpotCounter > 0 &&
  1818	                jackpotCounter < JACKPOT_LEVEL_CAP - 1
  1819	            ) {
  1820	                jpStep = 2;
  1821	            }
  1822	            if (jackpotCounter + jpStep >= JACKPOT_LEVEL_CAP) {
  1823	                ticketRedemptionOpen = false;
  1824	            }
  1825	        }
  1826	
  1827	        // Increment level at RNG request time when lastPurchaseDay = true.
  1828	        // lvl is already purchaseLevel (= level + 1), so set directly.
  1829	        // Only on a fresh daily request - a daily retry would double-increment, and an
  1830	        // in-flight mid-day lootbox request must not suppress this increment.
  1831	        if (isTicketJackpotDay && !isDailyRetry) {
  1832	            // Snapshot affiliate reward before level increment.
  1833	            // Scores routed to lvl (= level + 1) during the purchase phase just ended.
  1834	            _rewardTopAffiliate(lvl);
  1835	            level = lvl;
  1836	
  1837	            // Decimator window: open at x4/x99, close at x5/x00
  1838	            uint24 mod100 = lvl % 100;
  1839	            uint24 mod10 = lvl % 10;
  1840	            if ((mod10 == 4 && mod100 != 94) || mod100 == 99) {
  1841	                decWindowOpen = true;
  1842	            } else if (
  1843	                decWindowOpen && ((mod10 == 5 && mod100 != 95) || mod100 == 0)
  1844	            ) {
  1845	                decWindowOpen = false;
  1846	            }
  1847	
  1848	            // Resolve charity governance for the completed level.
  1849	            // lvl is the NEW level (old level + 1). CHARITY.currentLevel tracks
  1850	            // the CURRENT governance level (starts at 0, incremented by pickCharity).
  1851	            // The game's level 0->1 transition means level 0 gameplay is complete,
  1852	            // so we resolve governance for level 0 = lvl - 1.
  1853	            charityResolve.pickCharity(lvl - 1);
  1854	        }
  1855	    }
  1856	
  1857	    /// @notice Emergency VRF coordinator rotation (governance-gated).
  1858	    /// @dev Access: ContractAddresses.ADMIN only. The Admin contract enforces
  1859	    ///      stall duration via sDGNRS-holder governance (propose/vote/execute).
  1860	    /// @param newCoordinator New VRF coordinator address.
  1861	    /// @param newSubId New subscription ID.
  1862	    /// @param newKeyHash New key hash for the gas lane.
  1863	    function updateVrfCoordinatorAndSub(
  1864	        address newCoordinator,
  1865	        uint256 newSubId,
  1866	        bytes32 newKeyHash
  1867	    ) external {
  1868	        if (msg.sender != ContractAddresses.ADMIN) revert OnlyAdmin();
  1869	
  1870	        address current = address(vrfCoordinator);
  1871	        _setVrfConfig(newCoordinator, newSubId, newKeyHash);
  1872	
  1873	        // Detect what is in flight and re-issue on the new coordinator.
  1874	        // The request is accepted before the new subscription is LINK-funded; DegenerusAdmin
  1875	        // funds it in the same _executeSwap transaction (transferAndCall), and the VRF node
   620	        MintPaymentKind payKind,
   621	        uint256 costWei
   622	    );
   623	
   624	    /// @notice Emitted when a boon is consumed by a player.
   625	    event BoonConsumed(address indexed player, uint8 boonType, uint16 boostBps);
   626	
   627	    /// @notice Emitted when admin swaps game ETH for stETH.
   628	    event AdminSwapEthForStEth(address indexed recipient, uint256 amount);
   629	
   630	    /// @notice Emitted when admin stakes game ETH into Lido stETH.
   631	    event AdminStakeEthForStEth(uint256 amount);
   632	
   633	    /// @dev True when gameover liveness guard would fire within ~1 day (day-granularity).
   634	    ///      Used to activate distress-mode lootbox behaviour: 100% nextpool allocation
   635	    ///      and 25% ticket bonus on the distress-bought portion.
   636	    function _isDistressMode() internal view returns (bool) {
   637	        if (gameOver) return false;
   638	        uint24 psd = purchaseStartDay;
   639	        uint24 currentDay = _simulatedDayIndex();
   640	        if (level == 0) {
   641	            // Distress fires on the final day before the liveness guard would trigger.
   642	            return currentDay >= psd + _DEPLOY_IDLE_TIMEOUT_DAYS;
   643	        }
   644	        return currentDay >= psd + 120;
   645	    }
   646	
   647	    /// @dev Queues entries for a buyer at a target level. The `entries` arg is in
   648	    ///      entry units (price/4 each), NOT whole tickets — 4 entries per
   649	    ///      whole ticket. `owed` accumulates entries.
   650	    ///      If buyer has no existing entries at that level, adds them to the queue.
   651	    ///      Caps at uint32 max to prevent overflow.
   652	    /// @param buyer Address to receive entries.
   653	    /// @param targetLevel Level for which entries are queued.
   654	    /// @param entries Number of entries to queue (price/4 units).
   655	    function _queueEntries(
   656	        address buyer,
   657	        uint24 targetLevel,
   658	        uint32 entries,
   659	        bool rngBypass
   660	    ) internal {
   661	        if (entries == 0) return;
   662	        // No liveness gate here: tickets queued during the liveness-timeout window are harmless.
   663	        // They are never processed (the game-over drain ends the game without a further daily
   664	        // draw) or the resolving daily word has not been requested yet, so no terminal jackpot
   665	        // can be manipulated by them. Player purchase paths gate liveness at their own entry; the
   666	        // advance-chain daily-jackpot distribution also queues through this sink and must NOT be
   667	        // reverted here, so the gate stays off the shared sink.
   668	        emit EntriesQueued(buyer, targetLevel, entries);
   669	        bool isFarFuture = targetLevel > level + 5;
   670	        if (isFarFuture && rngLockedFlag && !rngBypass) revert RngLocked();
   671	        uint24 wk = isFarFuture
   672	            ? _tqFarFutureKey(targetLevel)
   673	            : _tqWriteKey(targetLevel);
   674	        uint40 packed = entriesOwedPacked[wk][buyer];
   675	        uint32 owed = uint32(packed >> 8);
  1460	        data = _setMintDay(
  1461	            data,
  1462	            day,
  1463	            BitPackingLib.DAY_SHIFT,
  1464	            BitPackingLib.MASK_32
  1465	        );
  1466	        // Front-load the LEVEL mint streak by the same freeze delta (survives pass expiry).
  1467	        data = _withPassStreakFrontLoad(
  1468	            data,
  1469	            ticketStartLevel,
  1470	            newFrozenLevel,
  1471	            levelsToAdd
  1472	        );
  1473	
  1474	        mintPacked_[player] = data;
  1475	    }
  1476	
  1477	    /// @dev Returns the current day index.
  1478	    function _simulatedDayIndex() internal view returns (uint24) {
  1479	        return GameTimeLib.currentDayIndex();
  1480	    }
  1481	
  1482	    /// @dev Whether the liveness-timeout game-over trigger is active.
  1483	    ///      Level 0: deploy idle timeout (365 days since purchaseStartDay).
  1484	    ///      Level 1+: 120-day inactivity timeout since purchaseStartDay.
  1485	    ///
  1486	    ///      Productive-phase pause: returns false while lastPurchaseDay or
  1487	    ///      jackpotPhaseFlag is set. The day clock would otherwise fire
  1488	    ///      inside the multi-call window between target-met and phase
  1489	    ///      transition close (when purchaseStartDay is finally updated to
  1490	    ///      the new level's start), but _handleGameOverPath is unreachable
  1491	    ///      in that window (gated by !inJackpot && !lastPurchase at
  1492	    ///      AdvanceModule:182), so a fire would deadlock _queueEntries calls
  1493	    ///      with no path to clear rngLockedFlag. phaseTransitionActive is
  1494	    ///      implied by jackpotPhaseFlag throughout — they clear together at
  1495	    ///      AdvanceModule:328-331.
  1496	    ///
  1497	    ///      Day math is evaluated first so mid-drain RNG requests (which set
  1498	    ///      rngRequestTime during _handleGameOverPath) cannot transiently flip
  1499	    ///      liveness back to false while the drain is in progress.
  1500	    ///
  1501	    ///      Stalled-advance bailout: if day math has not yet been met, ANY
  1502	    ///      condition that leaves rngRequestTime non-zero for _VRF_GRACE_PERIOD
  1503	    ///      fires liveness. This covers genuine VRF death (callback never lands)
  1504	    ///      and any bug that prevents the advance state machine from reaching
  1505	    ///      _unlockRng — gas exhaustion, unexpected reverts in drain/jackpot
  1506	    ///      paths, or any other failure mode that bricks the cycle after a
  1507	    ///      fulfilled callback. In all cases the historical-fallback path in
  1508	    ///      _gameOverEntropy engages, letting funds drain to players via
  1509	    ///      terminal jackpot rather than remaining trapped. Below that
  1510	    ///      threshold, liveness stays false — players can propose a coordinator
  1511	    ///      rotation via DegenerusAdmin, and missed days are credited back to
  1512	    ///      purchaseStartDay in AdvanceModule.rngGate on fulfillment.
  1513	    function _livenessTriggered() internal view returns (bool) {
  1514	        // Jackpot / last-purchase suppress the in-phase clocks (they would false-fire in the
  1515	        // productive window between target-met and phase-transition close), but the
  1516	        // phase-independent VRF-death deadman still fires here so a permanently-stalled game in
  1517	        // these phases reaches terminal fund release instead of bricking.
  1518	        if (lastPurchaseDay || jackpotPhaseFlag) return _vrfDeadmanFired();
  1519	        uint24 lvl = level;
  1520	        uint24 psd = purchaseStartDay;
  1521	        uint24 currentDay = _simulatedDayIndex();
  1522	        if (lvl == 0 && currentDay - psd > _DEPLOY_IDLE_TIMEOUT_DAYS) return true;
  1523	        if (lvl != 0 && currentDay - psd > 120) return true;
  1524	        uint48 rngStart = rngRequestTime;
  1525	        return rngStart != 0 && block.timestamp - rngStart >= _VRF_GRACE_PERIOD;
  1526	    }
  1527	
  1528	    /// @dev VRF-death deadman: true once no day has sealed for _VRF_DEADMAN_DAYS. dailyIdx
  1529	    ///      advances only in _unlockRng (a completed day), so currentDay - dailyIdx counts days
  1530	    ///      since real progress and freezes during any stall — and stays frozen until game-over
  1531	    ///      latches (the terminal _unlockRng runs after gameOver is set), so it never evaporates
  1532	    ///      mid-drain. Phase-independent, unlike _livenessTriggered: advanceGame consults it to
  1533	    ///      reach terminal fund release even while jackpotPhaseFlag / lastPurchaseDay are set.
  1534	    function _vrfDeadmanFired() internal view returns (bool) {
  1535	        return _simulatedDayIndex() - dailyIdx > _VRF_DEADMAN_DAYS;
  1536	    }
  1537	
  1538	    /// @dev Returns the day index for a specific timestamp.
  1539	    function _simulatedDayIndexAt(uint48 ts) internal pure returns (uint24) {
  1540	        return GameTimeLib.currentDayIndexAt(ts);
  1541	    }
  1542	
  1543	    /// @dev Gets the current mint day from dailyIdx or calculates from timestamp.
  1544	    function _currentMintDay() internal view returns (uint24) {
  1545	        uint24 day = dailyIdx;
  1546	        if (day == 0) {
  1547	            day = _simulatedDayIndex();
  1548	        }
  1549	        return day;
  1550	    }
  1551	
  1552	    /// @dev Updates the day field in packed mint data if changed.
  1553	    function _setMintDay(
  1554	        uint256 data,
  1555	        uint24 day,
    55	        20 ether;
    56	
    57	    // error E() — inherited from DegenerusGameStorage
    58	
    59	    /// @notice Process game over by distributing remaining funds via jackpots.
    60	    /// @dev Called when liveness guards trigger (1yr deploy timeout or 120-day inactivity).
    61	    ///      Sets terminal gameOver flag.
    62	    ///
    63	    ///      Distribution logic:
    64	    ///      - If game ended early (levels 0-9): Fixed 20 ETH refund per deity pass purchased,
    65	    ///        FIFO by purchase order, budget-capped to available funds minus claimablePool
    66	    ///      - Remaining funds: 10% to Decimator, 90% to next-level ticketholders
    67	    ///      - Decimator refunds flow to terminal jackpot pool
    68	    ///      - Any uncredited remainder swept to vault and sDGNRS
    69	    ///
    70	    ///      Reads rngWordByDay[day] for entropy; reverts if funds exist but word is not yet available.
    71	    ///      VRF fallback logic (historical word, stall timeout) is in AdvanceModule._gameOverEntropy.
    72	    /// @param day Day index for RNG word lookup from rngWordByDay mapping.
    73	    /// @custom:reverts Invariant When distributable funds exist but the RNG word is unavailable (defense-in-depth).
    74	    /// @custom:reverts TransferFailed When an stETH or ETH transfer fails.
    75	    function handleGameOverDrain(uint24 day) external {
    76	        if (_goRead(GO_JACKPOT_PAID_SHIFT, GO_JACKPOT_PAID_MASK) != 0) return; // Already processed
    77	
    78	        uint24 lvl = level;
    79	
    80	        uint256 totalFunds = address(this).balance + steth.balanceOf(address(this));
    81	
    82	        // Compute available funds FIRST (before any side effects)
    83	        // Deity pass refunds have not happened yet, so claimablePool is pre-refund.
    84	        // sDGNRS redemption ETH is segregated out of the game at submit (pullRedemptionReserve
    85	        // transfers it to the sDGNRS contract), so it is no longer part of totalFunds here —
    86	        // subtracting pendingRedemptionEthValue would double-count it.
    87	        uint256 reserved = uint256(claimablePool);
    88	        uint256 preRefundAvailable = totalFunds > reserved ? totalFunds - reserved : 0;
    89	
    90	        // RNG gate: when distributable funds exist, require RNG word.
    91	        // Defense-in-depth -- caller (_handleGameOverPath) already guarantees
    92	        // rngWordByDay[day] != 0 before calling, so this revert should never fire.
    93	        uint256 rngWord;
    94	        if (preRefundAvailable != 0) {
    95	            rngWord = rngWordByDay[day];
    96	            if (rngWord == 0) revert Invariant();
    97	        }
    98	
    99	        // === All side effects below this line (RNG confirmed or no funds to distribute) ===
   100	
   101	        // Deity pass refunds (levels 0-9): refund each owner what they paid, capped at the flat
   102	        // DEITY_PASS_EARLY_GAMEOVER_REFUND so a boon-discounted deity (paid < 20 ETH) never refunds
   103	        // more than it paid, then clamped to the remaining distributable budget (FIFO).
   104	        if (lvl < 10) {
   105	            uint256 ownerCount = deityPassOwners.length;
   106	            uint256 budget = preRefundAvailable;
   107	            uint256 totalRefunded;
   108	            for (uint256 i; i < ownerCount; ) {
   109	                address owner = deityPassOwners[i];
   110	                uint256 refund = deityPassPricePaid[owner];
   111	                if (refund != 0) {
   112	                    if (refund > DEITY_PASS_EARLY_GAMEOVER_REFUND) {
   113	                        refund = DEITY_PASS_EARLY_GAMEOVER_REFUND;
   114	                    }
   115	                    if (refund > budget) {
   116	                        refund = budget;
   117	                    }
   118	                    if (refund != 0) {
   119	                        _creditClaimable(owner, refund);
   120	                        unchecked {
   121	                            totalRefunded += refund;
   122	                            budget -= refund;
   123	                        }
   124	                    }
   125	                    if (budget == 0) break;
   126	                }
   127	                unchecked {
   128	                    ++i;
   129	                }
   130	            }
   131	            if (totalRefunded != 0) {
   132	                claimablePool += uint128(totalRefunded); // Safe: totalRefunded bounded by preRefundAvailable which fits uint128
   133	            }
   134	        }
   135	
   136	        // Latch terminal state
   137	        gameOver = true;
   138	        _goWrite(GO_TIME_SHIFT, GO_TIME_MASK, uint48(block.timestamp));
   139	
   140	        // Burn unallocated tokens
   141	        charityGameOver.burnAtGameOver();
   142	        dgnrs.burnAtGameOver();
   143	        // Flood FLIP's VAULT mint allowance as a one-shot worthless-token tombstone
   144	        flip.tombstoneAtGameOver();
   145	
   146	        _goWrite(GO_JACKPOT_PAID_SHIFT, GO_JACKPOT_PAID_MASK, 1);
   147	        _setNextPrizePool(0);
   148	        _setFuturePrizePool(0);
   149	        _setCurrentPrizePool(0);
   150	        yieldAccumulator = 0;
   151	        // Terminal state also clears the freeze: with the live pools drained, _unlockRng's
   152	        // _unfreezePool must not resurrect the pending pool back into them, and no
   153	        // post-gameOver box/Degenerette resolution may draw ETH from a phantom pending pool.
   154	        prizePoolPendingPacked = 0;
   155	        prizePoolFrozen = false;
   156	        // All three pools are drained to zero at game over. Emit the terminal snapshot here —
   157	        // before the available==0 early return below — so every game-over path logs it once. The
   158	        // daily snapshot in _unlockRng skips game-over (gameOver is set above) to avoid a duplicate.
   159	        emit PrizePoolDailySnapshot(
   160	            0,
   161	            0,
   162	            0,
   163	            claimablePool,
   164	            address(this).balance + steth.balanceOf(address(this)),
   165	            yieldAccumulator
   166	        );
   167	
   168	        // Recalculate available after refunds (claimablePool may have grown).
   169	        // sDGNRS redemption ETH was already segregated out of the game at submit, so it is not
   170	        // part of totalFunds here — only claimablePool is reserved.
   900	
   901	    /// @notice Explicit-ethValue ticket-buy entry: like `purchase`, but the fresh-ETH portion
   902	    ///         is the `ethValue` parameter rather than `msg.value`. Sole caller: the facade's
   903	    ///         foil purchase, which funds the ticket/lootbox leg with the fresh ETH it carved
   904	    ///         while the buyer's msg.value is in flight (that msg.value is ignored here — only
   905	    ///         ethValue is spent). payable so the carried msg.value does not revert the
   906	    ///         delegatecall.
   907	    function purchaseWith(
   908	        address buyer,
   909	        uint256 entryQuantityScaled,
   910	        uint256 lootBoxAmount,
   911	        bytes32 affiliateCode,
   912	        MintPaymentKind payKind,
   913	        uint256 ethValue
   914	    ) external payable {
   915	        _purchaseForWith(
   916	            buyer,
   917	            entryQuantityScaled,
   918	            lootBoxAmount,
   919	            affiliateCode,
   920	            payKind,
   921	            ethValue
   922	        );
   923	    }
   924	
   925	    /// @notice Redeem FLIP for current-jackpot tickets — allowed only inside the jackpot window.
   926	    /// @dev Reverts unless the FLIP purchase window is open: the prize target is met in the purchase
   927	    ///      phase, or the jackpot phase is live, with no RNG in flight. Outside that window FLIP ticket
   928	    ///      purchases revert so bonus tickets and prize ETH accrue to real-ETH buyers.
   929	    /// @param buyer Recipient of the purchased tickets.
   930	    /// @param entryQuantityScaled Number of tickets to purchase (2 decimals, scaled by 100).
   931	    function redeemFlip(
   932	        address buyer,
   933	        uint256 entryQuantityScaled
   934	    ) external {
   935	        _redeemFlipFor(buyer, entryQuantityScaled);
   936	    }
   937	
   938	    function _redeemFlipFor(
   939	        address buyer,
   940	        uint256 entryQuantityScaled
   941	    ) private {
   942	        if (_livenessTriggered()) revert E();
   943	
   944	        if (entryQuantityScaled != 0) {
   945	            // FLIP purchase window: opens the first time a redeem lands once the prize target is met
   946	            // in the purchase phase with no RNG in flight, latching a single warm slot-0 bit. It stays
   947	            // open through the jackpot days and is cleared in the advance at the final jackpot day's RNG
   948	            // request — the boundary where new tickets route to the next level (rngLockedFlag stays set
   949	            // from that request until _unlockRng, so it can never flip back on during the wind-down).
   950	            // While it is closed (an open/stalled purchase phase) FLIP purchases revert, so bonus
   951	            // tickets and prize ETH accrue to real-ETH buyers. The target-met condition holds for the
   952	            // whole of lastPurchaseDay, so even a one-day purchase phase still offers that day as a
   953	            // redemption window.
   954	            if (!ticketRedemptionOpen) {
   955	                if (
   956	                    rngLockedFlag ||
   957	                    _getNextPrizePool() < levelPrizePool[level]
   958	                ) revert E();
   959	                ticketRedemptionOpen = true;
   960	            }
   961	
   962	            uint24 cachedLevel = level;
   963	            (
   964	                ,
   965	                uint32 adjustedQty32,
   966	                uint24 targetLevel,
   967	                uint32 flipMintUnits,
   968	                ,
   969	                ,
   970	                ,
   971	                ,
   972	            ) = _callTicketPurchase(
   973	                    buyer,
   974	                    entryQuantityScaled,
   975	                    MintPaymentKind.DirectEth,
  1335	            entryQuantityScaled,
  1336	            lootBoxAmount,
  1337	            affiliateCode,
  1338	            payKind,
  1339	            ethValue,
  1340	            cachedJpFlag,
  1341	            cachedLevel,
  1342	            priceWei,
  1343	            ticketCost
  1344	        );
  1345	    }
  1346	
  1347	    /// @dev Core purchase body. `cachedJpFlag`/`cachedLevel`/`priceWei`/`ticketCost` are the
  1348	    ///      caller's same-frame _purchaseCostInputs snapshot (no external call sits between
  1349	    ///      that read and this frame, so the values cannot have changed).
  1350	    function _purchaseForWithCached(
  1351	        address buyer,
  1352	        uint256 entryQuantityScaled,
  1353	        uint256 lootBoxAmount,
  1354	        bytes32 affiliateCode,
  1355	        MintPaymentKind payKind,
  1356	        uint256 ethValue,
  1357	        bool cachedJpFlag,
  1358	        uint24 cachedLevel,
  1359	        uint256 priceWei,
  1360	        uint256 ticketCost
  1361	    ) private {
  1362	        if (_livenessTriggered()) revert E();
  1363	        uint256 lootboxFlipCredit;
  1364	
  1365	        if (lootBoxAmount != 0 && lootBoxAmount < LOOTBOX_MIN) revert E();
  1366	
  1367	        uint256 totalCost = ticketCost + lootBoxAmount;

  1227	    /// @dev Daily RNG processing gate called during advanceGame. Applies VRF word,
  1228	    ///      processes coinflip payouts, rolls daily quest, resolves pending gambling
  1229	    ///      burn redemptions, stores lootbox RNG, and handles VRF timeout retries (12h).
  1230	    function rngGate(
  1231	        uint48 ts,
  1232	        uint24 day,
  1233	        uint24 lvl,
  1234	        bool isTicketJackpotDay,
  1235	        uint8 coinflipBonus
  1236	    ) internal returns (uint256 word, uint32 gapDays) {
  1237	        // Already recorded for today
  1238	        uint256 recordedWord = rngWordByDay[day];
  1239	        if (recordedWord != 0) return (recordedWord, 0);
  1240	
  1241	        uint256 currentWord = rngWordCurrent;
  1242	
  1243	        // Have a fresh VRF word ready
  1244	        if (currentWord != 0 && rngRequestTime != 0) {
  1245	            // Backfill gap days from VRF stall before processing current day.
  1246	            // Gated on rngWordByDay[idx + 1] == 0 so the backfill runs at
  1247	            // most once per lock window: dailyIdx is only updated by
  1248	            // _unlockRng, so a multi-day drain would otherwise re-enter
  1249	            // this branch on each new wall-clock day and re-process the
  1250	            // same gap range, doubling purchaseStartDay and re-running
  1251	            // coinflip payouts for already-resolved days.
  1252	            uint24 idx = dailyIdx;
  1253	            if (day > idx + 1 && rngWordByDay[idx + 1] == 0) {
  1254	                uint24 gapCount = day - idx - 1;
  1255	                _backfillGapDays(currentWord, idx + 1, day);
  1256	
  1257	                // Backfill any lootbox indices that never got a VRF word (orphaned by stall).
  1258	                // Uses fresh VRF entropy, not predictable on-chain state.
  1259	                _backfillOrphanedLootboxIndices(currentWord);
  1260	
  1261	                // Extend death clock by the stall duration -- gap days don't count toward
  1262	                // the 120-day inactivity timeout since the game was stalled, not abandoned.
  1263	                purchaseStartDay += gapCount;
  1264	                gapDays = gapCount;
  1265	            }
  1266	
  1267	            // Normal daily RNG processing (request from current day)
  1268	            currentWord = _applyDailyRng(day, currentWord);
  1269	            coinflip.processCoinflipPayouts(coinflipBonus, currentWord, day);
  1270	            // Force the MINT_FLIP daily on the first jackpot day (lastPurchaseDay still set here,
  1271	            // jackpot not yet entered) so the FLIP-mint quest only lands when the redeem window is
  1272	            // live. Turbo (compressedJackpotFlag == 2) is skipped — its jackpot collapses at this
  1273	            // request, leaving no full open day for that quest.
  1274	            // Force the buy-a-foil-pack daily on the first purchase day of a level:
  1275	            // phaseTransitionActive is set at level end (_endPhase) and cleared only once
  1276	            // the transition completes (line 440), and this roll runs before that completion
  1277	            // in the same advance — so it is true exactly on the day the new purchase phase
  1278	            // opens. Gated on gapDays == 0 so a VRF-stall backfill (which defers the whole
  1279	            // transition to the next advance, line 412) does not roll the foil quest early.
  1280	            // Mutually exclusive with the first-jackpot-day MINT_FLIP force (opposite cycle ends).
  1281	            quests.rollDailyQuest(
  1282	                day,
  1283	                currentWord,
  1284	                lastPurchaseDay && compressedJackpotFlag != 2,
  1285	                phaseTransitionActive && gapDays == 0
  1286	            );
  1287	
  1288	            // Resolve the sentinel-stamped gambling-burn pool if any. Reading the
  1289	            // sentinel rather than deriving `day - 1` makes multi-day RNG stalls correct by
  1290	            // construction: the sentinel always names the (at most one) unresolved day, so a
  1291	            // single resolve call after the stall recovers covers the stuck pool exactly.
  1292	            {
  1293	                IsDGNRS sdgnrs = IsDGNRS(
  1294	                    ContractAddresses.SDGNRS
  1295	                );

  1297	                if (toResolve != 0) {
  1298	                    uint16 redemptionRoll = uint16(
  1299	                        ((currentWord >> 8) % 151) + 25
  1300	                    );
  1301	                    sdgnrs.resolveRedemptionPeriod(redemptionRoll, toResolve);
  1302	                }
  1303	            }
  1304	
  1305	            _finalizeLootboxRng(currentWord);
  1306	            return (currentWord, gapDays);
  1307	        }
  1308	
  1309	        // Waiting for VRF - check for timeout retry. A daily request (rngLockedFlag) gets the
  1310	        // 12h VRF retry; a lootbox-only mid-day request (rngLockedFlag == false) that bled past
  1311	        // the day boundary is abandoned after MIDDAY_RNG_STALL_TIMEOUT and promoted to this day's
  1312	        // daily request — _requestRng's isRetry path keeps the reserved index, so the fresh daily
  1313	        // word finalizes that bucket just as the mid-day word would have.
  1314	        if (rngRequestTime != 0) {
  1315	            uint48 elapsed = ts - rngRequestTime;
  1316	            uint48 timeout = rngLockedFlag ? 12 hours : MIDDAY_RNG_STALL_TIMEOUT;
  1317	            if (elapsed >= timeout) {
  1318	                _requestRng(isTicketJackpotDay, lvl);
  1319	                return (1, 0);
  1320	            }
  1321	            revert RngNotReady();
  1322	        }
  1323	
  1324	        // Need fresh RNG
  1325	        _requestRng(isTicketJackpotDay, lvl);
  1326	        return (1, 0);
  1327	    }

     4	import {IDegenerusGame, MintPaymentKind} from "../interfaces/IDegenerusGame.sol";

     8	/// @dev Minimal stETH interface (ERC20 subset)
     9	interface IStETH {
    10	    /// @param account Address to query balance of.
    11	    function balanceOf(address account) external view returns (uint256);
    12	    /// @param to Recipient address.
    13	    /// @param amount Transfer amount in wei.
    14	    function transfer(address to, uint256 amount) external returns (bool);
    15	}
    16	
    17	/// @dev Admin interface for VRF shutdown during final sweep.
    18	interface IDegenerusAdminShutdown {
    19	    function shutdownVrf() external;
    20	}
    21	
    22	/// @dev GNRUS interface for gameover GNRUS cleanup.
    23	interface IGNRUSGameOver {
    24	    function burnAtGameOver() external;
    25	    function onFinalSweep() external;
    26	}
    27	
    28	interface IFlipTombstone {
    29	    function tombstoneAtGameOver() external;
    30	}
    31	
    32	/**
    33	 * @title DegenerusGameGameOverModule
    34	 * @notice Handles game over logic including jackpot distribution and final sweeps.
    35	 * @dev Executed via delegatecall from DegenerusGame. Inherits storage layout.
    36	 */
    37	contract DegenerusGameGameOverModule is DegenerusGameStorage {
    38	    /// @notice stETH token contract for liquid staking rewards
    39	    IStETH private constant steth = IStETH(ContractAddresses.STETH_TOKEN);
    40	
    41	    /// @notice Admin contract for VRF shutdown
    42	    IDegenerusAdminShutdown private constant admin =
    43	        IDegenerusAdminShutdown(ContractAddresses.ADMIN);
    44	
    45	    /// @notice GNRUS contract for gameover cleanup
    46	    IGNRUSGameOver private constant charityGameOver =
    47	        IGNRUSGameOver(ContractAddresses.GNRUS);
    48	
    49	    /// @notice FLIP coin contract for the gameover worthless-token tombstone flood
    50	    IFlipTombstone private constant flip =
    51	        IFlipTombstone(ContractAddresses.COIN);
    52	
    53	    /// @notice Fixed refund amount per deity pass for early game over (levels 0-9)
    54	    uint256 private constant DEITY_PASS_EARLY_GAMEOVER_REFUND =
    55	        20 ether;
    56	
    57	    // error E() — inherited from DegenerusGameStorage
    58	
    59	    /// @notice Process game over by distributing remaining funds via jackpots.
    60	    /// @dev Called when liveness guards trigger (1yr deploy timeout or 120-day inactivity).
    61	    ///      Sets terminal gameOver flag.
    62	    ///
    63	    ///      Distribution logic:
    64	    ///      - If game ended early (levels 0-9): Fixed 20 ETH refund per deity pass purchased,
    65	    ///        FIFO by purchase order, budget-capped to available funds minus claimablePool
    66	    ///      - Remaining funds: 10% to Decimator, 90% to next-level ticketholders
    67	    ///      - Decimator refunds flow to terminal jackpot pool
    68	    ///      - Any uncredited remainder swept to vault and sDGNRS
    69	    ///
    70	    ///      Reads rngWordByDay[day] for entropy; reverts if funds exist but word is not yet available.
    71	    ///      VRF fallback logic (historical word, stall timeout) is in AdvanceModule._gameOverEntropy.
    72	    /// @param day Day index for RNG word lookup from rngWordByDay mapping.
    73	    /// @custom:reverts Invariant When distributable funds exist but the RNG word is unavailable (defense-in-depth).
    74	    /// @custom:reverts TransferFailed When an stETH or ETH transfer fails.
    75	    function handleGameOverDrain(uint24 day) external {
    76	        if (_goRead(GO_JACKPOT_PAID_SHIFT, GO_JACKPOT_PAID_MASK) != 0) return; // Already processed
    77	
    78	        uint24 lvl = level;
    79	
    80	        uint256 totalFunds = address(this).balance + steth.balanceOf(address(this));
    81	
    82	        // Compute available funds FIRST (before any side effects)
    83	        // Deity pass refunds have not happened yet, so claimablePool is pre-refund.
    84	        // sDGNRS redemption ETH is segregated out of the game at submit (pullRedemptionReserve
    85	        // transfers it to the sDGNRS contract), so it is no longer part of totalFunds here —
28- *      - Pull pattern for ETH/stETH withdrawals (claimWinnings)
29- *      - RNG lock prevents state manipulation during VRF callback window
30- *      - Access control via msg.sender checks
31- *      - Delegatecall modules use constant addresses from ContractAddresses
32: *      - 12h VRF timeout, 14-day gameover-RNG fallback, 120-day inactivity guard
33- */

240-      |  The heart of the game. This function progresses the state machine                     |

242-      |  Each call performs one "tick" of work. gameOver is terminal.                          |
243-      |                                                                                        |
244-      |  State Transitions:                                                                    |
245-      |  • PURCHASE (jackpotPhaseFlag=false): Process ticket batches until target met, then → JACKPOT|
246-      |  • JACKPOT (jackpotPhaseFlag=true): Pay daily jackpots, wait for burns, then → PURCHASE      |
247:      |  • GAMEOVER (gameOver=true): Terminal state, no transitions                             |
248-      |                                                                                        |
249-      |  Gating (tiered bypass):                                                                |
250-      |  • Deity pass holder — always bypasses                                                 |
251-      |  • Anyone — bypasses after 30+ min since level start                                   |

codex
Commit-pinned trace for `a79a9809`:

| Call | Relevant state/result |
|---|---|
| VRF returns on wall day 110 | With `dailyIdx=101`, `rngGate` computes `gapCount=8`, writes words for 102–109, writes day 110 separately, and updates storage `purchaseStartDay` from 100 to 108. The outer `psd += gapDays` is local only; the persistent credit is at AdvanceModule L1263. |
| Backfill call exits | `STAGE_GAP_BACKFILLED` exits before `_unlockRng`, leaving `dailyIdx=101` and `rngLockedFlag=true`. |
| Next advance | The cache guard clamps wall day 110 to 102 because `rngWordByDay[102]` exists; the lock skips L203, `rngGate` returns the recorded word, and—assuming empty queues and an unmet target—L551 calls `_unlockRng(102)`, setting `dailyIdx=102` and clearing the lock without changing `purchaseStartDay`. |
| Critical advance | The guard now clamps to 103 because word 103 was backfilled. With `purchaseStartDay=108`, purchase phase active, `lastPurchaseDay=false`, and `rngLockedFlag=false`, L203 evaluates checked `uint24` subtraction `103 - 108` and panics. |

The game-over branch is after L203 (L216), so it cannot provide an escape; GameOverModule is only reached through that later branch, while MintModule contains no writer that can repair these fields. L547 is not needed for the concrete unmet-target sequence; if the target were met while resolving day 102, its `day - psd` check would instead panic one call earlier.

Why: The 8-day backfill advances storage `purchaseStartDay` to 108 while replay sealing advances `dailyIdx` only one day per successful call. The next replayed day is therefore below `purchaseStartDay`, and the revert preserves the same clamped state on every retry.  
VERDICT: REAL

Commit-pinned trace for `a79a9809`:

| Call | Relevant state/result |
|---|---|
| VRF returns on wall day 110 | With `dailyIdx=101`, `rngGate` computes `gapCount=8`, writes words for 102–109, writes day 110 separately, and updates storage `purchaseStartDay` from 100 to 108. The outer `psd += gapDays` is local only; the persistent credit is at AdvanceModule L1263. |
| Backfill call exits | `STAGE_GAP_BACKFILLED` exits before `_unlockRng`, leaving `dailyIdx=101` and `rngLockedFlag=true`. |
| Next advance | The cache guard clamps wall day 110 to 102 because `rngWordByDay[102]` exists; the lock skips L203, `rngGate` returns the recorded word, and—assuming empty queues and an unmet target—L551 calls `_unlockRng(102)`, setting `dailyIdx=102` and clearing the lock without changing `purchaseStartDay`. |
| Critical advance | The guard now clamps to 103 because word 103 was backfilled. With `purchaseStartDay=108`, purchase phase active, `lastPurchaseDay=false`, and `rngLockedFlag=false`, L203 evaluates checked `uint24` subtraction `103 - 108` and panics. |

The game-over branch is after L203 (L216), so it cannot provide an escape; GameOverModule is only reached through that later branch, while MintModule contains no writer that can repair these fields. L547 is not needed for the concrete unmet-target sequence; if the target were met while resolving day 102, its `day - psd` check would instead panic one call earlier.

Why: The 8-day backfill advances storage `purchaseStartDay` to 108 while replay sealing advances `dailyIdx` only one day per successful call. The next replayed day is therefore below `purchaseStartDay`, and the revert preserves the same clamped state on every retry.

Authorized correctness verification of our OWN Solidity ahead of a public review contest. Neutral register. Review committed source at git ref a79a9809 ONLY (git show a79a9809:contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol; cross-ref MintModule, GameOverModule, JackpotModule, storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol). Do NOT trust the working tree.

Adversarially VERIFY OR REFUTE (bias to refute if unreachable/structurally protected):

C4 — Game-over ticket drain keys the queue at `lvl+1` unconditionally (L731 processTicketBatch, L746 in _handleGameOverPath/_gameOverEntropy drain block), but on the VRF-death DEADMAN entry the pending tickets sit at key `lvl`. Normal advance derives the key conditionally (L213 purchaseLevel=(lastPurchase&&locked)?lvl:lvl+1; L502 _runProcessTicketBatch(inJackpot?lvl:purchaseLevel)) because level pre-increments at the last-purchase daily RNG request (_finalizeRngRequest ~L1835 level=lvl) and jackpot-phase buys queue at cachedLevel=lvl (MintModule ~L1967). So on a locked last-purchase day / jackpot phase, queued tickets are at key lvl. If the daily RNG request was sent on the last purchase day (_swapAndFreeze L437, ticketsFullyProcessed=false, tickets in read slot at key lvl) then VRF never fulfills and the 120d deadman opens game-over via L216/686: the _gameOverEntropy drain does processTicketBatch(lvl+1) (empty -> finished), _swapTicketSlot with a NON-EMPTY read slot, latches ticketsFullyProcessed=true while both slots hold undrained lvl entries -> the largest final-day cohort is EXCLUDED from handleGameOverDrain terminal distribution (contradicts L712-714 "every purchased ticket eligible"). This is EXCLUSION, not the already-refuted post-gameover ticket INSERTION.

Is the drain key really unconditional lvl+1, do tickets really sit at key lvl on the deadman path, and are they genuinely excluded (not picked up by a later leg or GameOverModule runTerminalJackpot(lvl+1))? End with one line: VERDICT: REAL or VERDICT: REFUTED, plus 2-sentence why.

---
name: contract-auditor
description: Adversarial security auditor and gas optimization specialist for Degenerus Protocol contracts. Use when the user requests audits, gas-limit checks (especially advanceGame(0)), RNG/VRF exploit analysis, MEV/griefing scenarios, or economic attack reviews of Degenerus Solidity code.
---

# Contract Auditor

## Overview
Perform adversarial security review and gas optimization analysis of Degenerus contracts, focusing on RNG safety, gas limits, and economic exploits.

## Workflow

1. Scope and priorities
   - Ask for target contracts/modules, known concerns, and whether to focus on gas, RNG, or full audit.
   - Confirm if `advanceGame(0)` gas analysis should be first.

2. Map the surface
   - Identify external entry points, fund flows, RNG consumption points, and external calls.
   - Note delegatecall modules and trust boundaries.

3. Deep dive by mandate
   - Gas: trace `advanceGame(0)` and all loops; estimate worst-case bounds.
   - RNG: model request/fulfill windows; test for front-run, selective execution, and input grinding.
   - Economics: check for flash-loan, griefing, or game-theoretic exploits.

4. Report findings
   - Use the standard finding format from `references/output-format.md`.
   - Order findings by severity; provide PoCs or proof of impossibility where applicable.

## Response Rules

- Think like a 1000 ETH attacker; prioritize exploitability.
- Assume users are adversarial; external contracts are untrusted.
- Prefer code and tests for proof over opinion.
- If a doc conflicts with code, defer to code and flag mismatch.

## References

- `references/audit-brief.md` for mandates, attack scenarios, and methodology.
- `references/output-format.md` for required reporting format.

--- END? ---

# Degenerus Contract Auditor Brief

## Mindset
- Adversarial: 1000 ETH budget, goal is to exploit, grief, or break the protocol.
- Focus: gas limits, RNG exploitation, economic attacks, MEV/griefing.

## Primary Mandates

### 1) Gas Limit: `advanceGame(0)` must remain < 15M gas
- Trace all code paths from `advanceGame(0)`.
- Identify loops and maximum bounds.
- Consider state accumulation across many levels.
- Flag unbounded or user-controlled iterations.

### 2) RNG Security: zero exploitability on money-critical outcomes
Audit RNG use in:
- Jackpot winner selection
- Coinflip resolution
- Decimator outcomes
- Lootbox prizes
- Degenerette outcomes
- Trait extermination tiebreakers (if any)

Attack vectors:
- VRF result seen in mempool before use
- Selective accept/reject of RNG result
- Input grinding that combines with VRF
- Block builder collusion
- Timing around rngLockedFlag
- Index-based protection bypass

Key question: If I could see random value 1 block early, what could I do?

### 3) Gas Efficiency
- Cache repeated SLOADs
- Avoid storage writes in loops
- Tighten loop bounds
- Minimize event data and external calls
- Use calldata for read-only data where possible

### 4) Security Best Practices
- External contracts (Chainlink, stETH) are untrusted
- Watch for: access control bypass, unchecked blocks, DoS via revert, timestamp manipulation, delegatecall to untrusted target

## Attack Scenarios to Explore

### Economic
- Flash loan amplification
- Griefing (spend 10 ETH to deny 100 ETH)
- MEV (sandwich/backrun)
- Game-theoretic manipulation (traits, activity score)

### RNG
- Timing between request/fulfill/use
- Entropy manipulation via inputs
- Selective execution or callback DoS

### Gas
- State bloat across many levels/players
- Callback bombs on external calls
- Loop exploitation and worst-case iterations

## Audit Methodology

1) Architecture review: entry points, fund flows, RNG uses, state transitions, external calls
2) Gas analysis: measure `advanceGame(0)` worst-case paths
3) RNG security: model windows and mitigations
4) Adversarial testing: construct attacks and verify failure
5) Reporting: severity, repro steps, fixes, gas savings

--- OUTPUT FORMAT ---
# Reporting Format

## Finding Template

## [SEVERITY] Title

**Location:** `Contract.sol:function():L123-145`

**Description:**
What the issue is and why it matters.

**Attack Scenario:**
Step-by-step how an attacker would exploit this.

**Impact:**
What value is at risk or what denial of service is possible.

**Proof of Concept:**
Code or trace demonstrating the issue.

**Recommendation:**
How to fix it, with code if applicable.

**Gas Impact:** (if relevant)
Current: X gas | Proposed: Y gas | Savings: Z gas

## Gas Optimization Template

## [GAS] Title

**Location:** `Contract.sol:function():L123`

**Current Implementation:**
```solidity
// current code
```

**Proposed Implementation:**
```solidity
// optimized code
```

**Savings:** X gas per call
**Rationale:** Why this is safe and correct.

    24	/// @dev GNRUS interface for level-transition governance resolution.
