  1930	    /// @notice Chainlink VRF callback for random word fulfillment.
  1931	    /// @dev Access: VRF coordinator only.
  1932	    ///      Daily RNG: stores word for advanceGame processing (nudges applied there).
  1933	    ///      Mid-day RNG: directly finalizes lootbox RNG, no advanceGame needed.
  1934	    ///      SECURITY: Validates requestId and coordinator address.
  1935	    /// @param requestId The request ID to match.
  1936	    /// @param randomWords Array containing the random word (length 1).
  1937	    function rawFulfillRandomWords(
  1938	        uint256 requestId,
  1939	        uint256[] calldata randomWords
  1940	    ) external {
  1941	        if (msg.sender != address(vrfCoordinator)) revert OnlyCoordinator();
  1942	        if (requestId != vrfRequestId || rngWordCurrent != 0) return;
  1943	
  1944	        uint256 word = randomWords[0];
  1945	        if (word == 0) word = 1;
  1946	
  1947	        if (rngLockedFlag) {
  1948	            // Daily RNG: store for advanceGame processing (nudges applied there)
  1949	            rngWordCurrent = word;
  1950	        } else {
  1951	            // Mid-day RNG: directly finalize lootbox and clear state
  1952	            uint48 index = uint48(_lrRead(LR_INDEX_SHIFT, LR_INDEX_MASK)) - 1;
  1953	            lootboxRngWordByIndex[index] = word;
  1954	            emit LootboxRngApplied(index, word, requestId);
  1955	            vrfRequestId = 0;
  1956	            rngRequestTime = 0;
  1957	        }
  1958	    }
  1959	
  1960	    /// @dev Backfill rngWordByDay and process coinflip payouts for gap days
  1961	    ///      caused by VRF stall. Derives deterministic words from the first
  1962	    ///      post-gap VRF word via keccak256(vrfWord, gapDay).
  1963	    ///      NOTE: Gap days get zero nudges (totalFlipReversals not consumed).
  1964	    ///      NOTE: resolveRedemptionPeriod is NOT called for backfilled gap days —
  1965	    ///      the redemption timer continued ticking in real time during the stall;
  1966	    ///      it resolves only on the current day via the normal rngGate path.
  1967	    /// @param vrfWord The first post-gap VRF random word.
  1968	    /// @param startDay First gap day (dailyIdx + 1).
  1969	    /// @param endDay Current day (exclusive — not backfilled, handled by normal path).
  1970	    function _backfillGapDays(
  1971	        uint256 vrfWord,
  1972	        uint24 startDay,
  1973	        uint24 endDay
  1974	    ) private {
  1975	        // Cap at 120 gap days to stay within block gas limit (~9M gas).
  1976	        // Backfills oldest days first (most likely to have active coinflips).
  1977	        if (endDay - startDay > 120) endDay = startDay + 120;
  1978	        for (uint24 gapDay = startDay; gapDay < endDay; ) {
  1979	            uint256 derivedWord = uint256(
  1980	                keccak256(abi.encodePacked(vrfWord, gapDay))
  1981	            );
  1982	            if (derivedWord == 0) derivedWord = 1;
  1983	            rngWordByDay[gapDay] = derivedWord;
  1984	            // Gap days are calendar days that elapsed during the stall (no advance ran on
  1985	            // them), so none is a level-0 or first-jackpot day — always non-bonus (0).
  1986	            coinflip.processCoinflipPayouts(0, derivedWord, gapDay);
  1987	            emit DailyRngApplied(gapDay, derivedWord, 0, derivedWord);
  1988	            unchecked {
  1989	                ++gapDay;
  1990	            }
  1991	        }
  1992	    }
  1993	
  1994	    /// @dev Backfill any lootbox RNG indices that never received a VRF word.
  1995	    ///      Scans backwards from lootboxRngIndex - 1 until hitting a filled index.

  1336	    /// @dev Game-over RNG gate with fallback for stalled VRF.
  1337	    ///      After the 14-day GAMEOVER_RNG_FALLBACK_DELAY, uses earliest historical VRF word as
  1338	    ///      fallback (more secure than blockhash since it's already verified on-chain and
  1339	    ///      cannot be manipulated).
  1340	    ///      Also resolves any pending gambling burn redemptions (mirrors rngGate behavior, CP-06 fix).
  1341	    /// @return word RNG word, 1 if request sent, or 0 if waiting on fallback.
  1342	    function _gameOverEntropy(
  1343	        uint48 ts,
  1344	        uint24 day,
  1345	        uint24 lvl,
  1346	        bool isTicketJackpotDay
  1347	    ) private returns (uint256 word) {
  1348	        if (rngWordByDay[day] != 0) return rngWordByDay[day];
  1349	
  1350	        uint256 currentWord = rngWordCurrent;
  1351	        if (currentWord != 0 && rngRequestTime != 0) {
  1352	            currentWord = _applyDailyRng(day, currentWord);
  1353	            if (lvl != 0) {
  1354	                // Gameover settles the final day's flips but never grants a bonus (0).
  1355	                coinflip.processCoinflipPayouts(0, currentWord, day);
  1356	            }
  1357	            // Resolve the sentinel-stamped gambling-burn pool if any. Same shape as the
  1358	            // rngGate redemption resolution path — sentinel-keyed so multi-day stalls resolve
  1359	            // by construction.
  1360	            {
  1361	                IsDGNRS sdgnrs = IsDGNRS(
  1362	                    ContractAddresses.SDGNRS
  1363	                );
  1364	                uint24 toResolve = sdgnrs.pendingResolveDay();
  1365	                if (toResolve != 0) {
  1366	                    uint16 redemptionRoll = uint16(
  1367	                        ((currentWord >> 8) % 151) + 25
  1368	                    );
  1369	                    sdgnrs.resolveRedemptionPeriod(redemptionRoll, toResolve);
  1370	                }
  1371	            }
  1372	            _finalizeLootboxRng(currentWord);
  1373	            return currentWord;
  1374	        }
  1375	
  1376	        // VRF-death deadman: in a suppressed phase (jackpot / last-purchase) where no day has
  1377	        // sealed for _VRF_DEADMAN_DAYS, commit the historical fallback immediately — regardless
  1378	        // of whether a request is outstanding or how long it has been pending — so a
  1379	        // permanently-dead VRF there reaches terminal fund release without the
  1380	        // GAMEOVER_RNG_FALLBACK_DELAY wait. Gated to the suppressed phases so the normal
  1381	        // purchase-phase / genesis game-over keeps its two-step real-VRF request path. Outside
  1382	        // the deadman, honor the normal grace: an outstanding request waits the fallback delay,
  1383	        // otherwise a fresh request is issued.
  1384	        bool deadman = (jackpotPhaseFlag || lastPurchaseDay) && _vrfDeadmanFired();
  1385	        if (rngRequestTime != 0 || deadman) {
  1386	            if (!deadman && ts - rngRequestTime < GAMEOVER_RNG_FALLBACK_DELAY) {
  1387	                revert RngNotReady();
  1388	            }
  1389	            // Use earliest historical VRF word as fallback (more secure than blockhash)
  1390	            uint256 fallbackWord = _getHistoricalRngFallback(day);
  1391	            // Cancel any reverseFlip nudge from the fallback word: the VRF-dead fallback
  1392	            // never set rngLockedFlag, so reverseFlip stayed open and a committer could
  1393	            // otherwise steer the terminal distribution. Pre-subtracting cancels the +=
  1394	            // inside _applyDailyRng (and consumes the nudges), leaving the pure
  1395	            // historical+prevrandao word.
  1396	            unchecked { fallbackWord -= totalFlipReversals; }
  1397	            fallbackWord = _applyDailyRng(day, fallbackWord);
  1398	            if (lvl != 0) {
  1399	                // Gameover settles the final day's flips but never grants a bonus (0).
  1400	                coinflip.processCoinflipPayouts(0, fallbackWord, day);
  1401	            }
  1402	            // Resolve the sentinel-stamped gambling-burn pool if any. Fallback path
  1403	            // uses fallbackWord for the roll; sentinel still names the stuck day so resolves
  1404	            // are correct even after a GAMEOVER_RNG_FALLBACK_DELAY (14-day) stall.
  1405	            {
  1406	                IsDGNRS sdgnrs = IsDGNRS(
  1407	                    ContractAddresses.SDGNRS
  1408	                );
  1409	                uint24 toResolve = sdgnrs.pendingResolveDay();
  1410	                if (toResolve != 0) {
  1411	                    uint16 redemptionRoll = uint16(
  1412	                        ((fallbackWord >> 8) % 151) + 25
  1413	                    );
  1414	                    sdgnrs.resolveRedemptionPeriod(redemptionRoll, toResolve);
  1415	                }
  1416	            }
  1417	            _finalizeLootboxRng(fallbackWord);
  1418	            // Release the stall lock once fallback has committed; liveness now reads
  1419	            // from day math alone instead of short-circuiting on a stale VRF timer.
  1420	            rngRequestTime = 0;
  1421	            return fallbackWord;
  1422	        }
  1423	
  1424	        if (_tryRequestRng(isTicketJackpotDay, lvl)) {
  1425	            return 1;
  1426	        }
  1427	
  1428	        // VRF request failed; start fallback timer (rngRequestTime != 0 acts as lock).
  1429	        rngWordCurrent = 0;
  1430	        rngRequestTime = ts;
  1431	        return 0;
  1432	    }
  1433	
  1434	    /// @dev Get historical VRF fallback entropy for gameover RNG.
  1435	    ///      Collects up to 5 early historical VRF words and hashes them together
  1436	    ///      with currentDay and block.prevrandao. Historical words are committed VRF
  1437	    ///      (non-manipulable), prevrandao adds unpredictability at the cost of 1-bit
  1438	    ///      validator manipulation (propose or skip). Acceptable trade-off for a
  1439	    ///      gameover-only fallback path when VRF is dead.
  1440	    ///      If no historical words exist, falls through to prevrandao-only
  1441	    ///      entropy. This can only happen at level 0 (zero VRF history means
  1442	    ///      zero completed advances), so the 1-bit validator bias is irrelevant.
  1443	    /// @param currentDay Current day index.
  1444	    /// @return word Combined historical entropy.
  1445	    function _getHistoricalRngFallback(
  1446	        uint24 currentDay
  1447	    ) private view returns (uint256 word) {
  1448	        uint256 found;
  1449	        uint256 combined;
  1450	        uint24 searchLimit = currentDay > 30 ? 30 : currentDay;
  1451	        for (uint24 searchDay = 1; searchDay < searchLimit; ) {
  1452	            uint256 w = rngWordByDay[searchDay];
  1453	            if (w != 0) {
  1454	                combined = EntropyLib.hash2(combined, w);
  1455	                unchecked {
  1456	                    ++found;
  1457	                }
  1458	                if (found == 5) break;
  1459	            }
  1460	            unchecked {
  1461	                ++searchDay;
  1462	            }
  1463	        }
  1464	
  1465	        word = uint256(
  1466	            keccak256(abi.encodePacked(combined, currentDay, block.prevrandao))
  1467	        );
  1468	        if (word == 0) word = 1;
  1469	    }
  1470	
  1471	    /*+======================================================================+
  1472	      |                    FUTURE PRIZE POOL DRAW                           |
  1473	      +======================================================================+
  1474	      |  Release a portion of the future prize pool once per level.         |
  1475	      |  Normal levels draw 15%, x00 levels skip the draw.                   |
  1476	      +======================================================================+*/
  1477	
  1478	    function _nextToFutureBps(
  1479	        uint32 elapsed,
  1480	        uint24 lvl
  1481	    ) internal pure returns (uint16) {
  1482	        uint256 lvlBonus = (uint256(lvl % 100) / 10) * 100; // +1% per 10 levels within cycle
  1483	        uint256 bps;
  1484	        if (elapsed <= 1) {
  1485	            bps = NEXT_TO_FUTURE_BPS_FAST + lvlBonus;
  1486	        } else if (elapsed <= 14) {
  1487	            uint256 elapsedAfterDay = elapsed - 1;
  1488	            uint256 delta = NEXT_TO_FUTURE_BPS_FAST +
  1489	                lvlBonus -
  1490	                NEXT_TO_FUTURE_BPS_MIN;
  1491	            bps =
  1492	                NEXT_TO_FUTURE_BPS_FAST +
  1493	                lvlBonus -
  1494	                (delta * elapsedAfterDay) /
  1495	                13;
  1496	        } else if (elapsed <= 28) {
  1497	            uint256 elapsedAfterMin = elapsed - 14;
  1498	            uint256 delta = NEXT_TO_FUTURE_BPS_FAST +
  1499	                lvlBonus -
  1500	                NEXT_TO_FUTURE_BPS_MIN;
  1501	            bps = NEXT_TO_FUTURE_BPS_MIN + (delta * elapsedAfterMin) / 14;
  1502	        } else {
  1503	            bps =
  1504	                NEXT_TO_FUTURE_BPS_FAST +
  1505	                lvlBonus +
  1506	                (elapsed - 28) *
  1507	                NEXT_TO_FUTURE_BPS_DAY_STEP;
  1508	        }
  1509	        return uint16(bps > 10_000 ? 10_000 : bps);
  1510	    }

  1515	      |  Future ticket rewards are staged per level and activated at the     |

   542	    /// @notice Emitted when traits are generated for a player's ticket batch.

   544	    event TraitsGenerated(
   545	        address indexed player,
   546	        uint256 baseKey,
   547	        uint32 take
   548	    );
   549	
   550	    /// @notice Emitted when entries are queued for a buyer at a specific level.
   551	    event EntriesQueued(
   552	        address indexed buyer,
   553	        uint24 targetLevel,
   554	        uint32 entries
   555	    );
   620	        MintPaymentKind payKind,
   621	        uint256 costWei
   622	    );
   623	
   624	    /// @notice Emitted when a boon is consumed by a player.
   625	    event BoonConsumed(address indexed player, uint8 boonType, uint16 boostBps);
   626	
   627	    /// @notice Emitted when admin swaps game ETH for stETH.
   628	    event AdminSwapEthForStEth(address indexed recipient, uint256 amount);
   629	
   630	    /// @notice Emitted when admin stakes game ETH into Lido stETH.

   632	
   633	    /// @dev True when gameover liveness guard would fire within ~1 day (day-granularity).
   634	    ///      Used to activate distress-mode lootbox behaviour: 100% nextpool allocation
   635	    ///      and 25% ticket bonus on the distress-bought portion.
   636	    function _isDistressMode() internal view returns (bool) {
   637	        if (gameOver) return false;
   638	        uint24 psd = purchaseStartDay;
   639	        uint24 currentDay = _simulatedDayIndex();
   640	        if (level == 0) {
   641	            // Distress fires on the final day before the liveness guard would trigger.
   642	            return currentDay >= psd + _DEPLOY_IDLE_TIMEOUT_DAYS;
   643	        }
   644	        return currentDay >= psd + 120;
   645	    }
   646	
   647	    /// @dev Queues entries for a buyer at a target level. The `entries` arg is in
   648	    ///      entry units (price/4 each), NOT whole tickets — 4 entries per
   649	    ///      whole ticket. `owed` accumulates entries.
   650	    ///      If buyer has no existing entries at that level, adds them to the queue.
   651	    ///      Caps at uint32 max to prevent overflow.
   652	    /// @param buyer Address to receive entries.
   653	    /// @param targetLevel Level for which entries are queued.
   654	    /// @param entries Number of entries to queue (price/4 units).
   655	    function _queueEntries(
   656	        address buyer,
   657	        uint24 targetLevel,
   658	        uint32 entries,
   659	        bool rngBypass
   660	    ) internal {
   661	        if (entries == 0) return;
   662	        // No liveness gate here: tickets queued during the liveness-timeout window are harmless.
   663	        // They are never processed (the game-over drain ends the game without a further daily
   664	        // draw) or the resolving daily word has not been requested yet, so no terminal jackpot
   665	        // can be manipulated by them. Player purchase paths gate liveness at their own entry; the
   666	        // advance-chain daily-jackpot distribution also queues through this sink and must NOT be
   667	        // reverted here, so the gate stays off the shared sink.
   668	        emit EntriesQueued(buyer, targetLevel, entries);
   669	        bool isFarFuture = targetLevel > level + 5;
   670	        if (isFarFuture && rngLockedFlag && !rngBypass) revert RngLocked();
   671	        uint24 wk = isFarFuture
   672	            ? _tqFarFutureKey(targetLevel)
   673	            : _tqWriteKey(targetLevel);
   674	        uint40 packed = entriesOwedPacked[wk][buyer];
   675	        uint32 owed = uint32(packed >> 8);
   676	        uint8 rem = uint8(packed);
   677	        if (packed == 0) {
   678	            ticketQueue[wk].push(buyer);
   679	        }
   680	        unchecked {
   681	            owed += entries;
   682	        }
   683	        entriesOwedPacked[wk][buyer] = (uint40(owed) << 8) | uint40(rem);
   684	    }
   685	
   686	    /// @dev Converts a post-Bernoulli whole-ticket count into the entries unit the
   687	    ///      entriesOwedPacked sink accumulates. One whole ticket (priceForLevel(level))
   688	    ///      is 4 entries (each = price/4), so entries = wholeTickets << 2.
   689	    ///      The sole canonical whole->entries conversion both prize legs route through.
   690	    ///      `wholeTickets` is provably <= ~42.9M (scaledWholeTickets/100, uint32-capped), so
   691	    ///      `<< 2` <= ~171.8M fits uint32 (4.29e9) with a 25x margin — no overflow guard.
   692	    /// @param wholeTickets Whole-ticket count (each = priceForLevel(level)).
   693	    /// @return Entries count (each = price/4); 4 per whole ticket.
   694	    function wholeTicketsToEntries(uint32 wholeTickets) internal pure returns (uint32) {
   695	        return wholeTickets << 2;
   696	    }
   697	
   698	    /// @dev Queues scaled entries (2 decimal places) for fractional purchases.
   699	    ///      Handles remainder accumulation and promotes to a whole owed entry when
   700	    ///      remainder >= QTY_SCALE.
   701	    /// @param buyer Address to receive entries.
   702	    /// @param targetLevel Level for which entries are queued.
   703	    /// @param entriesScaled Scaled entries (entries x 100); owed gains entriesScaled / QTY_SCALE entries.
   704	    function _queueEntriesScaled(
   705	        address buyer,
   706	        uint24 targetLevel,
   707	        uint32 entriesScaled,
   708	        bool rngBypass
   709	    ) internal {
   710	        if (entriesScaled == 0) return;
   711	        // No liveness gate (see _queueEntries): post-liveness queued tickets are harmless.
   712	        emit EntriesQueuedScaled(buyer, targetLevel, entriesScaled);
   713	        bool isFarFuture = targetLevel > level + 5;
   714	        if (isFarFuture && rngLockedFlag && !rngBypass) revert RngLocked();
   715	        uint24 wk = isFarFuture
   716	            ? _tqFarFutureKey(targetLevel)
   717	            : _tqWriteKey(targetLevel);
   718	        uint40 packed = entriesOwedPacked[wk][buyer];
   719	        uint32 owed = uint32(packed >> 8);
   720	        uint8 rem = uint8(packed);
   721	        if (packed == 0) {
   722	            ticketQueue[wk].push(buyer);
   723	        }
   724	
   725	        uint32 whole = uint32(uint256(entriesScaled) / QTY_SCALE);
   726	        uint8 frac = uint8(uint256(entriesScaled) % QTY_SCALE);
   727	        unchecked {
   728	            owed += whole;
   729	        }
   730	
   731	        if (frac != 0) {
   732	            uint16 newRem;
   733	            unchecked {
   734	                newRem = uint16(rem) + uint16(frac);
   735	            }
   736	            if (newRem >= QTY_SCALE) {
   737	                unchecked {
   738	                    owed += 1;
   739	                }
   740	                newRem -= uint16(QTY_SCALE);
   741	            }
   742	            rem = uint8(newRem);
   743	        }
   744	        uint40 newPacked = (uint40(owed) << 8) | uint40(rem);
   745	        if (newPacked != packed) {
   746	            entriesOwedPacked[wk][buyer] = newPacked;
   747	        }
   748	    }
   749	
   750	    /// @dev Queues tickets for a contiguous range of levels with same quantity per level.
   751	    ///      Optimized for whale pass claims to minimize loop overhead.
   752	    /// @param buyer Address to receive tickets.
   753	    /// @param startLevel First level in range (inclusive).
   754	    /// @param numLevels Number of consecutive levels.
   755	    /// @param entriesPerLevel Entries to award per level (4 entries = 1 whole ticket).
   756	    function _queueEntryRange(
   757	        address buyer,
   758	        uint24 startLevel,
   759	        uint24 numLevels,
   760	        uint32 entriesPerLevel,
   761	        bool rngBypass
   762	    ) internal {
   763	        // No liveness gate (see _queueEntries): post-liveness queued tickets are harmless.
   764	        emit EntriesQueuedRange(buyer, startLevel, numLevels, entriesPerLevel);
   765	        // Loop-invariant slot-0 reads cached outside the loop (the body's mapping
   766	        // SSTOREs block the optimizer from hoisting them; neither value has a
   767	        // writer reachable from the body). The per-level lock check observes the
   768	        // same locked value either way.
   769	        uint24 currentLevel = level;
   770	        bool rngLockedCached = rngLockedFlag;
   771	        uint24 writeSlotBit = ticketWriteSlot ? TICKET_SLOT_BIT : uint24(0);
   772	        uint24 lvl = startLevel;
   773	        for (uint24 i = 0; i < numLevels; ) {
   774	            bool isFarFuture = lvl > currentLevel + 5;
   775	            if (isFarFuture && rngLockedCached && !rngBypass) revert RngLocked();
   776	            uint24 wk = isFarFuture ? _tqFarFutureKey(lvl) : (lvl | writeSlotBit);
   777	            uint40 packed = entriesOwedPacked[wk][buyer];
   778	            uint32 owed = uint32(packed >> 8);
   779	            uint8 rem = uint8(packed);
   780	            if (packed == 0) {
   781	                ticketQueue[wk].push(buyer);
   782	            }
   783	            unchecked {
   784	                owed += entriesPerLevel;
   785	            }
   786	            entriesOwedPacked[wk][buyer] = (uint40(owed) << 8) | uint40(rem);
   787	
   788	            unchecked {
   789	                ++lvl;
   790	                ++i;
   791	            }
   792	        }
   793	    }

   795	    // =========================================================================
   796	    // Packed Prize Pool Helpers
   797	    // =========================================================================
   798	
   799	    function _setPrizePools(uint128 next, uint128 future) internal {
   800	        prizePoolsPacked = (uint256(future) << 128) | uint256(next);
   801	    }
   802	
   803	    function _getPrizePools()
   804	        internal
   805	        view
   806	        returns (uint128 next, uint128 future)
   807	    {
   808	        uint256 packed = prizePoolsPacked;
   809	        next = uint128(packed);
   810	        future = uint128(packed >> 128);
   811	    }
   812	
   813	    function _setPendingPools(uint128 next, uint128 future) internal {
   814	        prizePoolPendingPacked = (uint256(future) << 128) | uint256(next);
   815	    }
   816	
   817	    function _getPendingPools()
   818	        internal
   819	        view
   820	        returns (uint128 next, uint128 future)
   821	    {
   822	        uint256 packed = prizePoolPendingPacked;
   823	        next = uint128(packed);
   824	        future = uint128(packed >> 128);
   825	    }
   826	
   827	    /// @dev Add a combined prize contribution to the active accumulator in ONE RMW. The purchase
   828	    ///      path computes each leg's next/future split (ticket and lootbox legs use different
   829	    ///      ratios), sums the post-split totals, and lands both in a single packed slot — the
   830	    ///      pending buffer while the pool is frozen, otherwise the live pools.
   831	    function _addPrizeContribution(uint128 nextAdd, uint128 futureAdd) internal {
   832	        if (nextAdd == 0 && futureAdd == 0) return;
   833	        if (prizePoolFrozen) {
   834	            (uint128 pNext, uint128 pFuture) = _getPendingPools();
   835	            _setPendingPools(pNext + nextAdd, pFuture + futureAdd);
   836	        } else {
   837	            (uint128 next, uint128 future) = _getPrizePools();
   838	            _setPrizePools(next + nextAdd, future + futureAdd);
   839	        }
   840	    }
   841	
   842	    // =========================================================================
   843	    // Ticket Queue Key Encoding
   844	    // =========================================================================
   845	
   846	    /// @dev Compute the ticket queue key for the write slot.
   847	    ///      Slot 0 uses raw level, slot 1 sets bit 23.
   848	    function _tqWriteKey(uint24 lvl) internal view returns (uint24) {
   849	        return ticketWriteSlot ? lvl | TICKET_SLOT_BIT : lvl;
   850	    }
   851	
   852	    /// @dev Compute the ticket queue key for the read slot (opposite of write).
   853	    function _tqReadKey(uint24 lvl) internal view returns (uint24) {
   854	        return !ticketWriteSlot ? lvl | TICKET_SLOT_BIT : lvl;
   855	    }
   856	
   857	    /// @dev Compute the ticket queue key for the far-future key space.
   858	    ///      Always sets bit 22, independent of ticketWriteSlot.
   859	    ///      Far-future tickets are not double-buffered; they persist until
   860	    ///      drained by processFutureTicketBatch.
   861	    function _tqFarFutureKey(uint24 lvl) internal pure returns (uint24) {
   862	        return lvl | TICKET_FAR_FUTURE_BIT;
   863	    }
   864	
   865	    // =========================================================================
   866	    // Single-Component Prize Pool Accessors
   867	    // =========================================================================
   868	
   869	    /// @dev Returns the next pool component.
   870	    function _getNextPrizePool() internal view returns (uint256) {
   871	        (uint128 next, ) = _getPrizePools();
   872	        return uint256(next);
   873	    }
   874	
   875	    /// @dev Sets only the next pool component.
  1480	    }
  1481	
  1482	    /// @dev Whether the liveness-timeout game-over trigger is active.
  1483	    ///      Level 0: deploy idle timeout (365 days since purchaseStartDay).
  1484	    ///      Level 1+: 120-day inactivity timeout since purchaseStartDay.
  1485	    ///
  1486	    ///      Productive-phase pause: returns false while lastPurchaseDay or
  1487	    ///      jackpotPhaseFlag is set. The day clock would otherwise fire
  1488	    ///      inside the multi-call window between target-met and phase
  1489	    ///      transition close (when purchaseStartDay is finally updated to
  1490	    ///      the new level's start), but _handleGameOverPath is unreachable
  1491	    ///      in that window (gated by !inJackpot && !lastPurchase at
  1492	    ///      AdvanceModule:182), so a fire would deadlock _queueEntries calls
  1493	    ///      with no path to clear rngLockedFlag. phaseTransitionActive is
  1494	    ///      implied by jackpotPhaseFlag throughout — they clear together at
  1495	    ///      AdvanceModule:328-331.
  1496	    ///
  1497	    ///      Day math is evaluated first so mid-drain RNG requests (which set
  1498	    ///      rngRequestTime during _handleGameOverPath) cannot transiently flip
  1499	    ///      liveness back to false while the drain is in progress.
  1500	    ///
  1501	    ///      Stalled-advance bailout: if day math has not yet been met, ANY
  1502	    ///      condition that leaves rngRequestTime non-zero for _VRF_GRACE_PERIOD
  1503	    ///      fires liveness. This covers genuine VRF death (callback never lands)
  1504	    ///      and any bug that prevents the advance state machine from reaching
  1505	    ///      _unlockRng — gas exhaustion, unexpected reverts in drain/jackpot
  1506	    ///      paths, or any other failure mode that bricks the cycle after a
  1507	    ///      fulfilled callback. In all cases the historical-fallback path in
  1508	    ///      _gameOverEntropy engages, letting funds drain to players via
  1509	    ///      terminal jackpot rather than remaining trapped. Below that
  1510	    ///      threshold, liveness stays false — players can propose a coordinator
  1511	    ///      rotation via DegenerusAdmin, and missed days are credited back to
  1512	    ///      purchaseStartDay in AdvanceModule.rngGate on fulfillment.
  1513	    function _livenessTriggered() internal view returns (bool) {
  1514	        // Jackpot / last-purchase suppress the in-phase clocks (they would false-fire in the
  1515	        // productive window between target-met and phase-transition close), but the
  1516	        // phase-independent VRF-death deadman still fires here so a permanently-stalled game in
  1517	        // these phases reaches terminal fund release instead of bricking.
  1518	        if (lastPurchaseDay || jackpotPhaseFlag) return _vrfDeadmanFired();
  1519	        uint24 lvl = level;
  1520	        uint24 psd = purchaseStartDay;
  1521	        uint24 currentDay = _simulatedDayIndex();
  1522	        if (lvl == 0 && currentDay - psd > _DEPLOY_IDLE_TIMEOUT_DAYS) return true;
  1523	        if (lvl != 0 && currentDay - psd > 120) return true;
  1524	        uint48 rngStart = rngRequestTime;
  1525	        return rngStart != 0 && block.timestamp - rngStart >= _VRF_GRACE_PERIOD;
  1526	    }
  1527	
  1528	    /// @dev VRF-death deadman: true once no day has sealed for _VRF_DEADMAN_DAYS. dailyIdx
  1529	    ///      advances only in _unlockRng (a completed day), so currentDay - dailyIdx counts days
  1530	    ///      since real progress and freezes during any stall — and stays frozen until game-over
  1531	    ///      latches (the terminal _unlockRng runs after gameOver is set), so it never evaporates
  1532	    ///      mid-drain. Phase-independent, unlike _livenessTriggered: advanceGame consults it to
  1533	    ///      reach terminal fund release even while jackpotPhaseFlag / lastPurchaseDay are set.
  1534	    function _vrfDeadmanFired() internal view returns (bool) {
  1535	        return _simulatedDayIndex() - dailyIdx > _VRF_DEADMAN_DAYS;
  1536	    }
  1537	
  1538	    /// @dev Returns the day index for a specific timestamp.
  1539	    function _simulatedDayIndexAt(uint48 ts) internal pure returns (uint24) {
  1540	        return GameTimeLib.currentDayIndexAt(ts);
  1541	    }
  1542	
  1543	    /// @dev Gets the current mint day from dailyIdx or calculates from timestamp.
  1544	    function _currentMintDay() internal view returns (uint24) {
  1545	        uint24 day = dailyIdx;
  1546	        if (day == 0) {
  1547	            day = _simulatedDayIndex();
  1548	        }
  1549	        return day;
  1550	    }
  1551	
  1552	    /// @dev Updates the day field in packed mint data if changed.
  1553	    function _setMintDay(
  1554	        uint256 data,
  1555	        uint24 day,
  1556	        uint256 dayShift,
  1557	        uint256 dayMask
  1558	    ) internal pure returns (uint256) {
  1559	        uint24 prevDay = uint24((data >> dayShift) & dayMask);
  1560	        if (prevDay == day) return data;
  1561	        uint256 clearedDay = data & ~(dayMask << dayShift);
  1562	        return clearedDay | (uint256(day) << dayShift);
  1563	    }
  1564	
  1565	    // =========================================================================

   591	
   592	    /// @dev Roll remainder chance for a fractional ticket (0-99).
   593	    function _rollRemainder(
   594	        uint256 entropy,
   595	        uint256 rollSalt,
   596	        uint8 rem
   597	    ) private pure returns (bool win) {
   598	        // Hash via scratch-slot keccak so player address (stored in rollSalt
   599	        // bits 191-32) reaches the low 7 bits of rollEntropy consumed by
   600	        // `% QTY_SCALE`. A plain XOR mix only diffuses bits a fixed span
   601	        // outward, leaving upper player-address bits invisible to the roll
   602	        // outcome; keccak gives full low-bit diffusion of the high-bit input.
   603	        uint256 rollEntropy = EntropyLib.hash2(entropy, rollSalt);
   604	        return (rollEntropy % QTY_SCALE) < rem;
   605	    }
   606	
   607	    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   608	    // External Entry Point — Current-Level Ticket Batch Processing
   609	    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   610	
   611	    /// @notice Processes a batch of current-level tickets with gas-bounded iteration.
   612	    /// @dev Called iteratively until all tickets for the level are processed. Uses a writes budget
   613	    ///      to stay within block gas limits. The first batch in a new level round is
   614	    ///      scaled down by 35% to account for cold storage access costs.
   615	    /// @param lvl Level whose tickets should be processed.
   616	    /// @return finished True if all tickets for this level have been fully processed.
   617	    /// @return didWork True if this call materialized at least one ticket entry or foil
   618	    ///         buyer. Lets the advance chain break out before composing a finishing batch
   619	    ///         with a same-tx BAF/jackpot, even when the batch both starts and finishes in
   620	    ///         one call (cursor returns to 0, so a cursor-delta probe would read no work).
   621	    function processTicketBatch(uint24 lvl)
   622	        external
   623	        returns (bool finished, bool didWork)
   624	    {
   625	        uint24 rk = _tqReadKey(lvl);
   626	        mapping(address => uint40) storage owedMap = entriesOwedPacked[rk];
   627	        address[] storage queue = ticketQueue[rk];
   628	        uint256 total = queue.length;
   629	
   630	        if (ticketLevel != lvl) {
   631	            ticketLevel = lvl;
   632	            ticketCursor = 0;
   633	        }
   634	
   635	        uint256 idx = ticketCursor;
   636	        uint32 writesBudget = WRITES_BUDGET_SAFE;
   637	        if (idx == 0) {
   638	            writesBudget -= (writesBudget * 35) / 100; // 65% scaling for cold storage
   639	        }
   640	
   641	        if (idx >= total) {
   642	            // Normal queue already drained (empty this level, or finished on a prior
   643	            // tx). Still drain the per-buy-day foil buckets on the full budget before
   644	            // declaring the level finished — the readiness gate depends on it. Returns
   645	            // false (resume next tx) only if a budget-short foil pack defers. _drainFoil
   646	            // short-circuits on _foilDrainPending, so a no-foil call is a single SLOAD.
   647	            if (total != 0) {
   648	                delete ticketQueue[rk];
   649	            }
   650	            (bool foilDoneEmpty, bool foilDrainedEmpty) = _drainFoil(writesBudget);
   651	            ticketCursor = 0;
   652	            if (foilDoneEmpty) {
   653	                ticketLevel = 0;
   654	                return (true, foilDrainedEmpty);
   655	            }
   656	            return (false, foilDrainedEmpty);
   657	        }
   658	
   659	        uint256 entropy = lootboxRngWordByIndex[uint48(_lrRead(LR_INDEX_SHIFT, LR_INDEX_MASK)) - 1];
   660	
   661	        uint32 used;
   662	        uint32 processed;
   663	
   664	        // Trait-batch scratch buffers shared by every normal entry this call (zeroed
   665	        // between entries inside _raritySymbolBatch), so memory does not grow per
   666	        // queue entry. The foil drain runs in a separate module and owns its scratch.
   667	        uint32[256] memory counts;
   668	        uint8[256] memory touchedTraits;
   669	
   670	        while (idx < total && used < writesBudget) {
   671	            (uint32 writesUsed, uint32 take, bool advance) = _processOneTicketEntry(
   672	                queue[idx],
   673	                lvl,
   674	                owedMap,
   675	                writesBudget - used,
   676	                processed,
   677	                entropy,
   678	                idx,
   679	                counts,
   680	                touchedTraits
   681	            );
   682	            if (writesUsed == 0 && !advance) break;
   683	            unchecked {
   684	                used += writesUsed;
   685	                if (advance) {
   686	                    ++idx;
   687	                    processed = 0;
   688	                } else {
   689	                    // Advance the within-player startIndex by the per-iter ticket
   690	                    // count, matching processFutureTicketBatch. A gas-budget-derived
   691	                    // writesUsed>>1 heuristic would diverge for take > 256.
   692	                    processed += take;
   693	                }
   694	            }
   695	        }
   696	
   697	        if (idx >= total) {
   698	            // Normal queue drained. Continue into the per-buy-day foil buckets on the
   699	            // leftover write budget (one shared envelope, so the combined advance
   700	            // stays gas-bounded). A foil buyer resolves a fixed FOIL_PACK_ENTRIES
   701	            // (16) boosted entries atomically; foilDrainDay/foilCursor make a
   702	            // budget-short deferral resumable. Only when BOTH the queue and the foil
   703	            // drain are caught up is the level finished, so the readiness gate cannot
   704	            // let the jackpot draw early.
   705	            if (total != 0) {
   706	                delete ticketQueue[rk];
   707	            }
   708	            // A final loop iteration can push `used` past writesBudget, so clamp the
   709	            // leftover to 0 (an over-budget call leaves no room for foil and defers it
   710	            // to the next tx) rather than underflowing the subtraction.
   711	            uint32 foilRoom = used >= writesBudget ? 0 : writesBudget - used;
   712	            (bool foilDone, bool foilDrained) = _drainFoil(foilRoom);
   713	            // Real work this call iff the normal loop consumed budget OR a foil buyer
   714	            // resolved — the signal the advance chain reads to refuse same-tx composition.
   715	            bool didWorkThisCall = used > 0 || foilDrained;
   716	            if (foilDone) {
   717	                ticketCursor = 0;
   718	                ticketLevel = 0;
   719	                return (true, didWorkThisCall);
   720	            }
   721	            // Foil queue has more entries than the budget could fit: resume next tx.
   722	            // The normal cursor stays at its terminal position (idx == total).
   723	            ticketCursor = uint32(idx);
   724	            return (false, didWorkThisCall);
   725	        }
   726	        // Mid-queue stop: persist the resume cursor. The finished path above writes
   727	        // its own terminal cursor/level pair, so the shared packed slot is stored
   728	        // exactly once per path.
   729	        ticketCursor = uint32(idx);
   730	        return (false, used > 0);
   731	    }
   732

   734	    ///      budget after the normal queue is exhausted. The drain itself lives in

   920	            payKind,

   923	    }
   924	
   925	    /// @notice Redeem FLIP for current-jackpot tickets — allowed only inside the jackpot window.
   926	    /// @dev Reverts unless the FLIP purchase window is open: the prize target is met in the purchase
   927	    ///      phase, or the jackpot phase is live, with no RNG in flight. Outside that window FLIP ticket
   928	    ///      purchases revert so bonus tickets and prize ETH accrue to real-ETH buyers.
   929	    /// @param buyer Recipient of the purchased tickets.
   930	    /// @param entryQuantityScaled Number of tickets to purchase (2 decimals, scaled by 100).
   931	    function redeemFlip(
   932	        address buyer,
   933	        uint256 entryQuantityScaled
   934	    ) external {
   935	        _redeemFlipFor(buyer, entryQuantityScaled);
   936	    }
   937	
   938	    function _redeemFlipFor(
   939	        address buyer,
   940	        uint256 entryQuantityScaled
   941	    ) private {
   942	        if (_livenessTriggered()) revert E();
   943	
   944	        if (entryQuantityScaled != 0) {
   945	            // FLIP purchase window: opens the first time a redeem lands once the prize target is met
   946	            // in the purchase phase with no RNG in flight, latching a single warm slot-0 bit. It stays
   947	            // open through the jackpot days and is cleared in the advance at the final jackpot day's RNG
   948	            // request — the boundary where new tickets route to the next level (rngLockedFlag stays set
   949	            // from that request until _unlockRng, so it can never flip back on during the wind-down).
   950	            // While it is closed (an open/stalled purchase phase) FLIP purchases revert, so bonus
   951	            // tickets and prize ETH accrue to real-ETH buyers. The target-met condition holds for the
   952	            // whole of lastPurchaseDay, so even a one-day purchase phase still offers that day as a
   953	            // redemption window.
   954	            if (!ticketRedemptionOpen) {
   955	                if (
   956	                    rngLockedFlag ||
   957	                    _getNextPrizePool() < levelPrizePool[level]
   958	                ) revert E();
   959	                ticketRedemptionOpen = true;
   960	            }
   961	
   962	            uint24 cachedLevel = level;
   963	            (
   964	                ,
   965	                uint32 adjustedQty32,
   966	                uint24 targetLevel,
   967	                uint32 flipMintUnits,
   968	                ,

   976	                    true,
   977	                    bytes32(0),
   978	                    0,
   979	                    cachedLevel,
   980	                    jackpotPhaseFlag
   981	                );
   982	
   983	            // MINT_FLIP quest leg only (no ETH spend, no lootbox): skips activity
   984	            // score, affiliate, and non-mint quests. The returned reward is a FLIP
   985	            // flip stake, awarded via creditFlip — the full coin cost was already
   986	            // burned inside _callTicketPurchase.
   987	            {
   988	                uint256 nextLevelPrice = PriceLookupLib.priceForLevel(
   989	                    cachedLevel + 1
   990	                );
   991	                (uint256 questReward, , , bool questCompleted) = quests
   992	                    .handlePurchase(
   993	                        buyer,
   994	                        0,
   995	                        flipMintUnits,
   996	                        0,
   997	                        nextLevelPrice,
   998	                        nextLevelPrice
   999	                    );
  1000	                if (questCompleted && questReward != 0) {
  1001	                    coinflip.creditFlip(buyer, questReward);
  1002	                }
  1003	            }
  1004	
  1005	            // Queue tickets on the captured adjusted quantity.
  1006	            if (adjustedQty32 != 0) {
  1007	                _queueEntriesScaled(buyer, targetLevel, adjustedQty32, false);
  1008	            }
  1009	        }
  1010	    }
  1011	
  1012	    /// @notice Emitted on a far-future salvage swap (sellFarFutureEntries).
  1013	    /// @dev `buyer` is the counterparty that funded the swap and received the far-future tickets:
  1014	    ///      sDGNRS normally, or the vault on the owner-enabled fallback when sDGNRS cannot fund it.
  1015	    ///      cashWei subdivides into ethCashWei (relabeled claimable) + flipTokens (buyer-owned FLIP
  1260	
  1261	    /// @dev Single-tx callers: the fresh-ETH portion is `msg.value`. Read here (a private fn)
  1262	    ///      so external non-payable callers (e.g. claimable-only paths) never reference msg.value.
  1263	    function _purchaseFor(
  1264	        address buyer,
  1265	        uint256 entryQuantityScaled,
  1266	        uint256 lootBoxAmount,
  1267	        bytes32 affiliateCode,
  1268	        MintPaymentKind payKind
  1269	    ) private {
  1270	        (
  1271	            bool cachedJpFlag,
  1272	            uint24 cachedLevel,
  1273	            uint256 priceWei,
  1274	            uint256 ticketCost
  1275	        ) = _purchaseCostInputs(entryQuantityScaled);
  1276	        // Single-tx path: cap fresh ETH at the mint cost and credit any overpay to the
  1277	        // payer's withdrawable afking, so excess never reverts or strands. The afking
  1278	        // ticket-buy path (purchaseWith) bypasses this, so it is unaffected.
  1279	        uint256 cost = ticketCost + lootBoxAmount;
  1280	        uint256 fresh = payKind == MintPaymentKind.Claimable
  1281	            ? 0
  1282	            : (msg.value < cost ? msg.value : cost);
  1283	        if (msg.value > fresh) _creditAfkingValue(msg.sender, msg.value - fresh);
  1284	        _purchaseForWithCached(
  1285	            buyer,
  1286	            entryQuantityScaled,
  1287	            lootBoxAmount,
  1288	            affiliateCode,
  1289	            payKind,
  1290	            fresh,
  1291	            cachedJpFlag,
  1292	            cachedLevel,
  1293	            priceWei,
  1294	            ticketCost
  1295	        );
  1296	    }
  1297	
  1298	    /// @dev Phase flag, level, whole-ticket price at the active purchase level, and the
  1299	    ///      ticket cost of `entryQuantityScaled` — read and computed once per purchase, then
  1300	    ///      threaded into _purchaseForWithCached so no caller recomputes them.
  1301	    function _purchaseCostInputs(uint256 entryQuantityScaled)
  1302	        private
  1303	        view
  1304	        returns (
  1305	            bool cachedJpFlag,
  1306	            uint24 cachedLevel,
  1307	            uint256 priceWei,
  1308	            uint256 ticketCost
  1309	        )
  1310	    {
  1311	        cachedJpFlag = jackpotPhaseFlag;
  1312	        cachedLevel = level;
  1313	        priceWei = PriceLookupLib.priceForLevel(
  1314	            cachedJpFlag ? cachedLevel : cachedLevel + 1
  1315	        );
  1316	        ticketCost = (priceWei * entryQuantityScaled) / (4 * QTY_SCALE);
  1317	    }
  1318	
  1319	    function _purchaseForWith(
  1320	        address buyer,
  1321	        uint256 entryQuantityScaled,
  1322	        uint256 lootBoxAmount,
  1323	        bytes32 affiliateCode,
  1324	        MintPaymentKind payKind,
  1325	        uint256 ethValue
  1326	    ) private {
  1327	        (
  1328	            bool cachedJpFlag,
  1329	            uint24 cachedLevel,
  1330	            uint256 priceWei,
  1331	            uint256 ticketCost
  1332	        ) = _purchaseCostInputs(entryQuantityScaled);
  1333	        _purchaseForWithCached(
  1334	            buyer,
  1335	            entryQuantityScaled,
  1336	            lootBoxAmount,
  1337	            affiliateCode,
  1338	            payKind,
  1339	            ethValue,
  1340	            cachedJpFlag,
  1341	            cachedLevel,
  1342	            priceWei,
  1343	            ticketCost
  1344	        );
  1345	    }
  1346	
  1347	    /// @dev Core purchase body. `cachedJpFlag`/`cachedLevel`/`priceWei`/`ticketCost` are the
  1348	    ///      caller's same-frame _purchaseCostInputs snapshot (no external call sits between
  1349	    ///      that read and this frame, so the values cannot have changed).
  1350	    function _purchaseForWithCached(
  1351	        address buyer,
  1352	        uint256 entryQuantityScaled,
  1353	        uint256 lootBoxAmount,
  1354	        bytes32 affiliateCode,
  1355	        MintPaymentKind payKind,
  1356	        uint256 ethValue,
  1357	        bool cachedJpFlag,
  1358	        uint24 cachedLevel,
  1359	        uint256 priceWei,
  1360	        uint256 ticketCost
  1361	    ) private {
  1362	        if (_livenessTriggered()) revert E();
  1363	        uint256 lootboxFlipCredit;
  1364	
  1365	        if (lootBoxAmount != 0 && lootBoxAmount < LOOTBOX_MIN) revert E();
  1900	        // Fresh ETH the clamp-to-50 left unused is credited to the payer's afking, not
  1901	        // sent back via a value call (no reentrancy surface, consistent with overpay).
  1902	        _creditAfkingValue(msg.sender, refund);
  1903	    }
  1904	
  1905	    /// @dev Bubble up revert reason from delegatecall failure.
  1906	    ///      Uses assembly to preserve original error data.
  1907	    /// @param reason The error bytes from failed delegatecall.
  1908	    function _revertDelegate(bytes memory reason) private pure {
  1909	        if (reason.length == 0) revert E();
  1910	        assembly ("memory-safe") {
  1911	            revert(add(32, reason), mload(reason))
  1912	        }
  1913	    }
  1914	
  1915	    /// @dev Execute ticket purchase: payment, boost, affiliate routing, quest unit accumulation.
  1916	    ///      x00 century bonus and ticket queuing are handled by _purchaseFor after score computation.
  1917	    /// @return bonusCredit Bulk/recycle bonus flip credit (affiliate kickback is added by the caller)
  1918	    /// @return adjustedQty32 Adjusted ticket quantity (with boost, without x00 bonus)
  1919	    /// @return targetLevel The level tickets are queued to
  1920	    /// @return flipMintUnits FLIP-paid mint quest units
  1921	    /// @return ticketFreshFlip Ticket fresh-rate FLIP basis for the caller's combined affiliate call
  1922	    /// @return ticketRecycledFlip Ticket recycled-rate FLIP basis for the caller's combined affiliate call
  1923	    function _callTicketPurchase(
  1924	        address buyer,
  1925	        uint256 quantity,
  1926	        MintPaymentKind payKind,
  1927	        bool payInCoin,
  1928	        bytes32 affiliateCode,
  1929	        uint256 value,
  1930	        uint24 cachedLevel,
  1931	        bool cachedJpFlag
  1932	    )
  1933	        private
  1934	        returns (
  1935	            uint256 bonusCredit,
  1936	            uint32 adjustedQty32,
  1937	            uint24 targetLevel,
  1938	            uint32 flipMintUnits,
  1939	            uint256 ticketFreshFlip,
  1940	            uint256 ticketRecycledFlip,
  1941	            uint256 ticketNextShare,
  1942	            uint256 ticketFutureShare,
  1943	            uint256 ticketClaimableDraw
  1944	        )
  1945	    {
  1946	        if (quantity == 0) revert E();
  1947	        // Liveness is gated by both callers (_purchaseForWithCached / _redeemFlipFor)
  1948	        // before any state is touched, so it is not re-evaluated here.
  1949	        // compressedJackpotFlag / jackpotCounter are consumed only on jackpot-phase
  1950	        // buys (every use below is short-circuit-gated on cachedJpFlag), so the
  1951	        // slot-0 reads are skipped during the purchase phase. nextStep mirrors the
  1952	        // JackpotModule step size for the level's remaining daily jackpots.
  1953	        uint8 cachedComp;
  1954	        uint8 cachedCnt;
  1955	        uint8 nextStep = 1;
  1956	        if (cachedJpFlag) {
  1957	            cachedComp = compressedJackpotFlag;
  1958	            cachedCnt = jackpotCounter;
  1959	            if (
  1960	                cachedComp == 1 &&
  1961	                cachedCnt > 0 &&
  1962	                cachedCnt < JACKPOT_LEVEL_CAP - 1
  1963	            ) {
  1964	                nextStep = 2;
  1965	            }
  1966	        }
  1967	        targetLevel = cachedJpFlag ? cachedLevel : cachedLevel + 1;
  1968	        // Last jackpot day fix: route to level+1 to prevent stranded tickets
  1969	        // (_endPhase breaks before _unlockRng, so no future daily draw at this level)
  1970	        if (cachedJpFlag && rngLockedFlag) {
  1971	            uint8 step = cachedComp == 2 ? JACKPOT_LEVEL_CAP : nextStep;
  1972	            if (cachedCnt + step >= JACKPOT_LEVEL_CAP)
  1973	                targetLevel = cachedLevel + 1;
  1974	        }
  1975	        uint256 priceWei = PriceLookupLib.priceForLevel(targetLevel);
  1976	        uint256 costWei = (priceWei * quantity) / (4 * QTY_SCALE);
  1977	        if (costWei == 0) revert E();
  1978	        if (costWei < TICKET_MIN_BUYIN_WEI) revert E();
  1979	
  1980	        uint256 adjustedQuantity = quantity;
  1981	        if (!payInCoin) {
  1982	            // Nested delegatecall straight into the boon module (this frame already
  1983	            // runs in the Game's storage context), skipping the external self-call
  1984	            // round trip through the Game dispatcher.
  1985	            (bool boostOk, bytes memory boostData) = ContractAddresses
  1986	                .GAME_BOON_MODULE
  1987	                .delegatecall(
  1988	                    abi.encodeWithSelector(
  1989	                        IDegenerusGameBoonModule.consumePurchaseBoost.selector,
  1990	                        buyer
  1991	                    )
  1992	                );
  1993	            if (!boostOk) _revertDelegate(boostData);
  1994	            uint16 boostBps = abi.decode(boostData, (uint16));
  1995	            if (boostBps != 0) {
  1996	                uint256 cappedValue = costWei > LOOTBOX_BOOST_MAX_VALUE
  1997	                    ? LOOTBOX_BOOST_MAX_VALUE
  1998	                    : costWei;
  1999	                uint256 cappedQty = priceWei == 0
  2000	                    ? 0
  2001	                    : ((cappedValue * 4 * QTY_SCALE) / priceWei);
  2002	                adjustedQuantity += (cappedQty * boostBps) / 10_000;
  2003	            }
  2004	        }
  2005	        adjustedQty32 = uint32(adjustedQuantity);
