     1	// SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-only
     2	pragma solidity 0.8.34;
     3	
     4	import {IDegenerusGame, MintPaymentKind} from "../interfaces/IDegenerusGame.sol";
     5	import {DegenerusGameStorage} from "../storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol";
     6	import {ContractAddresses} from "../ContractAddresses.sol";
     7	
     8	/// @dev Minimal stETH interface (ERC20 subset)
     9	interface IStETH {
    10	    /// @param account Address to query balance of.
    11	    function balanceOf(address account) external view returns (uint256);
    12	    /// @param to Recipient address.
    13	    /// @param amount Transfer amount in wei.
    14	    function transfer(address to, uint256 amount) external returns (bool);
    15	}
    16	
    17	/// @dev Admin interface for VRF shutdown during final sweep.
    18	interface IDegenerusAdminShutdown {
    19	    function shutdownVrf() external;
    20	}
    21	
    22	/// @dev GNRUS interface for gameover GNRUS cleanup.
    23	interface IGNRUSGameOver {
    24	    function burnAtGameOver() external;
    25	    function onFinalSweep() external;
    26	}
    27	
    28	interface IFlipTombstone {
    29	    function tombstoneAtGameOver() external;
    30	}
    31	
    32	/**
    33	 * @title DegenerusGameGameOverModule
    34	 * @notice Handles game over logic including jackpot distribution and final sweeps.
    35	 * @dev Executed via delegatecall from DegenerusGame. Inherits storage layout.
    36	 */
    37	contract DegenerusGameGameOverModule is DegenerusGameStorage {
    38	    /// @notice stETH token contract for liquid staking rewards
    39	    IStETH private constant steth = IStETH(ContractAddresses.STETH_TOKEN);
    40	
    41	    /// @notice Admin contract for VRF shutdown
    42	    IDegenerusAdminShutdown private constant admin =
    43	        IDegenerusAdminShutdown(ContractAddresses.ADMIN);
    44	
    45	    /// @notice GNRUS contract for gameover cleanup
    46	    IGNRUSGameOver private constant charityGameOver =
    47	        IGNRUSGameOver(ContractAddresses.GNRUS);
    48	
    49	    /// @notice FLIP coin contract for the gameover worthless-token tombstone flood
    50	    IFlipTombstone private constant flip =
    51	        IFlipTombstone(ContractAddresses.COIN);
    52	
    53	    /// @notice Fixed refund amount per deity pass for early game over (levels 0-9)
    54	    uint256 private constant DEITY_PASS_EARLY_GAMEOVER_REFUND =
    55	        20 ether;
    56	
    57	    // error E() — inherited from DegenerusGameStorage
    58	
    59	    /// @notice Process game over by distributing remaining funds via jackpots.
    60	    /// @dev Called when liveness guards trigger (1yr deploy timeout or 120-day inactivity).
    61	    ///      Sets terminal gameOver flag.
    62	    ///
    63	    ///      Distribution logic:
    64	    ///      - If game ended early (levels 0-9): Fixed 20 ETH refund per deity pass purchased,
    65	    ///        FIFO by purchase order, budget-capped to available funds minus claimablePool
    66	    ///      - Remaining funds: 10% to Decimator, 90% to next-level ticketholders
    67	    ///      - Decimator refunds flow to terminal jackpot pool
    68	    ///      - Any uncredited remainder swept to vault and sDGNRS
    69	    ///
    70	    ///      Reads rngWordByDay[day] for entropy; reverts if funds exist but word is not yet available.
    71	    ///      VRF fallback logic (historical word, stall timeout) is in AdvanceModule._gameOverEntropy.
    72	    /// @param day Day index for RNG word lookup from rngWordByDay mapping.
    73	    /// @custom:reverts Invariant When distributable funds exist but the RNG word is unavailable (defense-in-depth).
    74	    /// @custom:reverts TransferFailed When an stETH or ETH transfer fails.
    75	    function handleGameOverDrain(uint24 day) external {
    76	        if (_goRead(GO_JACKPOT_PAID_SHIFT, GO_JACKPOT_PAID_MASK) != 0) return; // Already processed
    77	
    78	        uint24 lvl = level;
    79	
    80	        uint256 totalFunds = address(this).balance + steth.balanceOf(address(this));
    81	
    82	        // Compute available funds FIRST (before any side effects)
    83	        // Deity pass refunds have not happened yet, so claimablePool is pre-refund.
    84	        // sDGNRS redemption ETH is segregated out of the game at submit (pullRedemptionReserve
    85	        // transfers it to the sDGNRS contract), so it is no longer part of totalFunds here —
    86	        // subtracting pendingRedemptionEthValue would double-count it.
    87	        uint256 reserved = uint256(claimablePool);
    88	        uint256 preRefundAvailable = totalFunds > reserved ? totalFunds - reserved : 0;
    89	
    90	        // RNG gate: when distributable funds exist, require RNG word.
    91	        // Defense-in-depth -- caller (_handleGameOverPath) already guarantees
    92	        // rngWordByDay[day] != 0 before calling, so this revert should never fire.
    93	        uint256 rngWord;
    94	        if (preRefundAvailable != 0) {
    95	            rngWord = rngWordByDay[day];
    96	            if (rngWord == 0) revert Invariant();
    97	        }
    98	
    99	        // === All side effects below this line (RNG confirmed or no funds to distribute) ===
   100	
   101	        // Deity pass refunds (levels 0-9): refund each owner what they paid, capped at the flat
   102	        // DEITY_PASS_EARLY_GAMEOVER_REFUND so a boon-discounted deity (paid < 20 ETH) never refunds
   103	        // more than it paid, then clamped to the remaining distributable budget (FIFO).
   104	        if (lvl < 10) {
   105	            uint256 ownerCount = deityPassOwners.length;
   106	            uint256 budget = preRefundAvailable;
   107	            uint256 totalRefunded;
   108	            for (uint256 i; i < ownerCount; ) {
   109	                address owner = deityPassOwners[i];
   110	                uint256 refund = deityPassPricePaid[owner];
   111	                if (refund != 0) {
   112	                    if (refund > DEITY_PASS_EARLY_GAMEOVER_REFUND) {
   113	                        refund = DEITY_PASS_EARLY_GAMEOVER_REFUND;
   114	                    }
   115	                    if (refund > budget) {
   116	                        refund = budget;
   117	                    }
   118	                    if (refund != 0) {
   119	                        _creditClaimable(owner, refund);
   120	                        unchecked {
   121	                            totalRefunded += refund;
   122	                            budget -= refund;
   123	                        }
   124	                    }
   125	                    if (budget == 0) break;
   126	                }
   127	                unchecked {
   128	                    ++i;
   129	                }
   130	            }
   131	            if (totalRefunded != 0) {
   132	                claimablePool += uint128(totalRefunded); // Safe: totalRefunded bounded by preRefundAvailable which fits uint128
   133	            }
   134	        }
   135	
   136	        // Latch terminal state
   137	        gameOver = true;
   138	        _goWrite(GO_TIME_SHIFT, GO_TIME_MASK, uint48(block.timestamp));
   139	
   140	        // Burn unallocated tokens
   141	        charityGameOver.burnAtGameOver();
   142	        dgnrs.burnAtGameOver();
   143	        // Flood FLIP's VAULT mint allowance as a one-shot worthless-token tombstone
   144	        flip.tombstoneAtGameOver();
   145	
   146	        _goWrite(GO_JACKPOT_PAID_SHIFT, GO_JACKPOT_PAID_MASK, 1);
   147	        _setNextPrizePool(0);
   148	        _setFuturePrizePool(0);
   149	        _setCurrentPrizePool(0);
   150	        yieldAccumulator = 0;
   151	        // Terminal state also clears the freeze: with the live pools drained, _unlockRng's
   152	        // _unfreezePool must not resurrect the pending pool back into them, and no
   153	        // post-gameOver box/Degenerette resolution may draw ETH from a phantom pending pool.
   154	        prizePoolPendingPacked = 0;
   155	        prizePoolFrozen = false;
   156	        // All three pools are drained to zero at game over. Emit the terminal snapshot here —
   157	        // before the available==0 early return below — so every game-over path logs it once. The
   158	        // daily snapshot in _unlockRng skips game-over (gameOver is set above) to avoid a duplicate.
   159	        emit PrizePoolDailySnapshot(
   160	            0,
   161	            0,
   162	            0,
   163	            claimablePool,
   164	            address(this).balance + steth.balanceOf(address(this)),
   165	            yieldAccumulator
   166	        );
   167	
   168	        // Recalculate available after refunds (claimablePool may have grown).
   169	        // sDGNRS redemption ETH was already segregated out of the game at submit, so it is not
   170	        // part of totalFunds here — only claimablePool is reserved.
   171	        uint256 postRefundReserved = uint256(claimablePool);
   172	        uint256 available = totalFunds > postRefundReserved ? totalFunds - postRefundReserved : 0;
   173	
   174	        if (available == 0) return;
   175	
   176	        emit GameOverDrained(lvl, available, claimablePool);
   177	
   178	        // remaining tracks unallocated funds.
   179	        uint256 remaining = available;
   180	
   181	        // 10% Terminal Decimator (death bet) -- refunds flow back to remaining for terminal jackpot
   182	        uint256 decPool = remaining / 10;
   183	        if (decPool != 0) {
   184	            uint256 decRefund = IDegenerusGame(address(this)).runTerminalDecimatorJackpot(decPool, lvl, rngWord);
   185	            uint256 decSpend = decPool - decRefund;
   186	            if (decSpend != 0) {
   187	                claimablePool += uint128(decSpend); // Safe: decSpend bounded by decPool which is a fraction of available
   188	            }
   189	            remaining -= decPool;
   190	            remaining += decRefund;
   191	        }
   192	
   193	        // 90% (+ decimator refund) to next-level ticketholders (Day-5-style bucket distribution).
   194	        // gameOver=true prevents auto-rebuy inside _addClaimableEth (tickets worthless post-game).
   195	        // Any leftover from empty trait buckets stays in the contract until handleFinalSweep
   196	        // (30 days later) folds it into the three-way split to vault / sDGNRS / GNRUS.
   197	        if (remaining != 0) {
   198	            IDegenerusGame(address(this)).runTerminalJackpot(remaining, lvl + 1, rngWord);
   199	        }
   200	    }
   201	
   202	    /// @notice Final sweep of all remaining funds after 30 days post-gameover.
   203	    /// @dev Pays each sink (vault, sDGNRS, GNRUS) the claimable balance still
   204	    ///      owed to it, then splits the remainder ~1/3 each (GNRUS absorbs the
   205	    ///      rounding wei). All other unclaimed player balances are forfeited.
   206	    ///      After GO_SWEPT=1, claimWinnings() reverts, so this is the last
   207	    ///      chance for the three sinks to receive what they earned in-game.
   208	    ///      Also shuts down the VRF subscription and sweeps LINK to vault.
   209	    /// @custom:reverts TransferFailed When ETH or stETH transfer fails
   210	    function handleFinalSweep() external {
   211	        uint256 goTime = _goRead(GO_TIME_SHIFT, GO_TIME_MASK);
   212	        if (goTime == 0) return; // Game not over yet
   213	        if (block.timestamp < goTime + 30 days) return; // Too early
   214	        if (_goRead(GO_SWEPT_SHIFT, GO_SWEPT_MASK) != 0) return; // Already swept
   215	
   216	        _goWrite(GO_SWEPT_SHIFT, GO_SWEPT_MASK, 1);
   217	        charityGameOver.onFinalSweep(); // stamp GNRUS with the sweep time (anchors its recovery gates)
   218	
   219	        uint256 owedV  = _claimableOf(ContractAddresses.VAULT);
   220	        uint256 owedSD = _claimableOf(ContractAddresses.SDGNRS);
   221	        uint256 owedG  = _claimableOf(ContractAddresses.GNRUS);
   222	        _debitClaimable(ContractAddresses.VAULT, owedV);
   223	        _debitClaimable(ContractAddresses.SDGNRS, owedSD);
   224	        _debitClaimable(ContractAddresses.GNRUS, owedG);
   225	        if (owedV  != 0) emit ClaimableSpent(ContractAddresses.VAULT,  owedV,  0, MintPaymentKind.Internal, owedV);
   226	        if (owedSD != 0) emit ClaimableSpent(ContractAddresses.SDGNRS, owedSD, 0, MintPaymentKind.Internal, owedSD);
   227	        if (owedG  != 0) emit ClaimableSpent(ContractAddresses.GNRUS,  owedG,  0, MintPaymentKind.Internal, owedG);
   228	        claimablePool = 0;
   229	
   230	        // Shutdown VRF subscription (fire-and-forget; failure must not block sweep)
   231	        try admin.shutdownVrf() {} catch {}
   232	
   233	        uint256 ethBal = address(this).balance;
   234	        uint256 stBal = steth.balanceOf(address(this));
   235	        uint256 totalFunds = ethBal + stBal;
   236	
   237	        emit FinalSwept(totalFunds);
   238	
   239	        if (totalFunds == 0) return;
   240	
   241	        // Invariant claimablePool >= sum(claimableWinnings[*]) and balance >=
   242	        // claimablePool guarantee totalFunds >= owedV+owedSD+owedG; if the
   243	        // invariant ever fails, _sendStethFirst reverts (hard-revert policy).
   244	        uint256 remainder  = totalFunds - (owedV + owedSD + owedG);
   245	        uint256 thirdShare = remainder / 3;
   246	        uint256 gnrusExtra = remainder - thirdShare - thirdShare;
   247	
   248	        stBal = _sendStethFirst(ContractAddresses.VAULT,  owedV  + thirdShare, stBal);
   249	        stBal = _sendStethFirst(ContractAddresses.SDGNRS, owedSD + thirdShare, stBal);
   250	        _sendStethFirst(ContractAddresses.GNRUS,          owedG  + gnrusExtra, stBal);
   251	    }
   252	
   253	    /// @dev Send stETH first to a recipient, then ETH for the remainder. Returns updated stETH balance.
   254	    ///      IMPORTANT: Hard-reverts on stETH/ETH transfer failure. Because game-over
   255	    ///      sets terminal state flags that roll back on revert, a stuck stETH transfer
   256	    ///      would block game-over processing until the transfer succeeds.
   257	    /// @param to Recipient address.
   258	    /// @param amount Total amount to send (stETH preferred, ETH as fallback).
   259	    /// @param stethBal Remaining stETH balance available for transfers.
   260	    /// @return Updated stETH balance after transfer.
     1	// SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-only
     2	pragma solidity 0.8.34;
     3	
     4	import {IStETH} from "../interfaces/IStETH.sol";
     5	import {DegenerusGamePayoutUtils} from "./DegenerusGamePayoutUtils.sol";
     6	import {ContractAddresses} from "../ContractAddresses.sol";
     7	import {EntropyLib} from "../libraries/EntropyLib.sol";
     8	import {PriceLookupLib} from "../libraries/PriceLookupLib.sol";
     9	import {JackpotBucketLib} from "../libraries/JackpotBucketLib.sol";
    10	import {IDegenerusJackpots} from "../interfaces/IDegenerusJackpots.sol";
    11	
    12	/**
    13	 * @title DegenerusGameJackpotModule
    14	 * @author Burnie Degenerus
    15	 * @notice Delegate-called module that hosts the jackpot distribution logic for `DegenerusGame`.
    16	 *
    17	 * @dev ARCHITECTURE NOTES:
    18	 *      - This contract is ONLY meant to be invoked via `delegatecall` from the main game contract.
    19	 *      - Storage layout inherits from `DegenerusGameStorage` to ensure slot alignment with the parent.
    20	 *      - All external functions lack access modifiers intentionally; the parent contract controls access.
    21	 *      - DO NOT deploy this contract standalone or call it directly—state would be written to the
    22	 *        module's own storage rather than the game's.
    23	 *
    24	 *      JACKPOT FLOW OVERVIEW:
    25	 *      1. Pool consolidation at level transition (prize pool splits and merges).
    26	 *      2. `payDailyJackpot` — Handles purchase phase jackpots and rolling dailies at EOL.
    27	 *      3. `payDailyFlipJackpot` — FLIP jackpot distribution to near-future ticket holders.
    28	 *
    29	 *      FUND ACCOUNTING:
    30	 *      - ETH flows through `futurePrizePool` (unified reserve), `currentPrizePool`,
    31	 *        `nextPrizePool`, `claimablePool`.
    32	 *      - The remainder goes to the entropy-selected solo bucket.
    33	 *      - `claimableWinnings` tracks per-player ETH; `claimablePool` is the aggregate liability.
    34	 *
    35	 *      RANDOMNESS:
    36	 *      - All entropy originates from VRF words passed by the parent contract.
    37	 *      - EntropyLib.hash2 provides full-diffusion keccak derivation for sub-selections.
    38	 *      - Winner selection intentionally allows duplicates (more tickets = more chances).
    39	 */
    40	contract DegenerusGameJackpotModule is DegenerusGamePayoutUtils {
    41	    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    42	    // Errors
    43	    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    44	
    45	    error OnlyGame();
    46	
    47	    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    48	    // Events
    49	    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    50	
    51	    /// @dev Emitted when a far-future ticket holder (5-99 levels ahead) wins the daily FLIP jackpot.
    52	    ///      These winners are drawn from ticketQueue (traits not yet assigned).
    53	    event FarFutureFlipJackpotWinner(
    54	        address indexed winner,
    55	        uint24 indexed currentLevel,
    56	        uint24 indexed winnerLevel,
    57	        uint256 amount
    58	    );
    59	
    60	    /// @dev ETH jackpot win.
    61	    ///      traitId is uint16: values 0-255 are real trait IDs; values ≥256 are
    62	    ///      sentinels for non-trait sources (e.g. BAF_TRAIT_SENTINEL = 420).
    63	    event JackpotEthWin(
    64	        address indexed winner,
    65	        uint24 indexed level,
    66	        uint16 indexed traitId,
    67	        uint256 amount,
    68	        uint256 entryIndex
    69	    );
    70	
    71	    /// @dev Ticket jackpot win. See JackpotEthWin for traitId sentinel semantics.
    72	    ///      entryCount is an entries count on all 3 paths and matches the
    73	    ///      entries queued by the adjacent _queueEntries call. roundedUp is
    74	    ///      true iff the BAF _jackpotTicketRoll (traitId = BAF_TRAIT_SENTINEL)
    75	    ///      Bernoulli sub-roll incremented the whole-ticket count; it is false
    76	    ///      on the two trait-matched paths, which have a zero fractional part
    77	    ///      by construction.
    78	    event JackpotTicketWin(
    79	        address indexed winner,
    80	        uint24 indexed entryLevel,
    81	        uint16 indexed traitId,
    82	        uint32 entryCount,
    83	        uint24 sourceLevel,
    84	        uint256 entryIndex,
    85	        bool roundedUp
    86	    );
    87	
    88	    /// @dev FLIP coin win (near-future, trait-matched).
    89	    event JackpotFlipWin(
    90	        address indexed winner,
    91	        uint24 indexed level,
    92	        uint8 indexed traitId,
    93	        uint256 amount,
    94	        uint256 entryIndex
    95	    );
    96	
    97	    /// @dev Emitted once per daily drawing with both main and bonus winning traits.
    98	    event DailyWinningTraits(
    99	        uint24 indexed day,
   100	        uint32 mainTraitsPacked,
   101	        uint32 bonusTraitsPacked,
   102	        uint24 bonusTargetLevel
   103	    );
   104	
   105	    /// @dev Whale pass awarded to solo bucket winner.
   106	    event JackpotWhalePassWin(
   107	        address indexed winner,
   108	        uint24 indexed level,
   109	        uint256 halfPassCount
   110	    );
   111	
   112	    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   113	    // External Contract References (compile-time constants)
   114	    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   115	
   116	    IStETH internal constant steth = IStETH(ContractAddresses.STETH_TOKEN);
   117	    IDegenerusJackpots internal constant jackpots =
   118	        IDegenerusJackpots(ContractAddresses.JACKPOTS);
   119	
   120	    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   121	    // Constants — Timing & Thresholds
   122	    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   123	
   124	    /// @dev Maximum number of daily jackpots per level before forcing level transition.
   125	    uint8 private constant JACKPOT_LEVEL_CAP = 5;
   126	
   127	    uint256 private constant SMALL_LOOTBOX_THRESHOLD = 0.5 ether;
   128	
   129	    /// @dev Sentinel traitId stamped on BAF jackpot payout events so indexers can
   130	    ///      distinguish BAF wins from trait-bucketed daily/coin wins. Sits above
   131	    ///      uint8.max (255) so it never collides with a real trait id.
   132	    uint16 private constant BAF_TRAIT_SENTINEL = 420;
   133	
   134	    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   135	    // Constants — Share Distribution (Basis Points)
   136	    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   137	
   138	    /// @dev Day-5 trait bucket shares packed into 64 bits: [6000, 1333, 1333, 1334] = 10000 bps.
   139	    ///      With rotation, the 60% share is assigned to the solo (1-winner) bucket.
   140	    uint64 private constant FINAL_DAY_SHARES_PACKED =
   141	        (uint64(6000)) |
   142	            (uint64(1333) << 16) |
   143	            (uint64(1333) << 32) |
   144	            (uint64(1334) << 48);
   145	
   146	    /// @dev Daily jackpot trait bucket shares: 2000 bps each × 4 = 8000 bps.
   147	    ///      Remaining 20% is assigned to the entropy-selected solo bucket.
   148	    uint64 private constant DAILY_JACKPOT_SHARES_PACKED =
   149	        uint64(2000) * 0x0001000100010001;
   150	
   151	    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   152	    // Constants — Entropy Salts
   153	    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   154	
   155	    /// @dev Domain separator for coin jackpot entropy derivation.
   156	    bytes32 private constant FLIP_JACKPOT_TAG = keccak256("coin-jackpot");
   157	
   158	    /// @dev Domain separator for per-pull level sampling in the daily coin jackpot.
   159	    ///      Distinct from FLIP_JACKPOT_TAG so (randomWord, FLIP_JACKPOT_TAG, ·) and
   160	    ///      (randomWord, FLIP_LEVEL_TAG, ·) keccaks cannot collide.
   161	    bytes32 private constant FLIP_LEVEL_TAG = keccak256("coin-level");
   162	
   163	    /// @dev Domain separator for rolling current-pool daily jackpot percentage.
   164	    bytes32 private constant DAILY_CURRENT_BPS_TAG =
   165	        keccak256("daily-current-bps");
   166	
   167	    /// @dev Domain separator for selecting daily carryover source level.
   168	    bytes32 private constant DAILY_CARRYOVER_SOURCE_TAG =
   169	        keccak256("daily-carryover-source");
   170	
   171	    /// @dev Domain separator for bonus trait derivation from same VRF word.
   172	    bytes32 private constant BONUS_TRAITS_TAG = keccak256("BONUS_TRAITS");
   173	
   174	    /// @dev Sentinel `excludeIdx` for `_rollHeroSymbol` meaning "no slot excluded":
   175	    ///      any value >= 32 matches no real `(quadrant << 3) | symbol` slot, so the
   176	    ///      roll runs over the full wager pool. The bonus draw instead passes the
   177	    ///      main hero's packed slot to force a distinct hero.
   178	    uint8 private constant _NO_HERO_EXCLUDE = 0xFF;
   179	
   180	    /// @dev Max forward offset for carryover source selection (lvl+1..lvl+4).
   181	    uint8 private constant DAILY_CARRYOVER_MAX_OFFSET = 4;
   182	
   183	    /// @dev Current-pool daily jackpot percentage bounds for days 1-4 (6%-14%).
   184	    uint16 private constant DAILY_CURRENT_BPS_MIN = 600;
   185	    uint16 private constant DAILY_CURRENT_BPS_MAX = 1400;
   186	
   187	    /// @dev Portion of purchase-phase reward-pool jackpots converted to loot boxes (3/4).
   188	    uint16 private constant PURCHASE_REWARD_JACKPOT_LOOTBOX_BPS = 7500;
   189	
   190	    /// @dev Max winners per single trait bucket (must fit in uint8 for _randTraitTicket).
   191	    ///      Set to 250 to allow all ticket winners in single trait if others are empty.
   192	    uint8 private constant MAX_BUCKET_WINNERS = 250;
   193	
   194	    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   195	    // Constants — Jackpot Bucket Scaling (Gas Guardrails)
   196	    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   197	
   198	    /// @dev Maximum ticket winners for purchase phase lootbox distribution.
   199	    /// Higher than ETH winners because ticket distribution is cheaper per winner.
   200	    uint16 private constant PURCHASE_PHASE_TICKET_MAX_WINNERS = 120;
   201	
   202	    /// @dev Maximum total ETH winners across daily + carryover jackpots.
   203	    ///      At max scale (6.36x, 200+ ETH pool): 159 + 95 + 50 + 1 = 305.
   204	    uint16 private constant DAILY_ETH_MAX_WINNERS = 305;
   205	
   206	    /// @dev Maximum winners for daily coin jackpot (coinflip.creditFlip is 1 external call each).
   207	    uint16 private constant DAILY_COIN_MAX_WINNERS = 50;
   208	
   209	    /// @dev Share of daily FLIP budget awarded to far-future ticket holders (25%).
   210	    uint16 private constant FAR_FUTURE_FLIP_BPS = 2500;
   211	
   212	    /// @dev Number of far-future levels to sample for FLIP jackpot (10 winners max).
   213	    uint8 private constant FAR_FUTURE_FLIP_SAMPLES = 10;
   214	
   215	    /// @dev Domain separator for far-future coin jackpot entropy derivation.
   216	    bytes32 private constant FAR_FUTURE_FLIP_TAG = keccak256("far-future-coin");
   217	
   218	    /// @dev Maximum winners for lootbox jackpot distributions (gas safety).
   219	    ///      Lower than the daily ETH winner ceiling because lootboxes do multiple rolls per winner.
   220	    uint16 private constant LOOTBOX_MAX_WINNERS = 100;
   221	
   222	    /// @dev Daily jackpot max scale (6.36x) producing bucket counts 159/95/50/1 at 200+ ETH.
   223	    ///      All 305 winners (159 + 95 + 50 + 1) are paid in a single call.
   224	    uint32 private constant DAILY_JACKPOT_SCALE_MAX_BPS = 63_600;
   225	
   226	    // =========================================================================
   227	    // External Entry Points (delegatecall targets)
   228	    // =========================================================================
   229	
   230	    /// @notice Terminal jackpot for x00 levels: Day-5-style bucket distribution.
   231	    /// @dev Called via IDegenerusGame(address(this)) from GameOverModule.
   232	    ///      Uses FINAL_DAY_SHARES_PACKED (60/13/13/13) with trait-based bucket distribution.
   233	    ///      Updates claimablePool internally — callers must NOT double-count.
   234	    /// @param poolWei Total ETH to distribute.
   235	    /// @param targetLvl Level to sample winners from (typically lvl+1).
   236	    /// @param rngWord VRF entropy seed.
   237	    /// @return paidWei Total ETH distributed (callers deduct from source pool).
   238	    function runTerminalJackpot(
   239	        uint256 poolWei,
   240	        uint24 targetLvl,
   241	        uint256 rngWord
   242	    ) external returns (uint256 paidWei) {
   243	        if (msg.sender != ContractAddresses.GAME) revert OnlyGame();
   244	
   245	        uint32 winningTraitsPacked = _rollWinningTraits(rngWord, false);
   246	        uint8[4] memory traitIds = JackpotBucketLib.unpackWinningTraits(
   247	            winningTraitsPacked
   248	        );
   249	        uint256 effectiveEntropy = _soloAdjustedEntropy(
   250	            traitIds,
   251	            EntropyLib.hash2(rngWord, targetLvl)
   252	        );
   253	
   254	        uint16[4] memory bucketCounts = JackpotBucketLib.bucketCountsForPoolCap(
   255	            poolWei,
   256	            effectiveEntropy,
   257	            DAILY_ETH_MAX_WINNERS,
   258	            DAILY_JACKPOT_SCALE_MAX_BPS
   259	        );
   260	        uint16[4] memory shareBps = JackpotBucketLib.shareBpsByBucket(
   261	            FINAL_DAY_SHARES_PACKED,
   262	            uint8(effectiveEntropy & 3)
   263	        );
   264	
   265	        paidWei = _processDailyEth(
   266	            targetLvl,
   267	            poolWei,
   268	            effectiveEntropy,
   269	            traitIds,
   270	            shareBps,
   271	            bucketCounts,
   272	            false // not jackpot phase
   273	        );
   274	    }
   275	
   276	    /// @notice Pays purchase phase jackpots OR rolling daily jackpots at level end.
   277	    /// @dev Called by the parent game contract via delegatecall. Two distinct paths:
   278	    ///
   279	    ///      JACKPOT PHASE PATH (isJackpotPhase=true):
   280	    ///      - Day 1-4: Distributes a random 6%-14% slice of remaining currentPrizePool.
   281	    ///      - Day 5: Distributes 100% of remaining currentPrizePool.
   282	    ///      - Day 1 also runs the early-bird lootbox jackpot (from futurePrizePool).
   283	    ///      - On day 2-4, takes 0.5% of futurePrizePool and buys current-level tickets
   284	    ///        for winners from a random source level in [lvl+1, lvl+4], deposited into nextPool.
   285	    ///      - Increments jackpotCounter on completion.
   286	    ///
   287	    ///      PURCHASE PHASE PATH (isJackpotPhase=false):
   288	    ///      - Triggered during purchase phase when burns occur.
   289	    ///      - Rolls winning traits (random + hero override) and runs trait-based jackpot.
   290	    ///      - Fixed winner counts [20, 12, 6, 1] = 39 ETH winners, up to 120 ticket winners.
   291	    ///      - Adds a 1% futurePrizePool ETH slice every purchase day with 75%
   292	    ///        converted to lootbox tickets and remainder distributed as ETH.
   293	    ///
   294	    /// @param isJackpotPhase True for jackpot phase dailies, false for purchase phase jackpot.
   295	    /// @param lvl Current game level.
   296	    /// @param randWord VRF entropy for winner selection and trait derivation.
   297	    function payDailyJackpot(
   298	        bool isJackpotPhase,
   299	        uint24 lvl,
   300	        uint256 randWord
   301	    ) external {
   302	        uint24 questDay = _simulatedDayIndex();
   303	        // One hero pass serves both rolls: main and bonus traits share the same
   304	        // (quadrant, symbol) hero result within a single resolution.
   305	        (
   306	            uint32 winningTraitsPacked,
   307	            uint32 bonusTraitsPacked
   308	        ) = _rollWinningTraitsPair(randWord);
   309	
   310	        if (isJackpotPhase) {
   311	            uint256 dailyEthBudget;
   312	            uint8 counterStep = 1;
   313	            bool isFinalPhysicalDay;
   314	            uint256 curPool;
   315	            {
   316	                uint8 counter = jackpotCounter;
   317	                uint8 compressedFlag = compressedJackpotFlag;
   318	                // Turbo (flag=2): all 5 logical days in 1 physical day.
   319	                // Compressed (flag=1): 5 logical days in 3 physical days.
   320	                if (compressedFlag == 2 && counter == 0) {
   321	                    counterStep = JACKPOT_LEVEL_CAP;
   322	                } else if (
   323	                    compressedFlag == 1 &&
   324	                    counter > 0 &&
   325	                    counter < JACKPOT_LEVEL_CAP - 1
   326	                ) {
   327	                    counterStep = 2;
   328	                }
   329	                isFinalPhysicalDay = (counter + counterStep >=
   330	                    JACKPOT_LEVEL_CAP);
   331	                bool isEarlyBirdDay = (counter == 0);
   332	                curPool = _getCurrentPrizePool();
   333	                uint16 dailyBps;
   334	                if (isFinalPhysicalDay) {
   335	                    dailyBps = 10_000; // Final physical day: 100% of remaining pool
   336	                } else {
   337	                    dailyBps = _dailyCurrentPoolBps(counter, randWord);
   338	                    // Double BPS on compressed days to combine two days' payouts.
   339	                    if (counterStep == 2) {
   340	                        dailyBps *= 2;
  1180	    ///      forwards as-is — re-encoding here would cost contract-size headroom for no behavior change.
  1181	    /// @return returnAmountWei Amount to return (non-zero if no winners or already resolved).
  1182	    function runTerminalDecimatorJackpot(
  1183	        uint256,
  1184	        uint24,
  1185	        uint256
  1186	    ) external returns (uint256 returnAmountWei) {
  1187	        if (msg.sender != address(this)) revert OnlySelf();
  1188	        (bool ok, bytes memory data) = ContractAddresses
  1189	            .GAME_DECIMATOR_MODULE
  1190	            .delegatecall(msg.data);
  1191	        if (!ok) _revertDelegate(data);
  1192	        if (data.length == 0) revert EmptyReturn();
  1193	        return abi.decode(data, (uint256));
  1194	    }
  1195	
  1196	    /// @notice Terminal decimator window. Open except lastPurchaseDay, gameOver, and
  1197	    ///         level 0: the terminal claim round's zero-initialized sentinel reads a
  1198	    ///         level-0 gameover as already resolved, so a level-0 round can never pay
  1199	    ///         its burners — burns are rejected instead of accepted unwinnable.
  1200	    /// @return open True if terminal decimator burns are allowed.
  1201	    /// @return lvl Current game level.
  1202	    function terminalDecWindow() external view returns (bool open, uint24 lvl) {
  1203	        lvl = level;
  1204	        open = !gameOver && !lastPurchaseDay && lvl != 0;
  1205	    }
  1206	
  1207	    /// @notice Terminal jackpot for x00 levels: Day-5-style bucket distribution.
  1208	    /// @dev Access: Game-only (self-call). Delegatecalls to JackpotModule.
  1209	    ///      Updates claimablePool internally — callers must NOT double-count.
  1210	    ///      Signature: runTerminalJackpot(uint256 poolWei, uint24 targetLvl, uint256 rngWord) —
  1211	    ///      the total ETH to distribute, the level to sample winners from, and the VRF entropy
  1212	    ///      seed. The signature matches the module function exactly (identical selector), so the
  1213	    ///      calldata forwards as-is — re-encoding here would cost contract-size headroom for no
  1214	    ///      behavior change.
  1215	    /// @return paidWei Total ETH distributed.
  1216	    function runTerminalJackpot(
  1217	        uint256,
  1218	        uint24,
  1219	        uint256
  1220	    ) external returns (uint256 paidWei) {
  1221	        if (msg.sender != address(this)) revert OnlySelf();
  1222	        (bool ok, bytes memory data) = ContractAddresses
  1223	            .GAME_JACKPOT_MODULE
  1224	            .delegatecall(msg.data);
  1225	        if (!ok) _revertDelegate(data);
  1226	        if (data.length == 0) revert EmptyReturn();
  1227	        return abi.decode(data, (uint256));
  1228	    }
  1229	
  1230	    /// @notice Emit DailyWinningTraits via jackpot module.
  1231	    /// @dev Access: Game-only (self-call). Delegatecalls to JackpotModule.
  1232	    ///      Used at purchaseLevel==1 where payDailyJackpot is skipped.
  1233	    ///      Signature: emitDailyWinningTraits(uint24 lvl, uint256 randWord, uint24 bonusTargetLevel)
  1234	    ///      — lvl is unused (preserved for signature compatibility), then the VRF entropy seed and
  1235	    ///      the target level for the first coin distribution. The signature matches the module
  1236	    ///      function exactly (identical selector), so the calldata forwards as-is — re-encoding
  1237	    ///      here would cost contract-size headroom for no behavior change.
  1238	    function emitDailyWinningTraits(

  1025	    /// @dev Unified ETH distribution across trait buckets. All buckets are paid
  1026	    ///      in a single call (the full DAILY_ETH_MAX_WINNERS ceiling).
  1027	    ///
  1028	    ///      JACKPOT PHASE vs PURCHASE/TERMINAL:
  1029	    ///      - Jackpot phase (isJackpotPhase=true): Solo bucket gets whale pass + DGNRS on final day.
  1030	    ///      - Purchase/terminal (isJackpotPhase=false): All buckets paid uniformly.
  1031	    ///
  1032	    /// @param lvl The level whose winners are being paid.
  1033	    /// @param ethPool Total ETH to distribute.
  1034	    /// @param entropy VRF-derived random word for winner selection.

  1037	    /// @param bucketCounts Number of holders in each trait bucket.
  1038	    /// @param isJackpotPhase True during jackpot phase (solo bucket gets whale pass).
  1039	    /// @return paidEth Total ETH actually paid out in this call.
  1040	    function _processDailyEth(
  1041	        uint24 lvl,
  1042	        uint256 ethPool,
  1043	        uint256 entropy,

  1045	        uint16[4] memory shareBps,
  1046	        uint16[4] memory bucketCounts,
  1047	        bool isJackpotPhase

  1050	            return 0;
  1051	        }

  1056	            ethPool, shareBps, bucketCounts, remainderIdx, unit
  1057	        );

  1059	        uint8[4] memory order = JackpotBucketLib.bucketOrderLargestFirst(
  1060	            bucketCounts
  1061	        );

  1067	            uint8 traitIdx = order[j];

  1069	            uint16 count = bucketCounts[traitIdx];
  1070	            uint256 share = shares[traitIdx];

  1072	                unchecked {
  1073	                    ++j;
  1074	                }
  1075	                continue;
  1076	            }
  1077	
  1078	            entropyState = uint256(
  1079	                keccak256(abi.encode(entropyState, traitIdx, share))
  1080	            );

  1082	            uint256 paidDelta;
  1083	            uint256 claimDelta;

  1089	                share,
  1090	                entropyState,

  1102	        }
  1103	    }

  1408	
  1409	    /// @dev Virtual deity entry count for a trait bucket of size `len` (zero
  1410	    ///      when no deity holds the trait's symbol):
  1411	    ///        Gold tier (color == 7): flat 1 virtual entry.
  1412	    ///        Common tier (color in [0..6]): floor(2% of bucket), minimum 2.
  1413	    function _deityVirtualCount(
  1414	        uint8 trait,
  1415	        uint256 len,
  1416	        address deity
  1417	    ) private pure returns (uint256 virtualCount) {
  1418	        if (deity != address(0)) {
  1419	            if (((trait >> 3) & 7) == 7) {
  1420	                virtualCount = 1;
  1421	            } else {
  1422	                virtualCount = len / 50;
  1423	                if (virtualCount < 2) virtualCount = 2;
  1424	            }
  1425	        }
  1426	    }
  1427	
  1428	    /// @dev Selects random winners from a trait's ticket pool, returning both addresses and indices.
  1429	    ///      Reads the bucket length and deity itself; distribution paths that
  1430	    ///      already hold them use the precomputed overload directly.
  1431	    function _randTraitTicket(
  1432	        address[][256] storage lvlTraitEntry_,
  1433	        uint256 randomWord,
  1434	        uint8 trait,
  1435	        uint8 numWinners,
  1436	        uint8 salt
  1437	    )
  1438	        private
  1439	        view
  1440	        returns (address[] memory winners, uint256[] memory ticketIndexes)
  1441	    {
  1442	        uint256 len = lvlTraitEntry_[trait].length;
  1443	
  1444	        // traitId layout: (quadrant << 6) | (color << 3) | symIdx
  1445	        // fullSymId = quadrant * 8 + symIdx
  1446	        uint8 fullSymId = (trait >> 6) * 8 + (trait & 0x07);
  1447	        address deity;
  1448	        if (fullSymId < 32) {
  1449	            deity = deityBySymbol[fullSymId];
  1450	        }
  1451	
  1452	        return
  1453	            _randTraitTicket(
  1454	                lvlTraitEntry_,
  1455	                randomWord,
  1456	                trait,
  1457	                numWinners,
  1458	                salt,
  1459	                len,
  1460	                deity
  1461	            );
  1462	    }
  1463	
  1464	    /// @dev Winner-selection core with caller-supplied bucket length and deity.
  1465	    ///      Winners beyond the real bucket land on the deity's virtual entries.
  1466	    function _randTraitTicket(
  1467	        address[][256] storage lvlTraitEntry_,
  1468	        uint256 randomWord,
  1469	        uint8 trait,
  1470	        uint8 numWinners,
  1471	        uint8 salt,
  1472	        uint256 len,
  1473	        address deity
  1474	    )
  1475	        private
  1476	        view
  1477	        returns (address[] memory winners, uint256[] memory ticketIndexes)
  1478	    {
  1479	        address[] storage holders = lvlTraitEntry_[trait];
  1480	        uint256 virtualCount = _deityVirtualCount(trait, len, deity);
  1481	
  1482	        uint256 effectiveLen = len + virtualCount;
  1483	        if (effectiveLen == 0 || numWinners == 0) {
  1484	            return (new address[](0), new uint256[](0));
  1485	        }
  1486	
  1487	        winners = new address[](numWinners);
  1488	        ticketIndexes = new uint256[](numWinners);
  1489	        for (uint256 i; i < numWinners; ) {
  1490	            uint256 idx = uint256(
  1491	                keccak256(abi.encode(randomWord, trait, salt, i))
  1492	            ) % effectiveLen;
  1493	            if (idx < len) {
  1494	                winners[i] = holders[idx];
  1495	                ticketIndexes[i] = idx;
  1496	            } else {
  1497	                winners[i] = deity;
  1498	                ticketIndexes[i] = type(uint256).max;
  1499	            }
  1500	            unchecked {
  1501	                ++i;
  1502	            }
  1503	        }
  1504	    }

   175	
   176	    /// @dev Bit mask for far-future ticket key encoding.
   177	    ///      Set bit 22 of the uint24 level key to create a third key space
   178	    ///      disjoint from both double-buffer slots (bit 23).
   179	    ///      Far-future = tickets targeting > currentLevel + 5.
   180	    ///      Three key spaces: Slot0 [0x000000-0x3FFFFF], FF [0x400000-0x7FFFFF],
   181	    ///      Slot1 [0x800000-0xBFFFFF]. Disjoint for all lvl < 2^22.
   182	    uint24 internal constant TICKET_FAR_FUTURE_BIT = 1 << 22;
   183	
   184	    /// @dev Deploy idle timeout in days (mirrors DegenerusGame / AdvanceModule).
   185	    uint32 internal constant _DEPLOY_IDLE_TIMEOUT_DAYS = 365;
   186	
   187	    /// @dev VRF stall duration that flips liveness from "grace" to "VRF-dead game-over".
   188	    ///      Below this, liveness is suppressed so players can propose a coordinator rotation.
   189	    ///      At or above, liveness fires so the game-over fallback path engages.
   190	    uint48 internal constant _VRF_GRACE_PERIOD = 14 days;
   191	
   192	    /// @dev RNG-stall length (in sealed days) past which the VRF-death deadman fires regardless
   193	    ///      of phase. dailyIdx only advances on a successful day-seal, so currentDay - dailyIdx
   194	    ///      counts days since the last good word; it freezes during ANY stall (dead coordinator,
   195	    ///      unfilled LINK, request-from-zero revert) and clears only after game-over latches.
   196	    ///      Far above the in-phase clocks, so it never fires on a healthy game.
   197	    uint24 internal constant _VRF_DEADMAN_DAYS = 120;

   750	            }
   751	            // dOk=false -> swallow, fall through to handleGameOverDrain.
   752	        }
   753	
   754	        (ok, data) = ContractAddresses.GAME_GAMEOVER_MODULE.delegatecall(
   755	            abi.encodeWithSelector(
   756	                IDegenerusGameGameOverModule.handleGameOverDrain.selector,
   757	                day
   758	            )
   759	        );
   760	        if (!ok) _revertDelegate(data);
   761	        _unlockRng(day);
   762	        return (true, STAGE_GAMEOVER);

  1140	    ///      VRF callback handles finalization directly - no advanceGame needed.
  1141	    function requestLootboxRng() external {
  1142	        if (rngLockedFlag) revert RngLocked();
  1143	        // Block while mid-day ticket processing is active — prevents entropy reroll
  1144	        // by requesting a new VRF word after inspecting the current one.
  1145	        if (_lrRead(LR_MID_DAY_SHIFT, LR_MID_DAY_MASK) != 0) revert MidDayActive();
  1146	        uint48 nowTs = uint48(block.timestamp);
  1147	        uint24 currentDay = _simulatedDayIndexAt(nowTs);
  1148	
  1149	        // Block in the 15-minute pre-reset window to avoid competing with daily jackpot RNG flow.
  1150	        if ((nowTs - 82620) % 1 days >= 1 days - 15 minutes) revert PreResetWindow();
  1151	        // Block until today's daily RNG has been consumed and recorded.
  1152	        if (rngWordByDay[currentDay] == 0) revert RngNotReady();
  1153	
  1154	        if (rngRequestTime != 0) revert RngInFlight();
  1155	
  1156	        // LINK balance check
  1157	        (uint96 linkBal, , , , ) = vrfCoordinator.getSubscription(
  1158	            vrfSubscriptionId
  1159	        );
  1160	        if (linkBal < MIN_LINK_FOR_LOOTBOX_RNG) revert InsufficientLink();
  1161	
  1162	        // Threshold check: pending ETH plus the FLIP ETH-equivalent (valued at the
  1163	        // current ticket price) must clear the owner-tunable threshold. This gates
  1164	        // only the mid-day fast path — the daily advance assigns the day's word to
  1165	        // the current index regardless, so pending boxes never wait past one cycle.
  1166	        uint256 pendingEth = _unpackMilliEthToWei(
  1167	            uint64(_lrRead(LR_PENDING_ETH_SHIFT, LR_PENDING_ETH_MASK))
  1168	        );
  1169	        uint256 pendingFlip = _unpackWholeFlipToWei(
  1170	            uint40(_lrRead(LR_PENDING_FLIP_SHIFT, LR_PENDING_FLIP_MASK))
  1171	        );

  1173	        uint256 totalEthEquivalent = pendingEth;

  1183	            uint64(_lrRead(LR_THRESHOLD_SHIFT, LR_THRESHOLD_MASK))
  1184	        );

  1208	    // BIT ALLOCATION MAP for VRF random word (currentWord after _applyDailyRng):
  1209	    //
  1210	    // Bit(s)   Consumer                    Operation                         Location
  1211	    // ------   --------                    ---------                         --------

  1213	    // 0        BAF fire gate               rngWord & 1                       AdvanceModule._consolidatePoolsAndRewardJackpots
  1214	    // 8+       Redemption roll             (currentWord >> 8) % 151 + 25     AdvanceModule.rngGate
  1215	    // full     Coinflip reward percent     keccak256(rngWord, epoch) % 20    Coinflip._resolveDay

  1219	    // full     Future take variance        rngWord % (variance * 2 + 1)      AdvanceModule._takeFuturePrizePoolSlice
  1220	    // full     Prize pool consolidation    via delegatecall (full word)      JackpotModule (consolidatePrizePools)

  1223	    // NOTE: Bits 0 and 8+ are the only direct bit-level consumers.

  1227	    /// @dev Daily RNG processing gate called during advanceGame. Applies VRF word,
  1228	    ///      processes coinflip payouts, rolls daily quest, resolves pending gambling
  1229	    ///      burn redemptions, stores lootbox RNG, and handles VRF timeout retries (12h).
  1230	    function rngGate(
  1231	        uint48 ts,
  1232	        uint24 day,
  1233	        uint24 lvl,
  1234	        bool isTicketJackpotDay,
  1235	        uint8 coinflipBonus
  1236	    ) internal returns (uint256 word, uint32 gapDays) {
  1237	        // Already recorded for today
  1238	        uint256 recordedWord = rngWordByDay[day];
  1239	        if (recordedWord != 0) return (recordedWord, 0);
  1240	
  1241	        uint256 currentWord = rngWordCurrent;
  1242	
  1243	        // Have a fresh VRF word ready
  1244	        if (currentWord != 0 && rngRequestTime != 0) {
  1245	            // Backfill gap days from VRF stall before processing current day.
  1246	            // Gated on rngWordByDay[idx + 1] == 0 so the backfill runs at
  1247	            // most once per lock window: dailyIdx is only updated by
  1248	            // _unlockRng, so a multi-day drain would otherwise re-enter
  1249	            // this branch on each new wall-clock day and re-process the
  1250	            // same gap range, doubling purchaseStartDay and re-running
  1251	            // coinflip payouts for already-resolved days.
  1252	            uint24 idx = dailyIdx;
  1253	            if (day > idx + 1 && rngWordByDay[idx + 1] == 0) {
  1254	                uint24 gapCount = day - idx - 1;
  1255	                _backfillGapDays(currentWord, idx + 1, day);
  1256	
  1257	                // Backfill any lootbox indices that never got a VRF word (orphaned by stall).
  1258	                // Uses fresh VRF entropy, not predictable on-chain state.
  1259	                _backfillOrphanedLootboxIndices(currentWord);
  1260	
  1261	                // Extend death clock by the stall duration -- gap days don't count toward
  1262	                // the 120-day inactivity timeout since the game was stalled, not abandoned.
  1263	                purchaseStartDay += gapCount;
  1264	                gapDays = gapCount;
  1265	            }
  1266	
  1267	            // Normal daily RNG processing (request from current day)
  1268	            currentWord = _applyDailyRng(day, currentWord);
  1269	            coinflip.processCoinflipPayouts(coinflipBonus, currentWord, day);
  1270	            // Force the MINT_FLIP daily on the first jackpot day (lastPurchaseDay still set here,
  1271	            // jackpot not yet entered) so the FLIP-mint quest only lands when the redeem window is
  1272	            // live. Turbo (compressedJackpotFlag == 2) is skipped — its jackpot collapses at this
  1273	            // request, leaving no full open day for that quest.
  1274	            // Force the buy-a-foil-pack daily on the first purchase day of a level:
  1275	            // phaseTransitionActive is set at level end (_endPhase) and cleared only once
  1276	            // the transition completes (line 440), and this roll runs before that completion
  1277	            // in the same advance — so it is true exactly on the day the new purchase phase
  1278	            // opens. Gated on gapDays == 0 so a VRF-stall backfill (which defers the whole
  1279	            // transition to the next advance, line 412) does not roll the foil quest early.
  1280	            // Mutually exclusive with the first-jackpot-day MINT_FLIP force (opposite cycle ends).
  1281	            quests.rollDailyQuest(
  1282	                day,
  1283	                currentWord,
  1284	                lastPurchaseDay && compressedJackpotFlag != 2,
  1285	                phaseTransitionActive && gapDays == 0
  1286	            );
  1287	
  1288	            // Resolve the sentinel-stamped gambling-burn pool if any. Reading the
  1289	            // sentinel rather than deriving `day - 1` makes multi-day RNG stalls correct by
  1290	            // construction: the sentinel always names the (at most one) unresolved day, so a
  1291	            // single resolve call after the stall recovers covers the stuck pool exactly.
  1292	            {
  1293	                IsDGNRS sdgnrs = IsDGNRS(
  1294	                    ContractAddresses.SDGNRS
  1295	                );

  1298	                    uint16 redemptionRoll = uint16(
  1299	                        ((currentWord >> 8) % 151) + 25
  1300	                    );

  1302	                }
  1303	            }

  1305	            _finalizeLootboxRng(currentWord);
  1306	            return (currentWord, gapDays);
  1307	        }
  1308	
  1309	        // Waiting for VRF - check for timeout retry. A daily request (rngLockedFlag) gets the
  1310	        // 12h VRF retry; a lootbox-only mid-day request (rngLockedFlag == false) that bled past
  1311	        // the day boundary is abandoned after MIDDAY_RNG_STALL_TIMEOUT and promoted to this day's

  1324	        // Need fresh RNG
  1325	        _requestRng(isTicketJackpotDay, lvl);
  1326	        return (1, 0);
  1327	    }
  1328	
  1329	    function _finalizeLootboxRng(uint256 rngWord) private {
  1330	        uint48 index = uint48(_lrRead(LR_INDEX_SHIFT, LR_INDEX_MASK)) - 1;

a79a9809:.planning/ADMIN-AUDIT.md:192:| A-02 gamePurchase | DegenerusVault.sol:513 | S-09 prizePoolsPacked, S-32 mintPacked_[player], S-30 presaleStatePacked, S-24..S-29 lootboxEth/lootboxDay/lootboxBaseLevelPacked/lootboxEvScorePacked/lootboxDistressEth/lootboxBurnie, S-35 lastPurchaseDay, S-52 ticketQueue, S-53 ticketsOwedPacked | RNGLOCK-CATALOG §15 S-09 (MintModule `_processMintPayment`/`_handleMintRevenue` from `purchase`); S-32 row `MintModule._allocateMintPacked` :240/:275/:369; S-30 `_presaleCapCheck` :1026; S-24 `_allocateLootbox` :1013; S-25 :991; S-26 :992; S-27 :1155; S-28 :1031; S-52 `_queueTicketsScaled` from `_purchaseFor` :1129; S-53 co-located; S-35 purchase-path writer | VIOLATION | Cross-refs §16 V-024 (S-09 MintModule), V-089/V-091/V-095/V-098/V-101 (S-24..S-28), V-105 (S-30), V-110 (S-32), V-127 (S-35), V-174 (S-52), V-179 sub-rows (S-53). Tactic (a) `rngLockedFlag` revert at MintModule.purchase entry. Inherited via dispatcher A-02. |
a79a9809:.planning/MILESTONES.md:9:Full milestone audit of the entire **`v73.0 → HEAD` contract batch** (29 `.sol`, +1873/−1032 — the sDGNRS level-start lootbox + pre-deploy hardening: VRF-death deadman, mid-day-RNG fold, 44h admin gate, permissionless-settlement relaxation, named-error migration, whaleBundle→whalePass + WWXRP renames, the in-scope-dormant gas faucet) that had never been milestone-audited, plus assembly of the complete Code4rena package. **Verdict: 0 open findings.** Posture = the subject is already committed; the milestone *verifies* it. Method = verify → freeze-confirm → harness-green → 8-cluster as-built code audit → manifest re-point → cross-model adversarial re-audit (Codex; Gemini CLI tier-ineligible) → live-agent/soak re-attest → C4A package → terminal. **The sole conditional contract gate (475) fired once:** Codex surfaced a **MEDIUM** — `DegenerusAdmin.vote()`/`canExecute()` kill-on-recovery was *lazy* (only invalidated a VRF-coordinator-swap proposal when poked while currently recovered), so a proposal created in stall-1 could survive an un-poked VRF recovery and execute in a later ≥44h re-stall at the age-decayed (→5%) threshold with stale votes — installing an arbitrary VRF coordinator below the governance bar. Owner-approved logic-only fix `93d17288` (also invalidate when `lastVrfProcessed > createdAt` ⇒ any fulfillment after creation kills the proposal, recovery-proof without a poke; regression test `83a5ce43`). The other 5 Codex surfaces clean / by-design / refuted (the >120d VRF-death prevrandao super-fallback by-design → KNOWN-ISSUES §3a; queue-gate / post-gameover ticket insertion refuted; sDGNRS-box pre-rng-request sizing refuted; affiliate ≤3-FLIP quest rounding immaterial). The 8 as-built clusters (SUBJ/HARN/SOLV/RNG/LIVE/ACCESS/EV/WIRE/GAS) all HOLD via isolated neutral-prompt reviewers + an adversarial-verify pipeline + the skeptic filter (Workflow `wf_00bd2866`); contracts git-verified unmodified after each read-only fan-out. **Harness:** forge 0-fail; Hardhat 1359/0/18 (3 genesis-stall guards documented-skipped); VRFGovernance green. **Manifest:** MAN-01 re-pinned v73→fixed-tree, **28→34 invariants** (incl. the REDEEM-05 sDGNRS-storage-restructure fix); `MAIN-INVARIANTS.md` byte-synced to `invariants.json`. **Soak:** live agent re-pointed at a local-fork of the fixed tree → fresh local soak (80 actions) = **0 final MAN-01 violations / 0 profit-vs-EV alarms** (the prior 1000+-step 0-viol soak carries; the lone governance delta is orthogonal to every soaked invariant). **C4A package:** `scope.txt` (50 sources, 20,070 nSLOC) + `out_of_scope.txt` + `SECURITY.md` + trust-model + `KNOWN-ISSUES.md` + `audit/C4A-CONTEST-README.md` (Main-Invariants verbatim from MAN-01) + `audit/ACCESS-CONTROL-MATRIX.md` + `audit/ETH-FLOW-MAP.md`, all against the fixed tree. **Subject byte-frozen** `contracts/` tree **`f06b1ef6`** @ impl **`93d17288`** (the v73→HEAD batch + the one milestone governance fix, the sole `.sol` delta vs `3986926c`); closure `MILESTONE_V74_AT_HEAD_93d17288ba6719e0a77723d6167c0ba4796b8467` (distinct from the v73 tree `d6615306`). 13 phases (466-478). Deliverables `audit/FINDINGS-v74.0.md` (chmod 444) + `audit/AUDIT-V74-REPORT.html`; archived `milestones/v74.0-{ROADMAP,REQUIREMENTS}.md` (+ the superseded "C4A Readiness" plan 457-465 → `v74.0-superseded-plan-{ROADMAP,REQUIREMENTS}.md`). Baseline = v73.0 closure (`d6615306` @ `64ec993e`). **Tag `v74.0` (local; not pushed — USER pushes).**
a79a9809:.planning/MILESTONES.md:69:**Findings — THREE actionable the prior Claude-only audits missed (the premise validated):** **V62-01 MED–HIGH** — permissionless lootbox auto-open is structurally dead for human/presale boxes (boxes enqueue at `boxPlayers[LR_INDEX]` but words land at `LR_INDEX − 1`; `_openHumanBoxes`/`boxesPending` read the active index → never auto-opened; presale boxes additionally skipped by the `lootboxEthBase==0` guard); re-opens the WHALE-01 anti-timing vector. **V62-02 HIGH (convergent Gemini+Codex)** — `advanceGame` gas-brick: the afking subscriber STAGE falls through to `rngGate`→`_backfillGapDays` with no intervening stage-break (`AdvanceModule.sol:336→341`); a 121+ day VRF-stall recovery with a large funded subscriber set composes a saturated all-evict chunk + the 120-day backfill in ONE tx → reproduced COLD at **20,255,533 gas > 16,777,216** = a permanent game-over-advance brick (the v60 `6d2c8d0c` decouple only separated the DOWNSTREAM jackpot, not the UPSTREAM subscriber stage). **V62-03 HIGH (solvency spine)** — sDGNRS redemption reentrancy: `claimRedemption`→`_payEth` sends direct ETH via `player.call` BEFORE transferring the stETH remainder with no reentrancy guard; the attacker reentrantly `burn()`s and the backing calc double-counts the in-flight `stethOut` → over-reserves a redemption → `pendingRedemptionEthValue` exceeds ETH+stETH held → breaks SOLVENCY-01 (reproduced: after-attack pending 0.791 ETH while ETH+stETH held = 0). Plus 4 LOW (V62-04 false-game-over-from-stall monitor · V62-05 deity `ticketStartLevel` 50-overshoot, convergent · V62-06 lazy-pass boon price-basis · V62-07 `resolveLootboxDirect` correlated seed), 1 open MED (coinflip presale-bonus-flag freshness — read after the daily word is public), and **~15 refuted/by-design** (incl. the divergent DOMINANT-class near-future ticket jackpot-stuffing claim — refuted via the `_swapAndFreeze` pre-RNG snapshot; affiliate multi-sub claim theft — refuted via the same-affiliate guard; the carried ~2500× affiliate-score asymmetry — refuted as intended economics).
a79a9809:.planning/MILESTONES.md:85:**The three features:** (1) AFPAY — every non-mintBurnie spend draws `msg.value → claimableWinnings → afkingFunding` via a shared `_settleShortfall` (lootbox/presale/3 whale sites/Degenerette ETH bet), `AfkingSpent` emitted; (2) PACK — the two balance mappings folded into one `balancesPacked` (uint128 `[afking|claimable]` halves, slot 7; gameOver zeroes claimable, preserves infra afking); (3) CURSE+SMITE — cashout-curse (+2/stale-≥5d-cashout, −100 bps/pt off the activity score, cured by a ≥1-ticket buy or `decurse` 100 BURNIE, active-afker exempt) + deity-smite (200 BURNIE → +1 shared-counter stack, `ownerOf` gate, active-afker immune).
a79a9809:.planning/MILESTONES.md:109:**Component 2 — maximal cross-model audit (`audit/FINDINGS-v60.0.md`, `PLAN-V60-MAXIMAL-AUDIT.md`):** operating model = Claude as abundant deep-verifier + delta-hunter; Gemini (`gemini-3-pro-preview`) + Codex (`gpt-5.5`) as scarce fresh-eyes hunters aimed at the "safe/by-design" spine (vindicated by **LIFECYCLE** — Claude refuted it, both externals confirmed). **Round 1** (3-lens recall-preserving verify): 17 raw candidates → 6 findings — SOLV-02 RESOLVED `c0565ee4`; FLOWBURNIE (MED) + LIFECYCLE (MED) + FLOWENTROPY (LOW–MED) + STREAK (LOW) FIXED `4cb9ccbf`; PRESALE (LOW) = WONTFIX. **Round 2** external-led rotation run to completion across every surface — solvency ✅ held · RNG/freeze ✅ (RNGRETRY HIGH `305fa1c1` + RETRYLOCK `a8e7d939`) · afking-accounting ✅ held · lootbox/decimator ✅ (DECSTREAK HIGH `6bd94bb5`) · composition ✅ (RNGREUSE `5847258a`) · coinflip-gambling ✅ (G-1/C-1/C-2 `0f4e2a54`) · degenerette-wwxrp ✅ (`3603822f`) · quest+boon ✅ (`07e930d5`) · **gas-DoS advance ceiling ✅ (GASCEIL `6d2c8d0c` — end-to-end PoC confirmed a game-over composition 17.54M single tx > 16.7M; fix breaks per ticket batch, post-fix max 6.37M, zero regressions)** · **whale/pass ✅ (WHALE-01 `2bee6d6f` pass-box auto-open enqueue gap + deity early-gameover refund capped at price-paid + deity 3→1 slot collapse) — LAST rotation area.**
a79a9809:.planning/MILESTONES.md:201:**Headline:** Resolved both v47-deferred MEDIUMs — **F-47-01** presale closing-box DGNRS over-distribution (divisor `1_000→400`; closing sweep now variance dust — Monte-Carlo mean 7.3% vs the old systematic ~60% windfall) + **F-47-02** redemption submit ETH-empty stETH-fallback gap (pure-ETH-OR-pure-stETH reservation, mid-game ETH→stETH fallback, fail-closed if neither covers, donation-robust). Shipped the sanctioned **sDGNRS far-future ticket salvage-swap** exit (`sellFarFutureTickets`, -EV by design, no-arb HOLDS at the jitter-band ceiling, ≥1 ETH claimable floor, caller-verified swap-pop preserving `membership ⟺ packed != 0`). Plus the **keeper rename** (autoBuy/autoOpen/autoResolve; crank/sweep/do-work PURGED) + two-tier 75/20/5 VAULT/SDGNRS affiliate capture (`bytes32("DGNRS")`), **AfKing pool recovery** (VAULT permissionless `recoverAfKingPool` + sDGNRS `receive()` AF_KING relax + `burnAtGameOver` auto-recover), the **gameover BURNIE tombstone** (1e36-wei `vaultAllowance` flood, uncirculated, one-shot, `totalSupply()` untouched), and the **Degenerette hero 2-pt rescale** (`S=A+2H`, standalone multiplier net-deleted, basePayoutEV=100 centi-x byte-reproduced, `S=9≡old M=8` relabel, RTP unchanged).

a79a9809:.planning/MILESTONES.md:604:**Result:** 6 of 6 §4 adversarial surfaces SAFE_BY_DESIGN / SAFE_BY_STRUCTURAL_CLOSURE (a bit-slice modulo-bias bound + b seed-reuse cross-correlation across sub-rolls + c hash2(seed, N) chunk-collision-free + d gas-griefing delta bounded + e BAF byte-identity ENT-05 verification + f commitment-window check); zero F-36-NN finding blocks emitted; AUDIT-01 §3d delta-surface table enumerates 17 rows for `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameLootboxModule.sol` (MODIFIED_LOGIC `_rollTargetLevel` + `_resolveLootboxRoll` + `_lootboxTicketCount` + `_lootboxDgnrsReward` + `_rollLootboxBoons` + REFACTOR_ONLY `_resolveLootboxCommon` parameter rename + 4 entry-point caller plumbing + NEW seed2 = hash2(seed, 1) ETH-amount-second branch chunk + NEW NatSpec bit-budget block per refactored function + DELETED 7 entropyStep callsites + DELETED L1585 dead WWXRP advance + DELETED nextEntropy return contract on 3 helpers + REFACTOR_ONLY EntropyLib.sol/JackpotModule.sol/MintModule.sol BYTE-IDENTICAL); AUDIT-04 §3d Part C zero-new-state attestation 5-row table all PASS (storage-slot scan + GameStorage byte-identity + zero new public/external + zero new modifier + EntropyLib API stable per ENT-04). AUDIT-03 §3e conservation re-proof: 8 SAFE invariants (ETH / BURNIE / DGNRS / WWXRP / Tickets / Boon / Solvency / Bucket-share-sum × pool — all preserved byte-identically across the entropy-derivation refactor). LEAN regression: 1 PASS REG-01 (v35.0 closure signal `MILESTONE_V35_AT_HEAD_5db8682bd7b811437f0c1cf47e832619d1478ac6` re-verified non-widening; per-pull-level helper UNTOUCHED) + 1 PASS REG-02 (v34.0 closure signal `MILESTONE_V34_AT_HEAD_6b63f6d4daf346a53a1d463790f637308ea8d555` re-verified non-widening; gold-solo-priority + JackpotBucketLib UNCHANGED) + 11 PASS + 0 SUPERSEDED + 0 REGRESSED REG-04 (prior-finding spot-check sweep across audit/FINDINGS-v25..v35 for v36-touched function reference set). KI envelopes EXC-01..03 RE_VERIFIED NEGATIVE-scope at v36 (lootbox-path refactor has zero affiliate-roll / AdvanceModule / gameover-RNG-substitution interaction); EXC-04 RE_VERIFIED with NARROWS scope — BAF-jackpot-only after lootbox-path xorshift consumption removal; STAT-01 chi² empirical evidence at `test/stat/LootboxEntropyDistribution.test.js` (6 sub-roll buckets, all uniform within Wilson-Hilferty Z<1.645 / CHI2_CRIT_05[4]=9.488 thresholds at α=0.05). a79a9809:.planning/MILESTONES.md:628:**Result:** 6 of 6 §4 adversarial surfaces SAFE / SAFE_BY_DESIGN / SAFE_BY_STRUCTURAL_CLOSURE (a predictability/trait-stacking commitment-window check + b level-salt collision between two near-future BURNIE callers + c deity-cache staleness across pulls + d cross-caller `_randTraitTicket` salt collision (legacy salt-parameter dropped on coin-jackpot caller) + e off-chain indexer semantic-shift (`JackpotBurnieWin.lvl` re-interpretation) + f gas-griefing via repeated cold SLOAD across 50 distinct (lvl', trait_i) slots); STAT-03 reframe row SAFE_BY_STRUCTURAL_CLOSURE (88.24% empty-bucket skip rate on natural-lifecycle fresh `deployFullProtocol` fixture reframed as fixture-calibration error per D-265-STAT03-01, NOT a finding); zero F-35-NN finding blocks emitted; AUDIT-01 §3d delta-surface table enumerates 10 rows for `contracts/modules/DegenerusGameJackpotModule.sol` (NEW `_awardDailyCoinToTraitWinners` helper + NEW `COIN_LEVEL_TAG` constant + 2 MODIFIED_LOGIC callsites + REFACTOR_ONLY `_randTraitTicket` salt-drop on coin-jackpot caller + DELETED `_computeBucketCounts` coin-jackpot CALL (def preserved for lootbox path) + DELETED `DAILY_COIN_SALT_BASE` constant + 2 DELETED dead blocks + NEW non-storage helper-internal locals); AUDIT-04 §3d Part C zero-new-state attestation 5-row table all PASS (GameStorage UNTOUCHED + zero new public/external + zero new admin + zero new modifiers + zero new upgrade hooks). a79a9809:.planning/PROJECT.md:31:**The five behavioral change clusters:** (1) purchase hot-path gas folds (combined prize-pool RMW / aggregate `claimablePool` decrement / activity-score skip / combined affiliate roll — solvency-sensitive); (2) VRF-death deadman + mid-day stalled-RNG recovery folded into the daily advance + fail-open swap + advance same-tx-composition guards + 44h admin gate + payable Admin `receive()`; (3) the **new sDGNRS level-start lootbox** (5%-of-claimable, 6 ETH cap, once-per-level) + `reinvestPct` removal (Sub repack); (4) broad **permissionless-settlement** relaxation (dispatch stubs, caller-funded gifts, `claimBingo`+address, full-permissionless `claimAffiliateDgnrs`, partial `claimWinnings`); (5) named-error migration + whaleBundle→whalePass + WWXRP rename + the now-in-scope `DegenerusGasFaucet`.
a79a9809:.planning/PROJECT.md:411:1. **`claimBingo` color-completion entrypoint** (FEATURE — RNG-touching, read-only) — new external `claimBingo(uint256 level, uint8 symbol, uint32[8] slots)`, likely in a new `DegenerusGameBingoModule.sol` delegatecalled from `DegenerusGame`. Validates `symbol < 32` / `!gameOver` / `level <= currentLevel`; derives `quadrant = symbol >> 3`, `symInQ = symbol & 7`; verifies the caller owns one `traitBurnTicket[level][traitId][slots[c]]` entry for each color `c ∈ [0,7]` of the requested symbol (`traitId = (quadrant<<6) | (c<<3) | symInQ`; duplicate-slot griefing impossible by trait-byte encoding). Three reward tiers with **quadrant-first-checked-before-symbol-first** precedence: regular (0.05% `Pool.Reward` + 1 000 BURNIE) · symbol-first (additive +0.05% +1 000 → 0.1% +2 000, marks the symbol-first bit) · quadrant-first (replacement 0.5% +5 000, marks BOTH the quadrant-first AND symbol-first bits, suppressing the symbol-first bonus). Per-player dedup once per (level, quadrant) → max 4 claims/player/level. sDGNRS via `sdgnrs.transferFromPool(Pool.Reward, …)`; BURNIE via `coinflip.creditFlip(…)`; empty-pool graceful no-op (claim bits + BURNIE still paid); `gameOver` hard cutoff; event-only leaderboard (`FirstQuadrantBingo` / `FirstSymbolBingo` / `BingoClaimed`). Storage adds `bingoClaimed` (per-player u8) + `firstQuadrant` (systemwide u8) + `firstSymbol` (systemwide u32), keyed by `uint24` level. Seed: `v51-claimbingo-color-completion-seed` + `.planning/PLAN-V51-CLAIMBINGO-COLOR-COMPLETION.md`.
a79a9809:.planning/PROJECT.md:494:**Goal:** Resolve the two v47.0-deferred MEDIUM findings (F-47-01, F-47-02), ship the sanctioned sDGNRS far-future ticket salvage-swap exit, and land a cluster of keeper / pool-recovery / gameover-tombstone / Degenerette refinements — shipped as ONE batched USER-APPROVED `contracts/*.sol` diff, proven by TST, and closed by a TERMINAL delta-audit + 3-skill adversarial sweep, matching the v44–v47 audit shape.
a79a9809:.planning/PROJECT.md:595:**Headline finding — VRF-rotation orphan index (CONFIRMED CATASTROPHE, 2026-05-20):** `updateVrfCoordinatorAndSub` (AdvanceModule ~:1687) clears `rngWordCurrent` + `LR_MID_DAY` but never backfills `lootboxRngWordByIndex[N]` — the index a stalled mid-day `requestLootboxRng` swap was bound to. Two timing-dependent outcomes: (A) same-day `advanceGame` → `processTicketBatch` reads `entropy = lootboxRngWordByIndex[N] = 0` (no zero-guard) → deterministic/entropy-0 traits (HIGH; EV-positive grind since rotation is governance-telegraphed + stall observable); (B) next-day → daily-drain gate reverts, `requestLootboxRng`/`retryLootboxRng` bricked, the orphan-backfill helper unreachable behind the revert → ~120-day freeze until `_livenessTriggered` forces a premature game-over (funds eventually recoverable). Self-inflicted by the documented emergency procedure. Aligns with memory `project_vrf_rotation_midday_orphan_index` + `v45-vrf-freeze-invariant`.

a79a9809:.planning/RNGLOCK-CATALOG.md:794:| `lastPurchaseDay` | `Storage.sol:1244` (`_livenessTriggered`: `if (lastPurchaseDay \|\| jackpotPhaseFlag) return false`) | `_livenessTriggered` is reached via `_queueTickets` (`:570`). A mid-window flip of `lastPurchaseDay` from true→false would unblock the liveness trigger and could revert the entire jackpot resolution. **However**, no external function writes `lastPurchaseDay = false` outside `advanceGame`'s state machine — it's only set true at `:176`/`:397` (mid-advance) and false at `:439` (post-jackpot transition). a79a9809:.planning/RNGLOCK-CATALOG.md:1445:- `traitBurnTicket` admin writers tactic (a) chosen because admin writes are operational, not user-facing — a revert during the game-over drain window is acceptable.
a79a9809:.planning/RNGLOCK-CATALOG.md:1452:**Caller chain:** `_handleGameOverPath` (`AdvanceModule.sol:522`) → `_gameOverEntropy` writes `rngWordByDay[day]` (`AdvanceModule.sol:1271`/`1841`) → multi-tx ticket drain (`STAGE_TICKETS_WORKING` re-entries) → `handleGameOverDrain` (`GameOverModule.sol:79`) → sets `gameOver=true` at line 139 → calls `runTerminalDecimatorJackpot` at line 168 with `rngWord = rngWordByDay[day]`.
a79a9809:.planning/RNGLOCK-CATALOG.md:1517:- `BurnieCoin.terminalDecimatorBurn` external entry point: `msg.sender` is the EOA / external contract; gate is `terminalDecWindow.open == (!gameOver && !lastPurchaseDay)`.
a79a9809:.planning/RNGLOCK-CATALOG.md:1518:- Across the multi-tx game-over window: after TX A writes `rngWordByDay[day]` via `_applyDailyRng` (`AdvanceModule.sol:1841`) — but BEFORE TX N reaches `handleGameOverDrain` (which is what flips `gameOver=true` at `GameOverModule.sol:139` and then calls the consumer at `:168`) — the global state is `rngWordByDay[day] != 0` (RNG word publicly readable) AND `gameOver == false` (terminal burn window OPEN). Multi-tx gap is forced by `STAGE_TICKETS_WORKING` early returns in `_handleGameOverPath` (`AdvanceModule.sol:596`, `:615`) when ticket queue exceeds single-tx gas.
a79a9809:.planning/RNGLOCK-CATALOG.md:1533:**Rationale expansion (out-of-table for traceability; the 80-char cell above is the verdict-matrix entry):** Tactic (a) `rngLockedFlag-gated revert` is the structurally minimal fix: introduce a revert in `recordTerminalDecBurn` (or in `BurnieCoin.terminalDecimatorBurn` via a view query) once the day's `rngWordByDay[day] != 0` AND a game-over path is in progress. Mirrors Phase 290 MINTCLN pattern at `DegenerusGameMintModule.sol:1221` (`if (cachedJpFlag && rngLockedFlag) {...}`). Tactic (b) snapshot/anchor is rejected: terminal-decimator burn-totals are aggregates, not per-day snapshots; snapshotting at game-over kickoff would require freezing across the multi-tx ticket-drain window, which is structurally the same as gating. Tactic (c) pre-lock reorder is rejected: there is no `advanceGame()`-internal reorder that closes the window, because the multi-tx STAGE_TICKETS_WORKING split is unavoidable for queue-exhaustion. Tactic (d) immutable is rejected: `terminalDecBucketBurnTotal` is an aggregate keyed on `bucketKey` that legitimately accrues throughout the level (cannot be made immutable). Phase 299 FIX sub-phase planning re-discovers design intent per `feedback_design_intent_before_deletion.md` discipline.
a79a9809:.planning/RNGLOCK-CATALOG.md:1548:**Caller chain:** EOA / contract → `DegenerusGame.advanceGame` (external) → AdvanceModule delegatecall → `_handleGameOverPath` (`AdvanceModule.sol:522`) → `_gameOverEntropy` writes `rngWordByDay[day]` (`AdvanceModule.sol:1271`/`:1841`) → multi-tx ticket drain (`STAGE_TICKETS_WORKING` early returns at `:596`/`:615`) eventually reaches → `delegatecall handleGameOverDrain(day)` (`AdvanceModule.sol:624-628`) → `_unlockRng(day)` (`AdvanceModule.sol:631`).

Also gates the deity-pass refund loop entry per `lvl < 10`.) |
a79a9809:.planning/RNGLOCK-CATALOG.md:1812:E-3 / E-4 / E-6 (`claimWinnings` + `sweepSdgnrsClaim`): these are player-withdraw paths that lack a liveness gate. During the multi-tx game-over drain (which can span many TXs because `STAGE_TICKETS_WORKING` early-returns chain), a player can call `claimWinnings` between TX A (where `rngWordByDay[day]` is written) and TX N (where `handleGameOverDrain` runs). This shrinks `address(this).balance` AND `claimablePool`, both of which feed `available` and downstream payout magnitudes. Tactic (a) gated revert: add `if (_livenessTriggered() && !gameOver) revert E();` to `claimWinnings` and `sweepSdgnrsClaim`. Once `gameOver == true` (after `handleGameOverDrain:139`), `claimWinnings` re-opens for the post-gameover claim period.

a79a9809:.planning/RNGLOCK-CATALOG.md:2086:**Cross-consumer VIOLATION rows surfaced (writer reached from another consumer's EOA entry, NOT this consumer's, but enumerated for catalog integration completeness):** D-1, D-2, D-6, D-7 (`openLootBox` / `openBurnieLootBox` reach). D-10 cluster needs Phase 299 per-callsite expansion. D-16 (`lastPurchaseDay` purchase-path writer — reaches into this consumer's resolution via `_livenessTriggered`'s short-circuit; if `lastPurchaseDay != 0` the function returns `false` early, masking liveness — i.e., the attacker can BLOCK the `_livenessTriggered` revert by purchasing a ticket in the same day, ensuring `_queueTickets` continues; this is a participating-slot manipulation).
a79a9809:.planning/RNGLOCK-CATALOG.md:2100:**D-16 (`lastPurchaseDay` purchase-path writer cross-mutates this consumer's gate):**
a79a9809:.planning/RNGLOCK-CATALOG.md:2101:- `_livenessTriggered` at `DegenerusGameStorage.sol:1244` short-circuits `false` when `lastPurchaseDay != 0`. This means any EOA-purchase-path write of `lastPurchaseDay` SUPPRESSES the death-clock check inside `_queueTickets` reached from `resolveRedemptionLootbox` (whale-pass branch — but here `allowBoons=false`, so the only `_queueTickets` reach is via the main ticket path at `LootboxModule.sol:1067`).
a79a9809:.planning/RNGLOCK-CATALOG.md:2102:- The participating-slot impact: attacker can ensure `_queueTickets` does NOT revert by making sure `lastPurchaseDay != 0` on the day of claim. Conversely, if attacker wants the consumer to REVERT (e.g., to roll back the entire `resolveRedemptionLootbox` partial-resolution and re-attempt in a later tx where they have a more favorable `level` or `poolBalances[Lootbox]`), they can ensure `lastPurchaseDay == 0` AND `jackpotPhaseFlag == 0` AND day-math triggers — though death-clock arithmetic is largely deterministic from `purchaseStartDay`, so this lever is narrow.
a79a9809:.planning/RNGLOCK-CATALOG.md:2113:| E-3 | D-16: `lastPurchaseDay` purchase-path writer cross-mutates `_livenessTriggered` gate during claimRedemption reach | **(a)** | Block claimRedemption while `rngWordByDay[period] != 0 && day != period` until next advance |
a79a9809:.planning/RNGLOCK-CATALOG.md:2119:**E-3 — tactic (a) rngLockedFlag-gated revert (variant):** The cleanest fix is to require `claimRedemption` to call into a Game-side view that asserts day-math is consistent at claim time — specifically, `block.timestamp / DAY > period` (claim must be at least one day past period) AND `_livenessTriggered() == false` at the moment of consumer-entry. The existing `claim.periodIndex != 0` gate is necessary but insufficient. Adding a `_livenessTriggered`-check inside `Game.resolveRedemptionLootbox` (`DegenerusGame.sol:1727`) before the delegatecall loop forces a revert path that does not give the attacker the partial-execution roll-back lever D-16 documents. Tactic (b) snapshot/anchor is partially applicable (snapshot `lastPurchaseDay` AND `jackpotPhaseFlag` AND `purchaseStartDay` AND `rngRequestTime` at burn submission) but bloats the struct unacceptably; tactic (a) is structurally simpler. Phase 299 FIX sub-phase planning re-discovers the design intent per `feedback_design_intent_before_deletion.md` discipline.
