   245	      |  • PURCHASE (jackpotPhaseFlag=false): Process ticket batches until target met, then → JACKPOT|
   246	      |  • JACKPOT (jackpotPhaseFlag=true): Pay daily jackpots, wait for burns, then → PURCHASE      |
   247	      |  • GAMEOVER (gameOver=true): Terminal state, no transitions                             |
   248	      |                                                                                        |
   249	      |  Gating (tiered bypass):                                                                |
   250	      |  • Deity pass holder — always bypasses                                                 |
   251	      |  • Anyone — bypasses after 30+ min since level start                                   |
   252	      |  • Pass holder (lazy/whale) — bypasses after 15+ min                                   |
   253	      |  • DGVE majority holder — always bypasses (last resort, external call)                 |
   254	      |  • RNG must be ready (not locked) or recently stale (12h timeout)                      |
   255	      |                                                                                        |
   256	      |  Presale: packed presale-active toggle (orthogonal to state machine)                    |
   257	      |  • Starts active: 62% bonus FLIP from loot boxes                                       |
   258	      |  • Auto-ends when PURCHASE→JACKPOT, or admin can end manually (one-way, cannot re-enable) |
   259	      +========================================================================================+*/
   260	
   261	    /// @notice Advance the game state machine by one tick.
   262	    /// @dev Anyone can call, but standard flows require an ETH mint today.
   263	    ///      This is the primary driver of game progression - called repeatedly
   264	    ///      to move through states and process batched operations.
   265	    ///
   266	    ///      FLOW OVERVIEW:
   267	    ///      1. Check liveness guards (1yr deploy timeout, 120-day inactivity)
   268	    ///      2. Apply tiered daily gate (deity > anyone after 30min > pass after 15min > DGVE majority)
   269	    ///      3. Process transition housekeeping during jackpot→purchase transition
   270	    ///      4. Gate on RNG readiness (request new VRF if needed)
   271	    ///      5. Process ticket batches
   272	    ///      6. Execute state-specific logic:
   273	    ///         - TRANSITION: Housekeeping + near-future ticket prep after burn completes
   274	    ///         - PURCHASE/JACKPOT: Process phase-specific logic
   275	    ///      7. Credit caller with FLIP bounty during jackpot time when not requesting or unlocking RNG
   276	    ///
   277	    ///      SECURITY:
   278	    ///      - Liveness guards prevent abandoned game lockup
   279	    ///      - Daily gate prevents non-participants from advancing
   280	    ///      - RNG gating ensures fairness (no manipulation during VRF window)
   281	    ///      - Batched processing prevents DoS from large queues
   282	    ///
   283	    ///      The signature matches the module function exactly (identical selector), so the calldata
   284	    ///      forwards as-is — re-encoding here would cost contract-size headroom for no behavior change.
   285	    function advanceGame() external returns (uint8 mult) {
   286	        (bool ok, bytes memory data) = ContractAddresses
   287	            .GAME_ADVANCE_MODULE
   288	            .delegatecall(msg.data);
   289	        if (!ok) _revertDelegate(data);
   290	        mult = abi.decode(data, (uint8));
   291	    }
   292	
   293	    /*+========================================================================================+
   294	      |                    ADMIN VRF FUNCTIONS                                                 |
   295	      +========================================================================================+
   296	      |  One-time VRF setup function called by ADMIN during deployment phase.                  |
   297	      +========================================================================================+*/
   298	
   299	    /// @notice Wire VRF config from the VRF ADMIN contract.
   300	    /// @dev Access: ADMIN only. Overwrites any existing config on each call.
   301	    ///      SECURITY: Config can be changed via emergency rotation (updateVrfCoordinatorAndSub).
   302	    /// @custom:reverts OnlyAdmin If caller is not ADMIN.
   303	    /// @dev Signature: wireVrf(address coordinator_, uint256 subId, bytes32 keyHash_) —
   304	    ///      Chainlink VRF V2.5 coordinator address, VRF subscription ID for LINK billing,
   305	    ///      and the VRF key hash identifying the oracle and gas lane. The signature matches
   306	    ///      the module function exactly (identical selector), so the calldata forwards as-is —
   307	    ///      re-encoding here would cost contract-size headroom for no behavior change.
   308	    function wireVrf(address, uint256, bytes32) external {
   309	        (bool ok, bytes memory data) = ContractAddresses
   310	            .GAME_ADVANCE_MODULE
   311	            .delegatecall(msg.data);
   312	        if (!ok) _revertDelegate(data);
   313	    }

codex
C5 is reachable.

After a normal seal, `ticketsFullyProcessed` can be true while later purchases accumulate at `_tqWriteKey(level + 1)`; Mint writes that queue without changing the flag. In the game-over entropy paths, neither `_tryRequestRng`/callback nor fallback calls `_swapTicketSlot`, and that helper is the sole false-writer for the flag.

Both proposed paths work, with two timing details:

- Full abandonment reaches the deadman at `currentDay - dailyIdx > 120` (first at day `D + 121`, not exactly `D + 120`). A successful game-over VRF request only locks/stores the word; on the next call `_gameOverEntropy` commits it and the true flag skips `DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:725`.
- The reverting-coordinator path is also reachable without the deadman: make `purchaseStartDay` older than 120 days while `dailyIdx` was sealed yesterday and the pool target remains unmet. The caught revert sets `rngRequestTime`; after 14 days the fallback commits without swapping, then reaches the same skipped branch. If the deadman is already true, fallback occurs on the next call rather than waiting 14 days.

The skipped queue is not indirectly consumed: `processTicketBatch(level + 1)` reads only `_tqReadKey`, while `handleGameOverDrain` calls `runTerminalJackpot(..., level + 1, ...)`, which samples `lvlTraitEntry[level + 1]` rather than either ticket queue. Once `gameOver` is latched, future advances only invoke final-sweep handling, so the write-slot entries never materialize and cannot participate in the terminal jackpot.

VERDICT: REAL — Both entropy paths preserve a stale true drain flag and leave the populated write slot unswapped. The terminal path pays only already-materialized trait entries and has no later queue-drain leg.

VERDICT: REAL — Both entropy paths preserve a stale true drain flag and leave the populated write slot unswapped. The terminal path pays only already-materialized trait entries and has no later queue-drain leg.
